user_comment: I got the X-Small, the one not many have reviewed. If you prefer small, lightweight bags like wristlets / crossbodies, you’ll love this! It’s well-made, durable, and cute! It features a zipper, and that area is recessed so you can set things on top. The two pockets in front are functional!! The strap I adjusted to its longest length, and it it’s my 5’4 frame at the hip when worn crossbody. When not taking this to work, I have tons more room because I don’t need the speaker or markers. I love it!!

I got the XL in these cropped, mostly cotton (95%) jeans. I'm 5'7" and 180 lbs with a thick belly.The fit is strange at the top. I thought it was due to my thick belly, so I had my adult daughter try them, she had the same issue, they fold a little under the pockets for some reason and look strange.I got the brownish color and it is a good muted neutral color that will transition well into the fall. The material is soft and comfortable as it is mostly cotton. The way that the waist band is made is not great quality. They are true to size. I like them as far as the concept, the color and the comfort. I won't wear them due to the strange fit and the way they are made and look along the waist band.

I got the XS and it still runs a little large. I’m 5ft tall and I’m 7 months pregnant. It’s a little tight on the belly but it holds the belly good. It’s been more than 3 or 4 months now that I don’t wear any jeans since my belly started growing. So this one will do the job now. Overall I do like it. I think it’s worth it for the price

I got these as a cheaper dupe and I LOVE THEM. Super comfortable. Real denim. Fit great. I'm 5'8" 128 lbs and got size small. Usually because of my height overalls are tough because they're either too short or to wide .. I don't have that problem with these! I wear them all summer long! Love!

I got these blue “high waisted” shorts during a flash sale for like $18 and I’m SOO glad I did! They fit wonderfully! I’m 5’1 142lbs and ordered the M, could get a S for a more snug fit but I love how comfy the M is. I haven’t washed them yet so I don’t know how they hold up in the wash, I’ll come back and edit once I do. They aren’t high rise really, definitely more mid rise which usually cuts into me weird but these don’t. The cut is extremely flattering. I have a leftover apron belly overhang from losing 86lbs this year, and the shorts hide it very well. So if I want to tuck in shirts, I can! Or maybe I’ll even try some crop tops, who knows! They make me feel so confident, I haven’t been able to wear jean shorts in years that actually look cute on me. I’m definitely ordering the black which they have a few variations to try. Maybe even white. They are also very flattering in the butt, which I have ZERO of since losing all this weight. The pockets unfortunately do poke out some, even though these are in no way tight. I might sew it a little to keep it from bulging out. I’ll come back when I get more to show what they look like. Ignore my Christmas shirt and dirty mirror - I really need to do laundry and I have a 2 year old who like to pretend she’s wa window washer.

I got these capris pants so that I'd have some casual summer capris in a fun color. SIZE/FIT: I got an XXL in the olive green, and they fit as expected (the waist is a little snug on me, but I knew that going in, since I have a 42" waist - but they're pretty comfortable and stretchy regardless). I'm 5"6', about 220 lbs. The capris pant stops about 5-6 inches above my ankle. APPEARANCE: I love the color of the fabric; it's a lovely, light, slightly muted green. I like how the capris look from the front and sides. On the backside, however, the fabric kept getting caught up on my behind, giving my a slight wedgie appearance. For that reason, I took off a star. FEATURES: These pants have nice sized, open pockets, big enough for a phone or wallet. I like the wide, elastic waist. FUNCTION/FABRIC: The fabric of these pants is light and kind of crinkled; they would be great for the beach or on a hot summer day. Overall, I'm very happy with these pants, aside from the wedgie issue.

I got these for a hippie halloween costume but actually fell in love with them. They are stretchy but also still have a bit of structure. The wash is actually is darker in person than in the product photo, which actually was a positive in my opinion. For my short girlie's, these will work for you! I am 4'11 and 160lbs and these are the perfect length. A small platform shoe was enough to keep then from dragging. I ordered a large and it was a perfect fit. These aren't like the BEST quality in my opinion but I don't think they will fall apart either. They're thin, but not too thin. I think I'll get a good many wears out of them. I would order these again.

I got these for a trip to Jamaica this Spring and I absolutely loved them. I've worn them many times since - dressed up to work, casual drinks with friends, and to travel in. They are very comfortable and light to wear in hot temperatures but they also have some weight to them to be comfortable when it isn't hot. They were not see through, although I would recommend wearing nude undergarments. I love that you can adjust the waist - great for when bloated! I am 5'4 130lbs so they are a tad long when I wear shoes without a heel but I also have offset that by wearing them higher on my waist. Highly recommend these!

I got these for Christmas from a family member and I swear I thought she had sized them down when ordering. But no it is just lying about the measurements. I actually have both pairs of jeans now and the flower embroidered one is great they are very comfortable, stretchy and I’ve had them for about 5 months and they are still not even worn out a little. The star ones however- in the same size might I add- are terrible. Not stretchy or comfortable, in fact, after spending 5 minutes trying to get them on, I measured them and the waist was the only slightly correct size and I’m really dissatisfied and upset. I got medium and it says the waist is supposed to be about 39 inches but when I measured it it was 32 at most I honestly don’t remember how much the out-seam was but I measured 38. I admit I’m a little curvy at 5’4”, 150lbs but this can’t be right. I’m upset because I can’t even return them since they were a gift and I don’t have the receipt.
I got these for my daughter. She's 14 weeks,5'1" and 160lbs. She loves them, they're comfortable, not see through, stretchy and cute. She will definitely purchase more colors.
I got these for my daughter. She's 14 weeks,5'1" and 160lbs. She loves them, they're comfortable, not see through, stretchy and cute. She will definitely purchase more colors.

I got these for my daughter’s birthday. She is 5’6”, about 125 lbs and usually wears a small. These fit well. They are stretchy and have some give. The length hits her about 2 inches above her ankle. The jeans are flattering and comfortable. They are a mid weight Jean.

I got these for my mom for her birthday, I have a similar pair from the same brand that I really like. She preferred the button pocket detailing on these. She is tall at 5’9 so they ended up being peddle pushers on her. I think theyre cute like that. Shes really happy with the fit and softeness of the fabric.

I got these for my trip to the Dominican Republic and I love them! The material is comfortable and high quality. I was worried the sand would stain them but it washed right out! I got an extra small or small (whatever the smallest size was, I can’t remember) and they fit great. The waist is stretchy and high waisted. I just wish they were a little longer. I’m 5’5 for reference and they ended right at my ankles and I wish they were more floor length.

I got these for my wife to wear around the house, and she likes them a lot, with a few exceptions. The legs are super long, and the ties to cinch the bottom up get to be a trip hazard if you don’t tuck them in good. See picture. My wife is 5’3” for comparison. The pockets are nice and hold things tight and secure. She likes the material and the tie waist also. They are comfortable and she has worn them several days already.

I got these for school color spirit days where I work. They are a nice deep red. I got a medium and would have liked a tighter fit like the model photos but I like the length so I'm going to keep them. I am 5' 5" 110lbs for reference.

I got these high waisted flare leg capri jeans because I needed some cute denim capris to wear this summer. SIZING & FIT: I ordered size XXL and the capris fit as expected; not too snug, not too loose. For context, I usually order a size XXL in Women’s Clothing. I am 5’6”, weigh ~220 lbs, with a small chest and wide hips. The jeans seem well made overall and look cute on me. I really love the five shiny buttons on the front; they make the jeans look fashionable. I am not a huge fan of the flare around the bottom hem, but it’s a subtle flare. The pockets are nice and big. Overall, I’d recommend these capris jeans!

I got these in 27 petite. For reference, I’m 5’0” and 107 lb. These pants were supposed to have a 29” inseam, according to the size chart, and they are much shorter than that, as you can see in this photo. The fit is otherwise comfy and fine though, so I’m going to try to exchange for regular inseam.

I got these in a 6 and while they fit me in how they're supposed to be worn high-waisted, the pleating does not always stay flat and they come right above my ankle and I am 5'7" 156, so I'm thinking maybe going up to an 8 would have given me more room throughout the hip area and also length at the bottom. Note the material is soft.

I got these in a small and I normally wear a medium. The fit is great for me and hugs my curves but I still have movement with the stretch in these. I also love that it has shorts built into them so they aren't completely see through. They are light-weight and would be great for vacation or any warm weather outing. These are the kind of pants that the wind breezes through. They come to my ankle at 5'7".

I got these in size small in white. The fit is decent, but the length is a tad short for my liking. I'm 5'6", and they hit just below my ankles, falling shorter than the model's portrayal. These would not be suitable for taller individuals. The jean is of moderate quality, not the finest available. The zipper doesn't glide seamlessly, and the button is passable. The material does have some stretch, making them very comfortable to wear. The picture was taken before washing and ironing.

I got these in today and had my fingers crossed that they would fit and yes, they fit nicely. The quality is good, the fit is exactly as described - - I'm 5'1 and weigh 144.9lbs and I ordered the large. They are comfortable and they are actually more flattering than any shorts that I've tried on lately.

I got these jeans for my daughter & they look adorable on her. She especially loves how stretchy & super comfy they are. Sizing might run a bit small. She’s a size 6, which the description equates to a "small," but I decided to order up 1 size as a precaution & opted for the "medium." Thankfully, that was the right decision. They fit her perfectly. The high waist is flattering. They’re fitted on the hips & backside without being too snug. Although they’re wide-leg, they don’t look baggy, maintaining a stylish shape. These jeans have a sturdy zipper, metal button, & back pockets, giving them that classic denim jeans look. However, the material is thinner than your average denim & even thinner than other stretchy denim I’m used to. Despite the thinner fabric, you can’t see through them in the white color, so it’s not a major concern. My daughter is 5'7", & the bottom hem lands just beneath her heel, which makes them look long with flat sandals. However, they pair well with shoes that have a raised sole or heel, like boots & wedges. She did mention they’re a bit long below the zipper, causing a slight bunching when she sits. Fortunately, this improved after washing, as I expected since stretchy denim tends to draw up a bit. Although the care instructions advise against tumble drying, I did toss them in the dryer on a low, wrinkle-free setting & they held up just fine. Overall, these jeans are stylish & fit great. I’d recommend getting precise measurements & comparing them to the size chart, as you may need to size up. The denim is decent quality, though I’m a bit concerned about how long they’ll last given the thinner material. But mostly, she’s thrilled with how comfortable they are.

I got these jeans for my daughter. She is 5’7”, about 120 lbs, and usually wears a small. I ordered the XS in these jeans according to the size chart and they fit her well. The pictures do not do these jeans justice. They really look cute in person and are very comfortable. Perfect for the Y2K look my daughter is going for. The jeans seem made well.
I got these jeans in size S small. I am 5’5 and weight 145 pounds. I love how they fit and the bottoms. I haven’t wear them in the cold so we’ll see. So far I recommend them

I got these overalls to paint for senior year and i love them! i’m 6 foot 170 pounds and got a Large and surprisingly they are long enough! If you are short you may have to roll them up. People were saying the black ones smelled really bad but imo it just smells like normal new clothes. They aren’t too tight in the legs or thighs either which is what i was worried about. overall i love these overalls 🤠

I got these pants for a trip to Miami and I was very surprised when it arrived not super wrinkly . I am 5’3” about 152 lb and I got a M . It is high waisted and feels great , I wore it with flats and a bodysuit and I loved the look . The color is very pretty and it’s very light , I washed it already and it did not need to be steamed after the dryer so good quality . It’s a keeper for sure !

I got these pants for party but I thought that they would be toooo short and would not go over my shoes. BUT BABYYY LET ME TELL YOU!! I LOVE THESE JEANS. THE FIT IS PERFECT AND THEY FEEL SO GOOD. I TOLD EVERYONE THAT THEY NEED TO GET THESE JEANS BECAUSE THEY ARE A MUST HAVE. AND THIS IS COMING FROM SOMEONE THAT IS PICKY ABOUT WHAT I WEAR. I am 5'4" 188lbs I got a size 14 Because I am very hippy and my butt is not small😂 they pants hide my stomach well without any help. GET THESE 👖.

They are a little long (as pictured), but I like that better than to other short. Because they are a straight leg pant, they aren’t very form fitting and are a little baggy, but I think it looks fine from the front and back. I’m going for function more than looks. I think they’d look great On someone a little taller than me (if you’re shorter than 5’5, I’d definitely go with these pants in Short)

I got these pants so I don't always need to wear shorts in the warm climate where I live. These pants are lightweight so I'm able to wear them outside even in warm temperatures. Thank goodness! I love the orange color! I can't wear it with everything, but that's okay. I've got plenty of jeans and black bottoms. The orange is something fun and different! The fit is pretty nice. I feel like a bit of a genie or gypsy in them. I think they look best with a tight-fitting top, although the right loose top would look pretty good as well. I just haven't found one I love with them yet. I feel like I have no butt in these, haha! But then again, I don't have much of a butt to begin with so that may be the problem. The pants come folded and wrapped up in a small round container. I thought that was pretty funny! I took pictures right after taking them out so they appear to be quite wrinkled in them. However, after a wash, they're much better. I didn't iron them and I don't have many wrinkles at all. I'm 5'9" and about 151 pounds and got a size large. I think that was the right call for a person of my size. I wish I had more of a defined waist, as I think these would look cuter. Overall, I'm happy with these lightweight, ankle length pants that add a fun flair to my wardrobe!

I got these sweatpants for my daughter. She's 5'8" & about 125 lbs., I usually get her a small but I decided to grab a medium in hopes they'd give her a little more length. Over all the fit is okay; the waist fits securely but you can see from my picture they're a little "hippier" than she needs. Luckily, the hips don't stick out too much & they're not as noticeable with movement etc. Other than that, the material is of medium thickness. It's not the soft fuzzy kind inside but more of a terry cloth feel, they're comfortable & not scratchy though. I've machine washed & let them hang to dry & they come out fine. I like the pockets, but these are really just a very basic pair of sweats.

I got these when they were 30% off and once the black pair came and I tried them on I immediately went and ordered the blue pair (will update when those come). I LOVE them. Wore them this weekend and got so many compliments. For reference I am 43 years old, 5’8”, 157 pounds and got a size 6. They do have stretch but the 6 was perfect. They are so comfortable. They’ll also look great in the cooler weather with socks and boots! Update: I love the blue too!

I got this because Levi's is one of my go to brands for jeans because they fit me perfectly and they last for years. This is a good pair of jeans that is flattering and very comfortable. IMO this is one of the prettiest women's jeans they have made. The light blue color which is what I got is a very soft shade of blue that looks really nice. They feel good on and wear comfortably. These jeans are very complimentary to most anything you pair it with. I got size 12S and they fit me perfectly. I am 5'9 but I have short legs and so happy they had the 'short' available in sizing. I am very happy with they fit and look. Mine had no loose threads or sew imperfections and I am all smiles with these. All five stars ! Hope this was !

I got this for a girls trip and was not disappointed. It is so soft and easy to wear. I am 5'10 and wear a size 4, but I did size up to a medium in this set and am glad I did. The knit is a bit clingy but not in an overly warm way. It just looks more like the product photo is styled if you size up.

I got this I’m my normal size which is a Large and I’m 5’10” 165lbs and 34B in cup size and was really big on me so my daughter took bc fits her better!! She is 5’4” and about 215ish (pregnant and in her 1st trimester) and she normally purchases a size xl and the large fits her perfectly!!!

I got this in both Apricot and Khaki XS and am in love. They’re so so comfy and cute and I feel like they can actually be dressed up or down depending on what you’re going for. It feels like you’re wearing pajamas but they still look elevated and presentable. They have a linen look with very little stretch (they’re so loose that I wasn’t bothered by lack of stretch). The tag says they’re 70% cotton and 30% linen. For reference, I’m 4’10”, 108-110 lbs, 26-27” waist, 36-37” hips and I had to get around 4” hemmed for these to fit as they do in the photos. WARNING: the attached photos were all taken prior to washing; after washing according to the tag’s care instructions (cold machine wash and line dry) they appear to have shrunk around 1 inch, which is unfortunate considering I paid to have these hemmed and apparently could’ve gotten an inch less and avoided this.

I got this jacket to wear over my 46 year old dingy Elton John t shirt. I wanted the logo to show but nothing else. I had to wear it for many hours and wanted it to be soft and stretchy. This jacket was exactly what I wanted! I LOVE it!!! To the one person who commented on this jacket that the sizing was off and it was not stretchy, well she must not have gotten the same jacket. The one I got was perfect. I am 5' 3" at 135 lbs and got a large. Thank you.

I got this pair first, and then also the white one. First you see what they look like with straight bottom. They become longer after being ironed, out of the package. I'm 5'10", so it is an acceptable length. However, It's still a little short for me, when I walk and move around and I found them not very flattering. I had this idea. Purchased a pair of shoelaces in similar color; cut a small hole on the 'wrong' side of the bottom of each pant leg so that it is between the very bottom and the horizontal seam, and then I threaded a shoelace in each, to tie them around the ankle. And these pants become what you see on the last picture, with a tie on the outer side of the leg. This style is more attractive to me, and works better with this garment for my height. I did the same with the white pair. And I wear both frequently. I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, since I wear them gently and mostly for walks. I heard from other reviews that these pants shrink and pill. Shrinkage is the bigger worry. I have other linen pants that shrink and stretch with washes and wear. My plan with THESE pants is to iron them while wet after the wash, and stretch them out, then hang-dry. In my experience, this works with clothes that are prone to shrinking. At least for a while. For pilling/fuzz, I keep a fabric-shaving machine. It's been a great help for many years, and there's a great selection of them right here on Amazon. Hope this helps.

The black are a different material than the blue jeans, so that could play a part. I also haven't washed the shorts yet so I can't say if they shrink after a wash and dry. Not a horrible buy, but just be aware I guess. I'm 5'5" and about 140 lbs

I guess I didn’t realize these are ankle pants, and boy are they ankle pants! With the waist o the pants pulled up to my true waist (now they are supposed to fit) they fall at about 4 inches above my ankle. If you pull them down so that they are not ankle pants, you have a strange “sagging” look and crotch line (second pic). I am 5’4” and bought a medium at first based on the pant measurements on this site. Those were too big, so I bought the small. Those fit right, but I’m not sure I like them. I’m a “pear” shape (more weight in my bottom half than my top half) and these pants REALLY add some weight to your hips and butt. It doesn’t look bad in pics, but makes me look very disproportioned in person. They are so comfy, but I’m not sure the bare ankles and chunky rear end are worth the comfort. Just not for me.

i had a few requirements for my jeans 1. no skinny jeans 2. not over 40$ and 3. fits good and is comfortable! this hit all 3! the quality of the denim is so nice the fit looks so good on me! for reference i am 5’5 weight 100lbs size 00 before pregnancy currently i am 28 weeks and i am 120(ish). i ordered a size small in the light wash i added a picture for reference on the color too. the band is black and not tan but it doesn’t bother me it’s super stretching very soft and comfy!

I had a pair of jeans like this back in high school and I think I wore them all the time. These will be no different! They fit true to size thank goodness! They have plenty of stretch if you need it, but fit really well without the stretch. They button up easily and don't really require a belt to stay up. They look great on and even my almost 60 year old body managed to make my tush attractive. The pockets are all fully functional and the leg length is just right for my 5' 5" frame. They sit at my natural waist and are very comfortable everywhere. Not too loose, not too tight anywhere. They're made well too. While they feel like an old pair of jeans when you put them on, the seams are firm and the material is thick enough to wear in winter in New Hampshire. A very good quality jeans and plenty of room to be worn with boots.

I had a very hard time determining how these would fit me based off of reviews as there weren’t many photo reviews or reviews in general that mentioned size ordered versus body size/weight. I ordered a 14 thinking maybe they’d run small, but it ended up being the opposite. These were very loose and I will need to return and order a smaller size. They are very stretchy which I honestly like a more structured jean, but if you like stretch then these would be great. They’re soft and comfy material. The buttons worked well. They’re not a thick, structured jean so if you lean more towards liking jeggings or like room for movement/ comfortable sitting, etc then you would like these. For reference I am 30 years old, 5’7” and 225lbs. I carry weight in my stomach, arms and back the most. I feel with how stretchy these fit, I could have gotten away with a size 12 or maybe even a 10. Other jeans I own and fit into are 12-14.

I had been looking for a good pair of maternity jeans. The pair I bought previously that had a stretchy side panel, but they were always sagging down. I also don’t like full panel jeans because they feel too hot. So these are perfect for me! They stay in place and aren’t too hot. I am a size 2 prepregnancy and about 124 lbs now at 19wks. I ordered size small and it’s a good fit. I am fairly short at 5’3”, so it’s a tad long at the bottom. (But I’m used to that) I’m very pleased with these and am happily donating my old pair of jeans.

I had doubts when I read other reviewers posts but I was really pleasantly surprised. I disagree that these are not breathable—I am one of those people who tends to run hot always and these pants are flexible, easy to run around and work with and don’t crease easily or get too hot! I think they’re very flattering and as a matter of fact want all the colors! The pleats and belt loops with scrunch back waistband are flattering. For reference: I’m 5’0”, curvy, and typically wear size 10-12. Ordered the large and fits well

I had expected the pants to be longer based on the photos. That being said I'm 5'7" so that happens a lot. I cuffed them and they look great. I wear them almost every day and they are my favorite work to fun pair of pants. Photos attached to show fit.

I had high hope for these pants. Boy, I'm really disappointed when it arrives. I'm 5' & 135lbs so I ordered size 8. Turns out the crotch is super high and the legs are super long. Don't buy if you're short than 5'2". The fabric is thin. You'll need to wear a thong to avoid panty line. I like that it has stitches in the front from top to bottom which makes your leg appears longer. I also like the very color is very pretty.

I had high hopes for these pants but my goodness, they look awful on me. The material is nice--lightweight and cool, 100% cotton--and the style is cute. Unfortunately, they're terribly unflattering. They even make my legs look short and I'm 5'10" with a 34" inseam. They make me look weirdly proportioned and make my booty look nonexistent (and it's definitely in existence!). My husband was like "uh uh no way--not even just here at home"😆. Normally the dress code at home is pretty relaxed (😉), so for him to say they look so bad that I shouldn't even wear them at home is pretty bad. They wash easily (I used cold, delicate, and tumble dry low). I wouldn't order these again, but not because there's anything wrong with the quality (from what I can see just by looking); rather, it would be because they look awful on my frame. For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape. I ordered the XL, but they say 2x, so if that's the case I would definitely say they're too big.

I had high hopes for these pants. They arrived pretty well packaged, and the color is nice. The snaps on the cargo pockets work well. However, the pants were a bit too long for my wife. She is 4'11". I know they are supposed to balloon out a bit but these are too long even when lifting them up and pulling the draw strings. I suppose if you're a bit taller or have long legs then it's fine. These are really basic pants though. They have no branding or tags on them. I couldn't even find a size/care tag inside the pants. There's a torn piece of fabric hanging on a draw string on the right pants leg but I couldn't figure out where it came from. It could have happened when it was manufactured. The material is thin and my wife said it did feel comfortable. Overall, I wouldn't really recommend these pants if you can spring for similar pants with a bit higher price tag but also better quality.

I had high hopes for these shorts, but they fell flat. I bought a size small and medium. I’m 5’1” for reference. The small was pretty snug around the waist, but fit fine throughout the leg and length. The medium was just right around the waist, but was oversized throughout the leg. These shorts aren’t flattering at all, if anything they kind of make your bottom side look flatter. Both pairs felt a bit constricted when just walking, really odd, like no big allowance for stride. Not to mention the picture of the product, the denim variance is why I bought it to begin with. I loved the contrast, but the product I received was just plain and flat. Not worth the price and I’ll likely return both.
I had high hopes for these. They are cute but not soft or stretchy. Very stiff and I found them too short. For reference I’m 5’ 7” 145 lbs wearing a medium

I had higher hopes for this one. I’m 192 pounds and 5’6. I got a size large. What I like about it: the color is beautiful. The fabric is almost silky and super soft. It has pockets. What I don’t love about it: the fit was a bit weird. Because of the waist band, I expected it to hold in your tummy a bit but it doesn’t. The pants are not see through but the fabric was thin enough that if I pulled up the waist band to be flattering on my waist, you could see my cellulite on my thighs. If you’re smaller and don’t have cellulite, these would probably work great for you! The problem is if I would have sized down one, the waist band probably would have been more flattering but then the pants would have been too tight for my liking on my thighs. All together: for me personally: they’re okay, but not super flattering. I probably won’t be wearing them out of the house, but they’re super comfortable for at home, they’re loose on the legs and it’s a silkyish stretchy material.

I had low expectations for the price, but these are surprisingly flattering and comfortable. One caveat is that you need to size up. I usually wear a size 4 jeans so I ordered a size 6. They fit comfortably but are tighter in the waist and hips than my barrel jeans from Anthro. So if you want more of a baggy look at the top, you'll want to go up two sizes. I am 5'2" and the length is just right for me, so for a taller person they might be too short. The fabric is super stretchy and comfortable, color is good. They are cheaper looking than the Antro/Free People versions, but also $125 less. :-) Updated: after a couple of washings and frequent wear, the jeans are pilling. I have never seen denim pill before, so it must be from the quantity of viscose that makes them soft? I'm glad I have a pill shaver but just thought I'd mention it.

I had low expectations. Usually stuff like this looks good but when it arrives I’m so disappointed. These are well-made, good material, comfy, you literally cannot tell that all the items are printed on. No button, no belt loops, no itchy stitching, no rips. All printed on the material and they are sooo comfortable. The inside truly feels like my old ratty sweatpants but they’re cute as a button. I am 5 foot seven, 160 pounds. If I lost 10 pounds I still think they would fit. If I gained 10 pounds probably not

I had not purchased this specific style of Levi before. I checked everything online and all the sizing sounded correct. It is not as loose fitting in the thigh as I hoped, but it is not to snug. Hopefully it will relax a little. I was disappointed that the regular is sold as a 32' inseam, but it actually is 30'. I have attached a picture of the tag. I am trying to decide if I want to keep them or not, as I have black jegging's already that are 30'. Unfortunately I bought this on Prime days and the price is much higher today. Tip: If it is on sale order more than one size and return one. :-0 I am 5'4 128lbs.

I had ordered the navy pair of these leggings in M/L a week ago & absolutely loved them! They fit perfectly & are so warm & cozy. So I decided to order another pair in the skin color & they are COMPLETELY different! Different style, thickness & sizing. The skin has seams down the inside of the legs. Navy are seamless. Skin has 5" waistband, navy is 3". Skin is 8% spandex, navy is 15%. Skin has no front or back seam going up the front & back. Skin has regular leg openings at ankles, navy has sort of a stirrup. The skin pair did still fit me (5'3", 130lbs), but with less stretch than the navy pair. The fleece inside the skin pair is much thinner & not plush like the navy ones. However, the shade of this skin pair is a much better shade of "skin" than the shade shown on the website. Because I liked the shade so much I contemplated keeping them (they actually match my skin), but they were too thin to wear as pants & although they fit more like tights, the seams running down the inside of the legs made them unacceptable to me to wear as tights with my sweater dress. These had to go back & I reordered the skin colored ones from a different vendor. I'm hoping the other vendor actually sends the product advertised & doesn't pull a bait & switch as this vendor did.

I had originally ordered these jeans to wear as part of my Halloween costume. I had hoped they would have a little more flare on the leg, but they are just fine. I am 5’9” and weigh 265lb and have a mom bod. The length is definitely longer than average, but are the perfect length to wear with boots or heels. I wear anywhere between a 20-22. These are a size 20 and are snug, but definitely wearable. The denim is lightweight. The buttons are plastic, as I had read in other reviews, but seem sturdy enough. All in all, I’d say this was a good purchase!

I had super low expectations for random brand Amazon jeans but wow, I am impressed! They have their flaws for sure, but they are WAYYYY better than expected and super comfortable. Some thoughts: - Run a bit large. I am 5'7" and 140lbs. I usually wear a 27/28 waist but went down to a 27 because they are oversized and I didn't want them TOO large. The fit is PERFECT, however, they are tighter on my legs than shown in the photos, I wonder if I had went with a 28 if they would fit more "boyfriend" like. - Unbelievably comfortable. They are so stretchy and so soft but they maintain their shape. - A bit shorter on me than the model. Again, I am 5'7" and they fit above my ankle. - Rips all look cute and are well placed - Butt pockets.....thats the biggest yuck. They are placed poorly for me and are not flattering on the booty. This is the reason I dropped them a star! Overall, I have already ordered another style from this brand! They're so comfy and cute! Very very happy! Definitely consider sizing down if you between sizes though!

I had to leave a review!! I was very nervous ordering denim shorts online but these are adorable!! I’m a curvy 51 YO, 5’1, 153 lb mama. I opted for a large and they work great! Comfy, thinner fabric, nice fit. I will be ordering more! Give them a try!
I had to order a size down. Normally buy a S but ordered an XS due to some of the reviews saying they run large. Got the XS and they fit perfect!! I weigh 127 and I’m 5’5

I had wanted a pair of these jeans for the longest time but didn't want to shell out the money for the Fre* P*ople version. I ordered some from a different seller but those measurements were way off and I ended up returning. These are so perfect!!! I paid attention to the reviews and sized up. I would normally wear a 10 or sometimes an 8. I wanted these to be baggy so I went with a 12. I could probably have gotten away with a 10 but these had the baggy fit I was wanting without being too big. The material is a great weight. And, I am tall so I was worried about the length but these were perfect. They will be my go-to jeans for awhile!For reference I am about 5 feet 7 1/2 inches all and about 155 pounds.

I had zero expectations for these, but I had them recommended by an Amazon influencer and decided to give them a try! First of all, I sized UP. I wanted a super baggy look, so after reading reviews I went with a Large instead of my usual Small. I’m glad I did! They’re super high rise, baggy in the legs but tighter in the waist. However, I can still pull them down lower and wear them slouchy. There is no stretch in the fabric, FYI. Length is long on me at 5’2” but I kinda like it. Print and fabric quality is actually really nice. But please note: they smell AWFUL right out of the bag. Like, truly so bad. I had to air them out overnight in my garage, then wash them inside out with detergent and laundry sanitizer. That seems to have helped tremendously. That’s really my only complaint. Still keeping them! :)

I hardly ever write product reviews, but as a chubby girl sometimes clothing items are impossible to buy based on size charts and reviews from people who do not have the same body type. I’m 5’3” and about 220 with a mom belly from having 2 kids back to back. They hit me at the perfect length and could be easily adjusted based on shoe type. These overalls are such a nice material of denim and have a crazy amount of stretch. The distressed look is super cute and the pockets actually function, since sometimes they don’t always have real pockets. All the buttons seem really well attached and stitching is great. They are incredibly comfortable and will work perfect for all seasons!

I hardly ever write reviews, but thought I would because I love these pants! I am a pear shape, and have a hard time finding flowy pants to fit my larger hips and smaller waist. These fit my curves so well, but also fit my waist in a flattering way. The material is light and flowy too. I hate wearing shorts in the summer, so these are a great fun option! For reference, I am 5’7” and about 185 pounds. I ordered an XL.

I hate buying jeans online, but these jeans really surprised Me. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 150lbs, 28” waist, 39” hips (hourglass figure). The fit is perfect and so comfortable to wear. I like a high waisted jeans to hide my rice belly and these jeans do just that. Best part is inseam is spot on so I didn’t need to get the length altered. I will be ordering more in different washes.

I hate ordering pants of any kind online bc they are either too big or small in the waist, or too short in length. These are absolutely perfect in length and fit snug (but not too tight) at the waist. I feel like of I wear them a few hours they will loosen up some. I'm also very happy that they are not baggy around the butt or thighs. I actually like the way they look on me. I'm 5'8" and 130lbs. Ordered a 7 long

I HATE pants that ride up at the ankles, especially when I sit down. I want mine touching the ground (although preferably not dragging). I got these because of the high waist, stretch, and long option. I ordered the longest inseam, and I love the length. It’s size 6, and I’m about 5’6”, 130 lbs. They’re honestly a smidge big, but they fit my waist without feeling like they’ll fall off, and I don’t like my pants to be too snug anyways. I don’t think I’ll exchange for a size 4, even though that’s probably a better fit. I also don’t think these stretch a ton, but that’s probably better since they’re already a little large. That said, the smell really is terrible. I haven’t made up my mind if I’ll keep them. If they shrink and come out crisp from the wash, it’s worth it, but if they don’t, it’s a waste of money.

I have 35" waist and 48" Hips. I wear typically 14W or 16/17 jeans. I got XXL as I don't like painted on pants. These fit comfortably. My ankles have blood flow to my feet with this size. The waist isn't digging in and they are warm. The fleece is something new I've never had pants with that before but come snowy days that sounds spectacular.

I have 35" waist and 48" Hips. I wear typically 14W or 16/17 jeans. I got XXL as I don't like painted on pants. These fit comfortably. My ankles have blood flow to my feet with this size. The waist isn't digging in and they are warm. The fleece is something new I've never had pants with that before but come snowy days that sounds spectacular.

I have a 26 inch waist and 40 inch hips. I usually can wear a 28 in Levi’s but went with 29. These are only slightly stretchy mostly denim. They’re nice and sturdy denim but flexible not stiff. I had about 1-2 inches of a waist gap but I almost always do with Levi’s or non curvy clothing. I absolutely love the length as I hate my cellulite on the back of my upper thighs so this conceals it. I am 5’3” for reference. It is a button up vs zipper. Holds the mom pooch in well. They do bag out a bit after wearing 5-6 hours I noticed they were more loose and baggy than when I first put them on but they stayed at that level of being bagged out for the rest of the day.

I have a 26" waist and, for where I like my belts to sit, this was a bit big. After adding two holes, it fit perfect. The triangle buckle is long making it a bit of a hassle to put on and remove. Keep in mind that the buckle prong won't sit correctly if the belt isn't a tighter fit (see last image). Even with the quirks, this is super duper cute and I will be wearing it lots :)
I have a 29” waist and 40” hips, and the medium fit me perfectly! They feel and look high quality. Good stretch. Comfortable. Excellent jeans!
I have a 32 inch inseam and they were perfect. Got them in white. Wore them to work. Not a soul knew they were yoga pants. Going to purchase more in different colors.
I have a big bum so hard to find jeans that support behinds. 5'7 220lbs wear size xl/16. These fit perfect for a round behind and wide hips.

I have a few articles of clothing from this brand and they never disappoint. I find this brand truth worthy. They are not at all see through but are still lined with an inner shorts. I did wash and lined dried these before wearing so they are wrinkled in the picture - nothing an iron can't fix. They are a little longer on me but fall right at the ankles - this was to be expected for my stature. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small fits me best. So, do order true to size. All the stitching and sewing was done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. I'm looking forward to wearing this during the warmer months.

I have a hard time finding dress pants that fit this good, so I’m thrilled I found these pants! They look much more high end than expected. Love the pockets. These fit me better than pants I’ve gotten from J Crew and the gap. They have plenty of stretch to the fabric, don’t wrinkle, and look classy. I’m 5’2”, 140lbs, and carry weight in my tummy from having 3 c-sections. These pants in size 8 fit amazing! They hit at my belly button and aren’t tight feeling when buttoned. The zipper does not slide down and stays put even without the button done. I’m wearing the “Peacoat” color, which looks navy blue in person. I’m going to get them hemmed about an inch and a half so they look more like the length on the model. Awesome pants!!

I have a lot of good things to say about these jeans! First off, the high waist is just perfect, it cinches the waist beautifully, creating a flattering silhouette. I also love the wide-leg style; it gives a lengthening effect that makes my legs look longer and adds a touch of retro chic to my look. The square pockets are a unique and also have a bit of a vintage vibe, which I adore. The classic denim is another highlight. It’s thick and stretchy, very comfortable,great for cooler autumn and winter weather. Plus, they’re super easy to style. I find they pair especially well with crop tops, creating a trendy, well-put-together outfit. Despite the quality and style, though, I’m not entirely satisfied with the Fit. I always order a Medium, but these jeans run large. I need a belt to keep them up. Also, the length is a bit much for me, given that all sizes have the same 30" inseam. At 5’2” and 116 pounds, they’re too long for my frame. If you’re on the shorter side and don’t want to hem them, keep in mind that they might be a bit lengthy. Overall, for style and quality, these jeans deserve a solid 5/5. If I were taller, the small size would have fit perfectly. Just be mindful of the sizing if you’re petite!

I have a lot of pairs of leggings and joggers from this brand and I've always been impressed that they come all the way down to my ankles, because I'm 5'10" and have trouble finding pants that are long enough. So I assumed these would fit similar to the same brand and size of leggings and joggers, but they're a full 2 inches shorter, as you can see in the photo. If you're average height or shorter they'll probably be fine, but I will not be purchasing this style again. Other than the length I like them. I'm keeping them because I got them for a Halloween costume and don't have time to replace them.

I have a male body, 6’3 and 220lbs. 30/34 pant size with a low torso and athletic legs. I never thought to cross the aisle and try these jeans but I am so happy that I did!! I got a large low rise stretch jean and they fit perfectly. Rides wonderfully on my thighs and there is very little snug on my calves. Hips are great and even the butt lift support gives the illusion that I actually have a butt!

I have a pair of stretchy pants that are “work acceptable” in white. I wanted a pair in black for those days that I just want to be comfortable. These are amazing! They feel like pajama pants. I am usually a XL, but I sized down to a L. For reference, I am 5’6 and weigh 212 lbs.
I have a really hard time finding jeans that fit. These are great! Usually in between a size 12/14. Ordered size 14x34 and they’re perfect. I am 5’6” and weigh about 205.

I have a small waist and a nice butt... I purchased a medium. My measurements are 27.5 in waist 42.5 in hips. In the pic with the pink shirt you can see how they’re loose in the back. And how the scrunch up in the front. Still like them. Maybe I’ll purchase more.

I have a very hard time finding jeans my size. I'm 5"1' and 94/95 lbs, with a small waist and regular hips, so almost everything is too big for me. I bought the 0 Short size and they did fit me perfectly. It's also midrise (see photo showing my belly button for reference) so they don't go too high. They are comfortable and soft. They are still a bit long for me but that's an easy fix! Overall I'm very satisfied.

I have a very hourglass shape and hate the gapping that occurs or flashing my underwear when kneeling down with typical jeans, and I didn't want to spend $80+ on blue jeans! I have lost a lot of weight over the last year, from a 22 down to a 12 that's getting loose and this style has been very kind to me. I just buy a couple pairs in a smaller size as I reach it! The fact that I don't even bother trying on any other brands, and have been consistently happy with the fit over so many sizes (I think I started with these at size 18?) should tell you something! I am short too, 5'3", and the short length is PERFECT!

I have an event coming up so I decided to order these and I really like them. They are light and fit well. I'm 5'4.5" and I will definitely need to wear at least 2" heels with these but I planned to anyway. The picture with my hands out of my pocket I am barefoot and the one from the back with hands in my pockets are with heels. I have my black shirt tucked in with army green shorts and neither were seen through the pant fabric. Glad I ordered these.

I have been in search of some baggy jeans for a while now. I am 2 months postpartum and nun of my jeans fit! So I bought these color: Medium Wash and they’re so cute and comfortable. i have been looking for a nice pair of mom jeans for so long. im 5’6 and weigh 135lbs, my waist measures at 28.5” and i ordered a size 28. I have long legs and most mom jeans i find are cropped so they look like capris on me. i was skeptical about purchasing these because of the price but overall these are so perfect i am so happy with how they fit/look! they dont stretch like at all and they’re really stiff material. the front is slightly odd near the crotch but other then that i am so happy with these. they are so cute and i will definitely be getting another pair!

I have been looking for a pair of fleece lined translucent leggings so a while.. so many reviews said they were too short or too tight etc.. I’m 5’9” round 135 lbs and got the m/l they fit perfectly and at plenty long on these long legs lol. I have been looking for a pair of jeans that will fit my curvy body and Ladies I FOUND THEM. I wanted looser-fit jeans that still hugged my curves in all the right places and these are them... I have been looking for a pair of pants like this for a long time. I am 5’4” and ordered the Large 29”. They are perfect and I received many compliments on them today. I have been looking for some cropped wide leg jeans for a while and haven’t liked any of them. I have been struggling to find the perfect pair of cargo jeans until now...these ETELLO jeans are everything! The quality of the jeans are great! I have been trying to find a nice, comfortable pair of jeans that weren't skinny or above my belly button. I always have an issue with pants buttons pushing into my gut any time I sit down. So I went with the 16 short and liked them for the most part. The issue was that they were a little baggy in the crotch and had a habit of sliding down. I included a squatting picture just to emphasize how stretchy and comfortable they are. The pictures with the green shirt are size 16 short, the purple shirts are the size 15 short. I would one hundred percent recommend these pants. I intend to get another pair later.

I have not worn jeans for 5 years, but these are comfortable and cute. The reasons why are-1) I have Interstitial Cystitis which means I cannot endure any pressure on my pelvic floor, and 2) I gained a lot of weight during CoVid lockdown so nothing fit. I’m finally down a little. Currently I am 5’7”, 178pounds, and hips measure 45”. I think that makes me size 14-16 depending on fit. I got the XL. I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

I have owned the fleece lined legging version of this pant for 3 years and loved them but it irks me to walk in the cold with no pocket for my phone. Shoving it in the front of my pants always cause my shirt to get tangled in it and I looked like a crazy person trying to keep my pace and wrestle it in the front of the pants. Enter the pocketed version. I ordered exact same size. Fit like a glove, right under my belly button, though the pocket is tapered and my phone is tight, I didn't think much of it and went on my walk this morning. I only did 1.75mi because I was so disgusted with how far these pants had fallen while walking that I turned around. I attached pics of before and after walk. I pulled them up once while walking and they resumed hip position again almost immediately. Also, due to the tapered pocket, only the top portion of your phone fits in the top, so you have to push, wiggle, wiggle, push, wiggle wiggle to get it far enough in, so it won't fall out...all while trying to keep pace. Within half a mile, I only push, wiggle, wiggled once and it fell right out and hit the ground...in the dark. Whomever designed this pocket, had zero thought about functionality while walking or running. Sure, the pocket looks sleek but it's awful. For reference, I have the S21 which is only exactly 3 inches wide. Do not buy these pants if you have an S10 or the giant Iphone because they won't fit. The only plus about these pants is that the fabric is very lint and pet hair resistant. I'm 5'5"/160# and ordered the large. I'm super pissed that I just wasted over $60 for fleece leggings that are pure caca

The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.

I have these in both the navy and the black. Purchased xs in both, I'm 115 lbs 5'5" for reference. Fit true to size. I've worn them running, biking, hiking, lounging, and running errands. Sooooo soft and cozy on the inside, pockets on either leg large enough to fit my Samsung Note (good sized phone), plus a small pocket on the waist band for card, key, etc. They been washed several times, turned inside out, and hung to dry with no noticeable wear. Hold their shape well. And, are more flattering than my more expensive cold weather leggings that cost 3 x what these did! A definite staple in my northeastern winter wardrobe! I plan to purchase more colors. Oh, and they're high-waisted, sit right at the smallest part of your waist to nip it right in! Perfect!

I have these pants in blue and black. I never wear the tie that came with them. They're slightly on the cheaply made size but still hold up well during wear and washing. They're comfortable to wear to the office. I am 5'3" and 130 lbs.

I have these pants in rust and apricot and I really love them. For reference: I am 5' 6" and about 125lbs. Pros: drawstring waist, lightweight, pockets, flattering for a flatter figure despite being a more flowy cut. They can be dressed up or down - I can wear them with almost any crop top...so great for travelling if you want to pack light but still have options. I could wear flats without tripping on the hem. Cons: I read reviews where they shrunk in the wash - I have avoided this potential issue by doing the lazy thing and not washing them. The lighter pair IS see-through, but I just wore nude spandex to remedy this issue.

I have to agree with other reviewers who referenced these as a Halara dupe 👍🏽. Every thing I need them to be: cute ✅ comfy ✅ perfect for sitting at a computer and getting work done ✅ running errands ✅ going to brunch ✅ lounging and relaxing ✅ not suddenly uncomfortable after eating a meal ✅. I just bought 4 more pairs in different colors as these will def be in my regular rotation of outfits. The material drapes well and doesn’t look too casual if you pair it with a dressier top and accessories. I have a 33 inch waist and a wear variable pant sizes depending upon the brand. I got a large in these and they fit perfectly! They were wrinkled on arrival, but I just sprayed them with wrinkle release and put them in the dryer for a few minutes and now they are free of wrinkles. Great addition to my wardrobe 👍🏽.

I have to start off by saying, just go ahead and add this dress to cart! It’s comfortable, lightweight, cute, and great quality. I wore this to the Houston rodeo at 25 weeks pregnant and received a lot of compliments. For reference I’m 5’7”, 180 lbs, a size 10/12 and ordered the large. It fit perfect! I’ll even be able to wear this postpartum which is nice. Definitely recommend for spring and summer!

I have tried on a lot of wide leg pants and these are my favorite. Very flowy material that drapes well. Comfortable, roomy, and elegant. I am 5’3 179 lbs with big tummy. Amazon recommended XXL. Technically it fit but was stylishly baggy, but too long unless I wear heels. I ordered M, L and XL to compare. Medium fit best because it was shorter length so I can wear it with flats. Can feel it is tighter at waist but still stretchy and comfortable. L feels like my correct size in waist but a little long, so am sticking with medium. L would be good with heels or I can hem them. I feel sizing is accurate overall since I am petite enough to wear size 8-10 M in oversized styles but tend to wear size 10-12 jeans or dresses. My recommendation is to choose your usual size if you are tall or wear heels. Size down if you are petite or short, because the style is so loose and flowy that it is better to get the length rightMy photos: XXL in khaki, M in brown and L in light blue

I have trouble with pants because I have wide set hips, a tummy, and Itty bitty chicken legs with no butt. These are a great solution because they're so stretchy!!! They fit comfortably around waist but aren't super baggy in the butt and legs. Still a bit bigger in those areas than I'd like but in a not quite tight enough way not a baggy way. They also cover my ankles even though they're high wasted and pulled up past my belly button! I'm almost 5'10 so pants always look like high waters on me. The most impressive thing about these was the fabric quality. Normally if I find jeans with amazing stretch like this then they're super super thin or like jegings. It doesn't bother me I like thin pants but then they wear and tear very fast. These are very thick sturdy durable denimaterial but SO stretchy and comfortable!!! An awesome pair of flattering jeans! Definitely a good order!

I have very long skinny legs and have a really hard time finding stretch jeans that fit my legs and small butt. These fit perfectly, I ordered a 4 long, I have a 29” waist, with skinny legs and I have a 33” inseam and I absolutely love the fit. They have a good amount of stretch but not too much that they lose their shape, comfortable and well made.

I have wanted a denim jacket for years now, but never wanted to pay the price of one. I was a little skeptical of this because of the price, but I LOVE IT! I ordered the 3x. and My weight is 260 lbs. It fits perfect! The denim is not tight at all! It feels as if you have leggins on lol. Definetly didnt expect it. The little added details at the top makes it even better to. Such a good buy!

I have wanted to purchase a pair of bell bottom for a while and was nervous about this purchase from Amazon. I'm so happy i got them. Great purchase. I highly recommend. When I first tried these jean I instantly love the fit. My hubby even gave me a compliment on them. I have hips/stomach and these pants made me look slimmer. I'm 5'4", 187 lbs and the jeans are a size large. I plan on ordering the white and blue pair net.
I have washed these once so far and will wash them again because they smell like chemicals. I am 5ft 165lbs 38 bust 38 waist 44 hips got the xl and they fit fine will update if I get the smell out.

I have worn and washed these pants a number of times before this review. I really like the cargo pockets for my phone - it keep the phone secure and flush with your thigh. The fabric is soft and stretchy and very comfortable. I am about 5'9 and these feel a little bit short for me, would have loved an extra inch of length. The pants fit extra snug around the thigh area, this might partially be due to the cargo pockets and the extra seams they introduce. The back pockets are functional and have nice placement. The waistband is high and doesn't dig in. Great pants for when you want to comfort of yoga pants, warmth of fleece, but still want them to look a bit more like slacks.

I haven’t ever worn corduroy type cargo pants before, but I LOVE these! They’re so much softer than I anticipated, fit nicely, and look genuinely cute. I’m 5’6 and 125 lbs and the small fit well. I love the high waisted aspect too, and think these are generally well-executed. I haven’t put them through the dryer, but they seem to hold up well through washing. Would recommend!

I haven’t had a denim jacket in years. I loved the last one I had and had worn it so much over the years it was just too worn out. I can across this one and because of the reviews gave it a try. It’s a great jacket. The arms are a bit long for me but I always have that issue with any jacket. I plan to wear it with the sleeves rolled up a bit anyway. I’m 5’2” and approximately 135lbs. I didn’t really try and wear the jacket closed yet but the jacket definitely has stretch which helps make it comfortable.

I haven’t worn a pair of overalls in ages! These are incredibly comfortable and they fit great! At 5’7” and 175 pounds (typically a size L/14), I got an XL in these. I can pull them on and off without using the side buttons, so I probably could have gone with a large, but the baggy look works for this style and I find them to be super cute! The denim fabric is soft and feels really durable. I might get another pair in a large in the darker blue color, but for late summer and fall wear, these are a great buy for the money!

I haven't worn jeans in ten years. I had these in my cart for months and rly happy I decided to finally get them! They fit true to size. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 175lbs. 32 waist, 45 hips. Usually I’m a size 12/14, but I ordered these in 16 for a looser fit and glad I did! The front pleats are well placed for a flattering fit, especially if you’re curvier. The material is fairly thick, but also stretchy. Surprisingly comfortable! They hold up well in the wash & don’t shrink when dried. The rise hits at just the right spot — although the waist is a little baggy on me, so I have to wear a belt. Very pleased with the quality. I never spend more than $20 on pants, but these are worth the money for sure. 10/10 recommend. If you’re questioning getting them, do itttt:)

I haven’t worn Lucky jeans in 20 years but I needed something casual and comfortable to replace some that ripped over the holidays. Ha. Gave these a go and went up a size and they are perfection! I am 5’8” 130lb and I ordered a 25w 32l and they will fit perfect over low boots, tennis kicks or flats/flips. A perfect daily pant. Hope this helps someone!

I haven’t worn these out yet, but first impressions are good. The color is rich and the material feels sturdy. I’m 5’6”, ~140 lbs and the Medium fit my waist and legs great. There is a slight bunching of the fabric near the crotch but nothing crazy. I could have gone for an inch or 2 more in length, so if you have long legs, you may be getting high waters with these. The high waist and stretchy fabric make them comfortable to sit in and pretty easy to move around in.
I hit the jackpot!! I’m 5’3 135lbs and the size 9 fit perfect!!

I honestly didn’t think I would like how these fit. I’m 44 so I rocked the flare pant 25 years ago. I remember them being too low rise, too tight in the thighs and too long. But these are perfection! I’m 5’4, 119 pounds and I got a 26 short. They are the perfect length. I won’t be putting them in the dryer as that will shrink them for sure. If you’re 5’4 or under I would go with the short.

I immediately purchased a second pair. And then told my mom and friends to order them. These pants feel like BUTTA! They are so comfortable and have a coolness to them which is perfect for someone who works in the Florida heat. I’m 5’8 and probably about 160 (thank you quarantine weight) and I am very bottom heavy.

I initially ordered this product in a small but it was tight. I’m weigh 138, size 4/6, and have an athletic build with larger thighs. Medium worked much better. Soft. The right amount of stretch. A little shorter than I’m used to, but not obscene and only wearing at home. Gave it a 4 in some areas due to a weird crotch issue and because when I put my hands in the pockets to adjust the lining properly, it almost went through the ripped section so I have to be cautious. A great buy and glad I purchased. I’ll keep the small as I’m losing weight and it will fit great in another 5-10 lbs.
I just got my skort today and tried it on and fell in love with it!!! I got a large, I’m 5’ 4” and 160 lbs and I’m a solid 8 in pants and shorts. I can’t wait to wear it in Miami this weekend!

I just got them, so too soon to tell for the long term, but right off the bat: the material and fit is super cute, comfortable, and flattering. (For reference- I tend to be a size 8/medium and am about 5'4" tall and I got the 2-10 size) The material does seem a bit cheap so I can see how they would tear like in other reviews. Good thing I am not a gymnast or flexible because I could see them tearing right open if you did the splits. Would buy again if they keep the price low/affordable since they are obviously not meant for long term use. Will likely launder in a bag and on the gentle cycle in attempt to keep them in tact for awhile (fingers crossed!) Update: even being very careful with washing- got a tear right after the first use. Such a bummer since they seemed great otherwise. Would only buy again if priced under $20. Wouldn't pay more since you cannot guarantee more than one use.

I just got these and I love them! I ordered the light gray and the dark blue pair and they're both great! I am 5'9" and regularly size 16, I ordered the 3x and they fit great! I’m so in love with the ways these feel and look! I can wear them longer or roll them and make them shorter. Both look great! I swear I’ve ordered and returned so many things this year. Nothing feels or looks right. But these are my absolute favorite! Great stretch and soft feel! Great quality. I’m over the moon thrilled with this purchase!

I just got these yesterday and I had to hop on here to say that THEY ARE WONDERFUL. The ONLY bad thing I can find to say about them is there are no actual back pockets. I almost sized up but I’m glad I didn’t. They are stretchy and SO soft and lightweight. And the length could not be better for me. I have a hard time finding good fitting bottoms as a pear-shape with big hips/thighs/butt. Hence my over-excitement. Really can’t recommend enough! Love them!

I may try a size up for a looser fit but overall I'm really happy and wear these almost every day.
I just have to say I love these. They fit perfectly and the material is super soft, not scratchy at all! I am 5'0", 118 lbs and ordered a size 4.

I just received these today. Haven't washed or dried them, so not sure if they will hold their shape and size, but first impressions are great. The fit was perfect, I am 5'6 with longer legs than torso, and 125 lbs. As you can see my toes stick out, but the jeans are otherwise on the ground. I just took it out of the package and tried it on. I was looking for a long, white blouse. I love the fact that it has 85% cotton and only 15%polyester. The material feels a little more rough, but it is not uncomfortable. You can see the black pants through the blouse, but for layering it is great. I’m 5‘4, 152 pounds and pear shaped.

I just tried these on and I’m wondering what the raves are about in hiding the lower belly that is stretched from having kids. As I sit, muffin top is real. I’m 5’3 188 pounds so I ordered a 16 short. I received a size 16 but had to roll the pants up to fit my height so didn’t receive short. On the model the pants seem to hug the legs to show great legs but on me the knees are super baggy which is very disappointing. The thigh is baggy, the calf fit is good but these are not short and my knees look like they lost 100 pounds bc the fit is insulting. I utterly hate the process of returns and wish Amazon offered a doorstep return. These cost too much to be disappointed. To avoid the whole return process I’ll just chalk them up to yard pants and take this as this brand isn’t providing the look the model is wearing bc of her knee coverage. I’ve found better on Amazon with pants that are about $20. I’ll take my business there. Not as promised. Any pants I have are a size 16 and have never experienced such a muffin top nor - just picture this - you’re wearing tight leggings but the knee fit is 3xs your knee size.

I just wanna preface this written review and say that yes, I’m a dude, and believe that this is a great 90’s look jeans purchase regardless of your gender. The men’s ones out there are just horrendous and I’m tired of buying and then returning (Amazon and in-store like Levi’s or GAP), and the ones from Temu never fits me because of the horrible sizing. I’m 5’9 with a size 30 waist and an athletic build (I wear size L or XL depending on brand for men’s shirt). After numerous research, I find that there some dudes out there still that still swears by women’s jeans only. Since why not? I used to wear tight skinny women’s jeans back in my emo-phase days in the early 2000’s anyways, so I decided to check out this baggy version that is in-style nowadays as I really like the style. I ordered a large and they fit okay, I could’ve sized down to a medium though, but the large is still good with a belt on. These are stretchy, lightweight, and comfortable. However, just like the women’s jeans back in the day when I used to wear the skinny ones, I’m still disappointed that women’s jeans doesn’t have a big pocket like the men’s jeans do. Why is that? Oof. I wish that would change as some of the ladies out there have these mega tablet-like size phone, and with my iPhone Pro Max, it was sticking out and when I sit down, or shift to a weird angle, they slide off. Same thing for my wallet. Overall though, these are the exact jeans I wanted and highly recommend.

I just want to saw that I really do genuinely like these pants! I purchased the size small (5'5 & 135) and they fit me really well. Loved the pockets & they looked great with a few different pair of heels. However, I returned because the belt is only held in place by those 2 loops on the hips. Just while trying them on the belt was moving all over in the back and exposing the zipper. When I sat down the belt would rise above the pants and onto my lower back. Please please add some extra loops in the back or sew the belt in place and I will happily repurchase!

I kept seeing these on my insta feed and finally caved and purchased them, and honestly, I kinda love them. I am a small and 5’1 and they are long on me but works with my chunky sandals. The material is a thin jean so it works for summer months. 10/10 recommend if you’re looking for a fun style of overalls.

I kept seeing these pants come up in a reel for a Flamingal ad on FB and I loved them but the model was so tiny and in a little cropped top and I thought they wouldn’t look as good on me and I didn’t know or trust an FB ad but after a few weeks of seeing them I decided to order them but I looked for them on Amazon instead of ordering through FB and there they were! I had them in two days and I’m in love with them! I ordered a M in khaki. I’m 5’3 and weigh about 160lbs and they fit perfectly. I kept seeing reviews saying they're too big so that’s why I ordered a medium instead of a large so maybe go down a size. They’re so comfortable and so stylish! I wore them from 7am to 6pm and they looked exactly like they did when I put them on that morning. They are stretchy but they do NOT bag out at all. The quality of these pants is excellent. They do not feel cheap at all and that’s coming from someone who only wears $300 Mother Denim jeans and thousand dollar shoes so trust me when I tell you that these pants are nice and not cheap. I’m actually ordering another color right now. I think the dark grey and also the charcoal color. I highly recommend them!

I kid you not - these overalls are AH-MAZING! I'm 5'4" and 160lbs of muscle and finding pants that fit is like trying to find a needle in a haystack. If they fit my hips, I can't zip or button. If they fit my tree trunks of a leg, then they're much too big in the hips. These are a PERFECT fit. A beautiful and comfortable mix of loose and hugging in just the right places. These really bring me back to my childhood days of wearing overalls. I have received umpteen compliments of how they fit/look! Do yourself a favor and get yourself a pair or 5.
I kind of wish I would have sized down, I do like the oversized look though. It is nice I can wear a hoodie underneath. I am 135 lbs and ordered a Medium.

I like how the Dickies look on my wife and can't detect any obvious issues, but my wife feels these jeans are oddly sized. When I looked more closely with a discernible eye, I concur that it's an imperfect fit. The basic design suggests form fitting comfort and in some areas of the body, it delivers on that point. But where some areas fit right, other spots are incongruous and are not a good match on my curvy wife. First off, the size 10 R is long. My 5'5 ½" wife wore 2 inch heeled boots (see photos) and the leg hem is to the floor. In all fairness, my wife wears size 8-10 pants and perhaps the 8 would work better. These definitely run large. She needs to keep pulling them up as they slide down, even over her curvy hips. She will need to wear a belt and high heels to make them work. They look good around the legs and derriere, but are too wide on the waist and feel loose and bunchy around the hips and crotch. The actual material feels like value fabric. These are not super smooth jeans. They feel and look a wee vintage and in that regard seem like old fashioned jeans of the 80s but with a little more stretch than pure cotton pants of the past. The stitching and weave is decent and the button and zipper work great. They appear to be sturdy, breathable pants that will last a long time. The pockets are nice and deep and there is a slight stretch throughout, but almost too much stretch to the point of slippage and instability. My wife isn't sure if she will wear the Dickies much as she has other shape jeans that look and feel better. But all things considered, it's a good value for a basic classic bootcut jean for the right body type. Just bear in mind that they run long and large. ~ Kort

I like the fit and flattering style of this LookbookStore Women's Short Sleeve Button Down Flowy Tiered Babydoll Denim Dress. For size reference, I am 5'4", pear shaped, 36b, size 10, 150lbs. I picked up the medium in the reef blue and I am happy with the color and the fit. It is a good length on me. My calves aren't bad, but above the knee things take a turn for the worse. This dress hits me just right, short enough to be cool and comfortable in the summer, but long enough to fully hide my thighs. The empire waist is flattering and gives me shape without overwhelming me with ruffles. It is an interesting design, as the tiers are only in the back. The front is flat with just the one seam at the waist. The sleeve is nice as well, decent in length to hide my upper arms. Made in China from 85% cotton, 15% polyester, this dress appears to be well made, the buttons are well sewn and all the seams and hems are finished nicely. Despite the cotton/polyester blend, the fabric is not stretchy at all, but I went with my usual size and it fits well. I just wanted to point out that I am not at all busty, I carry my weight in the bottom, so this style dress works well for me. If you have a large chest or are very broad across your back, you may find this dress too restricting. Overall, I am very happy with this dress and think it will serve me well this summer, even into the fall. This can be worn very casually with a pair of Converse sneakers to a back yard picnic, or dressed up a bit with a sexy strappy sandal and a great chunky bracelet for dinner out. It would even work well as a bathing suit cover up. I could also see this with boots and a little cardigan for fall. It washed up well, I did a cold water wash and hung it to dry. There was minimal shrinkage and no color fading, it didn't wrinkle too badly either. Bottom line, a great wardrobe basic that you will reach for often!

I like these Dokotoo cargo-style hiking shorts more than I thought I would. They fit me pretty well, and I like that the waistband is tie-style, it’s stretchy and adjustable. The material itself has some stretch and give, it isn’t completely stiff but don’t expect it to stretch a whole size or anything. For reference, I’m about 130lbs at 5’5”, and my measurements are 36”/28”/38”. I usually wear a size 8 in pants, though I recently lost some weight so I might fit a 6. Medium works well on me! The pant legs are a little larger than my thighs, but I’d rather have them be a bit too big over too small. The only things I’m not as thrilled about is the fact the pockets on the back aren’t real (but the front pockets are and nice and roomy!), and that the care instructions seem difficult. The label says it must be hand-washed cold, and can’t be dry cleaned or tumble dried, and to cool iron if needed. I’m not sure who really has time to hand wash their clothes, but it isn’t me! However I did throw them in my washer on a regular cycle with the rest of my laundry (on cool temp water) and they seemed to come out just fine. I hung them up to dry and it did seem like they wrinkled more than I would like, but that’s nothing an iron can’t handle. Overall I’m liking these shorts and will continue to test and wear them over the summer! So far they’re very comfortable and trendy. I recommend them and hope they will last me at least for the next few seasons!

I like these jeans quite a bit. I broke my foot and wanted something that would fit over my orthopedic boot, and these did great. But I also found them to be very flattering. I'm 5'7" and about 200 lbs, usually a size 14-16, with a slight pear-shaped body and I carry most of my weight in my belly (Mother of six, any surprise?) The 16 fit me nicely, and I don't think I would have been comfortable in a 14. The jeans are fitted through my thigh and knee and open up below that. They just skim the floor when I wear low sneakers or flats, so they can easily be worn with some heels and boots as well. The waist is the definition of mid-rise, coming just above my hips and about to my belly button. After a couple days of wear, they loosened just the tiniest bit, which added to the comfort but didn't have to be hitched up constantly. (That's important to me as a mom!) I got a darker wash, which I like but they kind of turned my hands blue before I washed them. I definitely recommend washing these separately the first time. These aren't "Oh my gosh! Best jeans ever!" But they are very good jeans, and I don't regret the purchase at all, especially at this price, and with the option of Amazon wardrobe. I also tried the Amazon Essentialls mid-rise authentic bootcut jean and preferred these because of the fitted upper leg, which the "authentic" style lacked.
