I was not happy with these jeans I bought a medium. I’m normally a large weighing in at 170 5’4 . The bottom of the legs are not skinny jeans at all like in the picture. I had to roll them up at the bottom to give a more appealing look. I also had to pull them up as if they were hiwaist pants and put a belt on for them to even fit halfway decent. I tried to send them back for a small and the only option they gave was to get my money back.

I was on the fence about getting a 24 or 25. I went with 24 based on measurements and it’s perfect! Proper low rise, hugs the right places, and comfy. It feels tight at first but has a little bit of stretch room. For reference I’m 5’3”, 24in waist, 35 inch hip
I was on the fence about these, but I’m so glad I bought them! They are so comfy, and cute! I’m 5’10 and they were perfectly long enough too.

I was on the look out for a new denim jacket and couldn't be happier! I'm 5' 3", 165lbs and ordered a large. The fit is loose, the color is perfect, it's comfy, sylish, and could be dressed up or dressed down. If you're looking for a feminine denim jacket then add to cart!

I was pleasantly impressed with these pants. I am 5'6", 29" waist, 40" (39") hips, 136lbs. These feel a bit like parachute pants. They are 17% nylon after all. But they do have a good amount of stretch to them. I wish they were a little more supportive in the rear and that might have been achievable maybe one size down if I thought they were really stretchy enough for that sort of feature but that's just wishful thinking for a forty-something y/o whose assets aren't where they used to be. The waistband is very comfortable, the length is just where I want it, and I think they look really good on my body. As for my measurements, I am 5'6" but I have short-ish legs and a long torso - specifically my "rise". Like, high-rise pants fit me more like mid-rise because there is over eleven inches between my crotch and my bely button. I mention this because these pants are a little tight in the crotch for me and have a little camel toe effect but the waist is so forgiving, I can adjust and pull them down whenever I feel them creeping up in there;) Plus they are black and it's not noticeable. I don't think that would be the case with lighter colors though which is a bummer because I like these pants so much I'm tempted to buy them in all the colors. Anyway, lots of reviews complaining about how they don't hold up to washing but it clearly states that these pants are best dry cleaned. I never dry clean anything so I'll probably wash on cold in the gentle or hand wash cycle and hang to dry. I included front (heeled and flat footed), back, and side pics because I know that even someone with the same measurements as me may have a different shape. Hope this info helps.

I was pleasantly surprised at how much I loved these! Not too long and the perfect length with a little heel. I’m 5’3 and the fit so perfectly in all the right places. They have a heavier feel to them but do have stretch. I’m 165 and bought a size 10.

I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of this belt, the strap is sturdy and the buckle is comparable to the ones I've seen at Urban Outfitters or Topshop. I have a 25" waist and bought the 24"-28" belt. The fit is quite true to size. In addition, the width of the belt is about 1 inch, which might be a tight fit if your belt loops are on the smaller side. I've worn this belt maybe 10+ times and have noticed minimal scuffing. Overall, it's a great quality belt for a decently inexpensive price.

I was pleasantly surprised by these pants. The inseam says 20 inches. Being that I'm only 4'11 I was hoping that they would fit more like a wide leg crop instead of a capris. They fit just as I was hoping!! I'm about 140lbs and usually wear size 10 and the sizing was spot on. The material seems to be nice quality with a little stretch! I would recommend and plan to order another color.

I was ready to love these jeans because of the shape and style, but unfortunately I’m stuck with a pair of jeans that stretches out too much and looks too big after just one wear. I’m 5’7, 180lb, 33” waist, 45” hip - based on the size chart first I ordered size 14, they fit somewhat big and didn’t “suck me in” at all, so I exchanged them for a size 12. At first 12 fit pretty well, comfortable, just loose enough but not sloppy or baggy. However after just one wear, they stretched out too much and are not as flattering as I would like them to be. I should have sized down at least to a 10. As far as the quality - these jeans are well made, no complaints. I feel like the color is somewhat different in person, definitely has a “vintage” look to it. Not a deal breaker just a matter of personal preference. Bottom line - if it wasn’t for the sizing, these jeans could have been a good find.

I was really excited for these pants and love the way they look online. In person I was disappointed a little with some aspects. For reference I am 5‘,5“ tall and 115 pounds. My measurements are 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips. I ordered the size extra small and it fit perfectly. It was even slightly snug in the waist and hips on me, but not a dealbreaker. I was disappointed online the color is a very pretty light sky blue. However in person it’s a darker, dusty blue color. I already have pants this color and really wanted a light pale, blue pant. The other thing I didn’t care for are the front pleats they’re not very flattering and add a little bit of bulk to me. Finally they were also a little bit short. I’m only 5‘5“ tall and these fell right at my ankles to where I really need to wear flats with them in order for them to not look like high waters. I typically like my pants longer, and to cover most of my shoe. All in all they are an OK pair of pants. I wasn’t thrilled at the color in person is not the same and didn’t particularly like the front pleats but I will still wear them. Hope that helps!

I was really hoping to love these jeans. The reviews were mixed so I knew it would be a crap shoot whether they worked or not. Unfortunately, these are a swing and a miss for me. I’m 5ft 7in, 165lbs, carrying most of my weight in my belly. I wear 8/10 bottoms and M/L tops. I ordered size Large. I liked how much stretch there was but it’s almost too much. I suspect these won’t hold their shape over time and jeans this stretchy tend to accentuate my belly vs slimming it. Cute from the front, not as much from the side. The fabric is a bit rough and scratchy on my skin. The belt is super cute. These just touched the floor with bare feet, but became too short with a sneaker on. Because of that I would only wear these with flip flops and that’s just not versatile enough to add this piece to my wardrobe. Will be returning. Bummer :(

I was really nervous about buying such a baggy style jean with my body type. I’m 5’0” and about 155lbs, with a curvy, athletic build. Usually, baggy clothes are not my friend, but I have always wanted to pull off the look so I bought these before a vacation! Wasn’t sure during my first try-on, but they became my fav jeans on my trip. I’m getting used to styling them and pairing them with different tops, but really happy with my purchase. They’re a gorgeous color, feel really high quality, and fit just how I wanted to — although I did size up. My one complaint is that they’re a little loose in the waist, but that’s on me because I could’ve purchased a smaller size and opted to go with the oversized fit. I also DO have to wear platform shoes in an effort to keep these off the ground and they still barely touch when I do. All in all, great jeans!!! Definitely a closet staple for me now.

I was really nervous about ordering these just because you know, Amazon is really hit or miss, and I saw these recommended in some article I saw on Facebook. But, I took a chance and am glad I did! For reference I am 5’9 and generally a size 4-6. I ordered a small and they fit very nicely. I would recommend ordering a size down so you don’t drown in these. They actually have a nice shape and are super comfy. Definitely recommend. Great pants for summer or lounging on the beach, around the house, or for a night out even. I just ordered two more pairs!

I was reluctant to purchase these jeans but I’m glad I did! They are super soft and fit perfectly. I’m glad I went with a size small, a medium would be too big. For reference, I’m 5’7, 150lbs. These hit perfectly on me. Not too long, not too short. I’m impressed with the amount of stretch. I wear between a 4 and 6 in Old Navy and that translates to a small in this brand.

I was scared of these pants not fitting me. I went based off the reviews and thank y’all for your help 🙂 I'm 5’3 and fit 8-10 pants. I ordered a size 10. I still have plenty of room! Feels stretchy and not too tight. I could size down to an 8 if I wanted to. I’ll be ordering more colors 😇

I was scared to order these. But I’m so glad I did! I’ve been trying to lose pounds and I was thinking a 12 might be too small. Fits great! I’m 5’6” 174 #. These are comfy and soft. You can sleep in them!!!

I was scared to order these. But I’m so glad I did! I’ve been trying to lose pounds and I was thinking a 12 might be too small. Fits great! I’m 5’6” 174 #. These are comfy and soft. You can sleep in them!!!

I was searching for the perfect long jean jacket to wear with rompers and oversized shirts. This is it and I can't believe how affordable it is. I'm 5'2" and 135 pounds. I'm a consistent medium in sizing and that's what I ordered. The medium was perfect and has an oversized look. If you are much taller than me, you may want to consider going up in a size (the larger sizes are longer in length), that may work for you. I read that some people wished they were a bit longer but for me.... the length was perfect for me and my height. I'm really happy.

I was shocked at how comfortable these are! I thought they were going to be too small and see through and they fit like a glove! Not see with nude underwear. The flare is straight from the 70s and I LOVE. 5’3” 150lbs

I was shocked that these fit so well. I am 5’0” and was fully anticipating having to get these hemmed by 4-6 inches with leaving room for heels, but they’re so perfect. The material does feel cheap but they’re so cute. In my photo I am wearing them fresh out of the bag!

I was skeptic buying it but I’m so glad I did!! I’m a curvey 5’3 and 180 lbs and the XL was perfect! The material is soooo nice, not cheap at all. Super soft and great material. It keeps me warm too. I can actually wear as casual and also dress it up with heels and jacket. Perfect! I’d recommend these pants to anyone!!! I will definitely buy more.

I was skeptical about buying these because My jeans are usually too short on me, but these are perfect length. I’m 5’7 180 pounds and got size 31. I definitely recommend going up a size Absolutely love these and can’t wait to wear them out on Friday

I was skeptical about buying these from other reviews, but they fit just like my other lucky jeans which say just "Ava legging" inside. Size 6/28. "28WX28L" I'm about 5'4 and 130-135 LBS. I added some pics they're not great Don't mind the little spots in the mirror just wanted others my size to be confident in ordering these I love them.

I was skeptical about less than 15 dollar pants, not gonna lie, but these are better than I hoped. Pardon the mess but I wanted to show the length and my room is a wreck. I’m 5’7 and 130 lbs and took a chance on size small, and they fit like a glove. Waistband feels secure but has some give, and length(which I was most worried about) is just right, resting on my ankle bone. There is a good bit of stretch too, so I can sit or lay however and they don’t feel stressed or pulled too tight. I do wish there was a little more slouch in the calves above the bands, but purely for aesthetic reasons. They’re also really, really soft. Thin but the kind of soft that’s inherently warm. I’ve ordered two more!
I was skeptical about ordering pants online because I'm tall and I have a booty so things fit me weird, but these turned out great! I am 5'11 and wear a 12 in pants normally and I got a L.

I was skeptical about the jeans. However, when I received them and tried them on. I was very satisfied.Soft fit, stretchy material, true to size and very comfortable. The only negative comment is the right knee rip will not stay in place (no big deal). I'm 5"9 and the length is perfect with low shoes.You will not be disappointed with this purchss.

I was skeptical about these at first. Then I tried them on, and I was pleasantly surprised! I'm ordering more colors. I love that they match so much of my Lilly perfectly! I'm 5'5 and 115 lbs and they fit pretty good. An XS would have been perfect but these will work fine with a belt.

I was skeptical about these because of my height and struggle with weight problems. But the second in got my first pair in and felt how soft, warm and cute they are I got the red ones. Main concern was of course if they’d be long to my ankles. Go past my ankles a little even with them pulled up to high waist but they fit wonderfully!!! I’m about 115-120 around 5’ 8-5’10, they fit perfectly and in it so small. If your debating on getting these I highly suggest you do. As soon as I put mine on I went to their page, had better deals so in ordered the green and the light camo. I plan on getting almost every color.

I was skeptical about these because of reviews but honestly not a bad fit! For reference I’m 5’1, 130 pounds. Bought a Medium. Plenty of room in the butt and crotch. I had to cuff the bottoms since they were a little long but nothing to complain about. Waist area is slightly snug but not enough to make me feel uncomfortable. Sherpa lining is SUPER warm! I could probably wear thin Cuddle Duds underneath as a base layer if I wanted. Definitely worth buying!!

I was skeptical at first because of the size complaint but went with my gut and went for it, im 5'7 ,180lbs and got the medium ..they fit perfect and are cute, the quality is good as well....im satisfied

I was skeptical at first since they didn’t have any reviews but I don’t like the maternity jeans that have that big belly band so I wanted to give these a try and I was not disappointed ! They’re super comfortable and seem good quality. I’m 16 weeks pregnant in the pictures and there’s still plenty of room to grow for the belly! I’m 5’8” and got a size medium which fits pretty loosely and is very comfortable.

I was skeptical because I was given the very last body shape available, BUT I ordered a XL as per Amazon recommendation, but these bad boys stretch and are FLATTERING! Almost looks like I have a butt! Great front pockets and cute little faux ones in the back. Placement of back pockets is flattering as well so yea, if you need cute dress pants (or slacks as my job calls them lol) grab a pair! I am 5'3 and they bunch a little at the bottom but it's really not a big deal. It's not amazons fault I stopped growing by 12 years old

I was skeptical buying pants for work online without trying them on and read tons of reviews first before buying. While I was looking for a lower rise pant and not one that goes to my belly button, these are amazing in every other way. They fit so nicely, keep the crease well in the leg, and look professional. They are great to wear more casually with a blouse tucked in but also would pair nicely with a suit jacket for more formal days when I don’t want to wear a skirt. I am definitely going to check out the other colors. I love the chunky waistband, it gives some personality without being over the top as I don’t ever wear belts. For reference I am 5’ 3” and they do drag the ground if I’m not in heels so I imagine with wearing flats most days the bottoms will get tattered if I don’t hem them up a bit. I am not sure how they will hold up after multiple washings but I am hopeful! They did not stretch throughout the day and start falling off like most other pants seem to do. Overall very happy and impressed for the price.

I was skeptical of these pants, since most in this style are too short on me. I generally look for a 33 inch inseam on my pants. These surprised me. They fit as expected. I’m 5’7 plus a little (I used to be 5’8” but I have scoliosis) and 205 lbs. I normally wear 14/16 and I ordered an XL. I don’t wear heels often (prefer flats) but the third picture is me on my tippy toes. They allow for heels if you bring them down slightly up top. I also appreciate the placement of the second seam up top. It doesn’t split the belly like some pants do. I already ordered a second pair and I might order more. If you’re a taller lady that gets discouraged over the inseams, give these pants a chance. :-) Shorter ladies will likely need to hem them.

I was skeptical on buying these but I’m so glad I did! Cheetah is so fun & They snug the right places and they aren’t too tight or too lose except waistband part could be a little more tight. (Not a bitch deal tho) And all the pattern options are amazing. I’ll be buying more :-) I’m 5’5 and about 120 lbs and I got a small.

I was skeptical to purchase these because I don’t know how jeans on Amazon fit but definitely it’s giving the mom jeans look kind of baggy, but snug at the waist. No stretch, real true denim/cotton jeans Once I take pictures with them, I can upload them here for reference. I am 152 pounds, a size 30 in waist, and got a medium.

I was so excited about these and was not disappointed! They are awesome and fit perfectly. They totally look like real jeans. I usually get large (5'7"/160lbs) in leggings and the L/XL is a great fit. I ordered the one that came with 2 pair. The blue ones are pretty short and the grayish ones go to my ankle and I actually had to cuff them to look right with my booties. Overall these are super comfy and I highly recommend trying them!

I was so excited to finally have some nice pants..... they did not send the size ordered. The price tag and the product tag were not the same size. Well.... my daughter can wear them atleast. I reviewed the fit based on her wearing them. Not too tight and not too loose on her. She is 5'9 and they drag on the ground a tiney bit in back. Fit her great and look nice.

I was so excited to find Democracy Jeans because I typically have trouble finding jeans that fit my waist comfortably. (I’m 5’7 and 140 pounds. I’d describe my body type as slim but with excess weight in the belly area.) I usually wear a 6, but these jeans were so stretchy that the 6 was too large everywhere so I exchanged for a 4. The 4 fits so nicely at the waist and really hides my stomach! Sitting down is comfortable and I don’t have a bright red waistband line across my stomach when I take these off! But the fit in the legs and butt is too large (definitely doesn’t give me the promised lift) and there are little folds of excess material everywhere. Also, the fly won’t stay up to save my life! Every time I stand up, the barn door is WIDE open. Overall, these jeans deliver comfort… and I love that they exist to accommodate more body types! But I don’t think mine is one of them :(

I was so excited to get these insulated jeans, just in time for cooler weather! The quality is nice, but the fit just doesn't work for me. (5' 10" 155 lbs. 30" inseam wearing a size L) Pros: Cozy, soft insulated jeans that look like any other pair of jeans. Color and stone washing add cute styling. They do fit fairly true to size, just not quite right for me. They feel like quality materials. Cons: They are too short! To be fair, most standard jeans are, but these feel extra short. They bunch funny around the knee. The fit at my waist, hips, bum looks like riding pants...too loose in the wrong places. I'm keeping the pants, I don't hate them, I will just wear high boots and long sweaters, sweatshirts or jackets to hide the akward parts and enjoy the warmth of these soft insulated jeans.

I was so excited to get these shorts! Read lots of reviews and loved the look of these. But...PROS: The initial fit was amazing! Put them on and they fit perfectly from the start. Love the 3 1/2 inch inseam, not too short, not too long. Love the functioning flap pockets in the back. I truly thought I'd found my perfect summer shorts and would be returning to purchase more colors. However...CONS: The spandex... Unreal! What did fit absolutely perfectly were pretty much falling off of me after about an hour of wear. Too much spandex? I don't know. They are 96% cotton and 4% spandex? But they no longer fit perfectly.I'm about 5' 6" and 135 pounds, I wear size 6 in bottoms for pretty much everything. I bought these in a size 7. These things were SO sloppy loose after wearing a very short time, I could hardly keep them up. I literally can put both arms down inside the sides of the shorts along my hips and I still have room. I can take them down to my ankles without unbuttoning them or even trying to get them over my hips. Such a disappointment. I don't know. The second pic is how they end up just sitting on my hips, sagging. Even though I've worn them, I'm thinking I'll return them... Ugh.If you're considering these I'd either reconsider or size down a size...

I was so excited when I received the Belle Poque Denim Skirt. It looked beautiful on the model in the photos. Unfortunately for me, that was not the case. I'm 5'5", and this skirt fell to just above my ankles (see photo). Granted, the model looks like she's wearing this above her naval - I found that if I hiked the skirt up just as high, that I was looking way off proportion. In addition, the sizing is off. I wear a size 12 and have a waist size of 34. I ordered per the sizing chart, which recommended an XL. Unfortunately, the XL skirt fits me pretty loosely - and on top of that, this skirt has a decent amount of stretch in it. I wanted to love this skirt, but to make it fit well - I'd have to take it to a tailor to have it shortened to my knee length. I deducted 1 star for the inaccurate sizing chart and somewhat misleading "knee length" in the title. Despite these issues, the fabric feels nice and soft. One note: after it's washed and dried - you may need to iron over the bottom hem as it likes to flip up.

I was so excited when I received the Belle Poque Denim Skirt. It looked beautiful on the model in the photos. Unfortunately for me, that was not the case. I'm 5'5", and this skirt fell to just above my ankles (see photo). Granted, the model looks like she's wearing this above her naval - I found that if I hiked the skirt up just as high, that I was looking way off proportion. In addition, the sizing is off. I wear a size 12 and have a waist size of 34. I ordered per the sizing chart, which recommended an XL. Unfortunately, the XL skirt fits me pretty loosely - and on top of that, this skirt has a decent amount of stretch in it. I wanted to love this skirt, but to make it fit well - I'd have to take it to a tailor to have it shortened to my knee length. I deducted 1 star for the inaccurate sizing chart and somewhat misleading "knee length" in the title. Despite these issues, the fabric feels nice and soft. One note: after it's washed and dried - you may need to iron over the bottom hem as it likes to flip up.

I was so happy to find a comfortable, basic pant that has stretch waist but don't look like PJs! These are lightweight material. The detail at the ankle is just enough to elevate them beyond lounge pants. POCKETS in the front. Highwaisted - yay! I'm a 10 typically and the LARGE was a comfortable looseness. I probably could have gone down to the medium, but wanted them a little more relaxed. I'm 5'5" with longer legs (my shortness is in my torso) - and they are full length for me. I'm glad since I prefer this over ankle crops. It says to hang dry, which I intend to do as I can see myself wearing this often and want them to last!

Size chart said to buy a small I was worried being that I carry extra weight in my belly but these stretch perfectly. I am 5’1 and 114lbs. I immediately bought another pair. Only thing annoying is these prices on all the shorts change drastically every day so you have to just buy them when they are cheaper.

I was so nervous about how these were going to fit because I struggle so much with clothes due to my body type. They're pretty much perfect. The material is a nice feeling, the overall size is great, and they seem like good quality. I am 5'2" and around 180 lbs and I ordered the XL. I might not recommend them if you're tall.

I was So Skeptical about this purchase because I thought the price was to good to be true for this particular style. Especially do to the fact that there was no review for this specific jacket....Well I’m happy I took the risk I Definitely Lucked Up on this one. I Ordered a Medium I am 5’2, 148lbs bra Size 34DDD.

I was so skeptical because of the reviews. But I purchased them anyways as a plan B outfit and bc of the weather I ended up wearing them. I am 5'2 195Lbs and I wore a 3' heel and the were perfect. They had a little stretch to them but they are definitely FUPA friendly.

I was so skeptical choosing from XS-XL sizes but these are amazing! I am 11 weeks with my second baby and I feel like I’m getting much bigger much faster(per usual) so these saved my life. I’m 5’2 138lbs and the S fits amazing. Length is just below the ankle! PERFECT

I was SO skeptical when I ordered these - overalls never fit because I'm too tall - 5'8". I was SO excited when i put these on and they are LONG! Like, touch the floor long! I have been waring them with sandals and they work out perfect. So many compliments on them. They are soft and super comfortable! Wearing a L

I was so worried that these pants wouldn’t fit. But they FIT! I was so happy. You do need a shoe with a heel and every now and then they slide down a little. Nevertheless they made my outfit! I am 5”5’ and a half!

I was sorely disappointed by these pants. When I first saw them online I was obsessed with the pattern. However when they came my excitement ended abruptly. They looked exactly like the picture EXECPT that that were 6 inches shorter than expected. I’m only 5’6 so I’m not sure where the extra length went. It’s almost like it needed a second waist band. Needless to say I send them back the next day.

I was super bummed because these shorts are very comfortable but much shorter than anticipated. I had tried looking all over for the inseam length but could not find it listed anywhere. If you do not have a large booty like me, you may not have this issue. I’m 5’5”, about 135 lbs and got a size small. It fit well with just enough room to spare and a good amount of stretch! I just not a fan of shorts that show my bum when I even slightly bend over, these days.

I was super excited about these pants but they just didn't fit. I'm 5'8 and about 150lbs and I had to suck in my stomach just to button them and even then I had issues zipping them up. The return methods aren't ideal being ups drop off or USPS returns so I just donated them. Overall I'm pretty upset since they were about 40ish bucks with shipping that ill never get back and tears shed over not fitting in the large which is my regular size.

I was super excited to get these, especially because of all the raving views. Not one single review mentioned anything about the pockets being messed up. One pocket is normal. The other one I can fit THREE fingers in, not even past my fingers. I love the length as they fit like regular pants on me (I’m only 5’). I sized up to an XL because I wanted them to fit lose. For the price, I was NOT expecting to have messed up pockets.

I was super nervous to get these, all the reviews said to size down but i’m the type to rather have them be a little big than too small. I vary between a medium and a large, typically go with a large to leave room for any shrinkage in the wash. I was also very concerned about the length as some reviews said they were too short. I am 5’7 and have an issue with pants being too short. these fit perfect length and size wise! so happy I went with a medium. I am 160lbs with a little bit of a bigger butt/thighs and a smaller stomach. even with getting a medium there is so much room for stretch and growth!! they are a little loud but honestly when I wear these to the bars no one will know. perfect length and perfect size. i’d say if you’re in between sizes, SIZE DOWN! there is so much stretch I feel like even if I got a small I could make them work (maybe a little uncomfortable) but doable. so happy!!

I was super unsure about these jeans when I tried them on so I put them away in my closet - but kept thinking about them. I actually think I kind of love them but felt awkward initially because I was so used to everything being so “skinny” for so long. I’m barely 5 ft tall and weigh 150 right now and I find these to be flattering. I took off one star because I do not love the wash of the jeans -you know, that kind of deliberate faded wash - but I can deal with it. My 19 yo daughter says I give off “thick art teacher vibes” in these jeans and you know what? I’m not mad at that.

I was surprised by the quality of these pants. The elastic waistband was good, the material was nice, it wasn't see through and it has some stretch to it. I just didn't have the right body shape to wear these. They were a good length also. I'm 5'7 for reference and they would be cute with high heels or flat shoes.

I was surprised by these jeans because normally Levi’s don’t fit me well. Ordered a 26 and my normal size is a 26. I’m 132 & just under 5’5”. They fit snug for a few minutes and loosened right up to a perfect fit.

I was surprised how much I liked these jeans. I am 5'8" 125lbs and I ordered size 6. I went off the size chart and got the six because of the waist measurement. These fit well, but I probably could have gotten the size 4 for a more fitted look and still had a comfortable fit. The material is soft and stretchy, and also not too thin. These are straight wide leg and high waist. They also have a line detail down the front which is elongating and flattering. The length was ok, it hit right at my ankles, but I was kind of hoping they would be longer and slouchier like in the web pic. So, if you are above 5'8 they might hit short unless you are going for the ankle or cropped look. They have actually useable pockets on the front and back, as well. Overall, a really comfortable and cute pair of jeans, but pay attention to the length and you might want to size down.

I was surprised that it fit nicely everywhere. Usually jeans that can go over my thighs are loose on my waist. It looked just like product photo but the jeans are thin to be stretchy. If you thick thighs like me, I know this will chafe and create holes in thigh area down the road. I wanted to try it for a 70s party and loved the big bell bottom pant legs. I am 5’7” and 150 lbs and ordered the M.

I was surprised these “jeggings” are actually real denim and have functional pockets. The only thing that doesn’t qualify them as jeans is a zipper, I suppose. I’m 115lbs, 5’7”, 27”Waist, 35”Hips, 18”Thighs, and got an XS. I have zero curves, but these make it look like I might actually have hips. For once, jeans that hug my skinny thighs instead of being baggy. Normally I would get a Small, but I’m happy I sized down. The length is perfect -hitting just above the floor. The waistband is really tight though, I almost couldn’t pull them up. But it’s made of the same denim the rest of the pants are, which I prefer over a separate stretcher material. No belt-loops though. Overall, very flattering and comfortable denim pants.

I was surprised to like these striped, cotton drawstring pants as much as i do. They are cute with the stripes and pockets (in the front and back). The description says they are a blend of cotton but the tag reads 100% cotton. They do feel stiffer than most cotton garments I’m used to. This doesn’t bother me for pants. They actually got a little stiffer after washing and machine drying. There didn’t seem to be a noticeable difference in any shrinkage. I am 5‘4“. 110 lbs and I got the small. The waist is just right on me. I wouldn’t want it any tighter. The length is good. They hit the top of my foot. I love the pink amd brown coloring. It matches all neutrals and whites. It’s a fun casual pant that flatters my shape.

But these pants were initially tight and then they stretched as I wore them through the day and it was damn near a perfect fit! They aren’t sheer!! I would recommend them and pretty good quality!

I was very surprised this fabric is comfortable, warm with the soft fleece inside and the color, it's a classic blue denim. Works well when we transition from fall to winter. Fit true to size, get your regular size. Fits me like a glove. Not super tight around my belly. Has a little room for comfort. Has some stretch so it's giving after you have had a big meal. However, I think I could also fit the XS for a tighter fit. But the Small is a good size when I feel bloated for example. For reference, I am 5'4, 117lbs. I like this a lot and want it in other colors. Great value. Thank you for carrying this. I hope my pictures and review was to you. Have a wonderful day!

I was weary at first with buying something online once again and they not fit. However I am so thrilled they fit perfect. I'm glad I didn't go up a size like the reviews recommended. My butt looks amazing in them per my husband..lol I love them and so happy that I finally bought something online that fits ☺️

I was worried about the sizes. I usually wear a 10/12 and a Large pants but I didn’t want a super baggy look. I ended up getting Medium Long and they fit perfectly. I’m 5’7 and 165lbs. These are so comfortable, I’m definitely ordering more colors. My biggest complaint is that it bags a little in the front at the hips and there is only 2 belt buckles. Also, you can slightly see underwear lines.

I was worried about these looking too casual to wear to work but I was totally wrong. These are high quality dress pant material! The model photo doesn't do them justice. SEE MY PIC! They are really dressy with a seam down the middle and up the back. They make your butt look good too! Nice fitted material in the waist line. Wide legs and long enough to cover the top of your shoes. I am 5'7" and length of wide leg pants are sometimes an issue for me. These are just long enough. I am very happy with this look!
I was worried i’d be too tall, but they fit like a glove! Super stretchy and cute. I’m 5”9

I was worried. But these came out being my new favorite pants! They are amazing! They stretch so they aren’t tight on your tummy, but have amazing hold to make your butt look good. I’m 5’8 so the length was good even with the booties I have on now.
I washed before the first wear and the deep color didn’t fade. This is a solid denim purchase. Stitching and seams are good. Flattering and comfortable. I ordered an 8 regular. I’m 5’6” and 158lbs

I wasn't expecting it fit quite as snug to the body as they do but am pleased just how flattering they are. I'm 5'10 and they fit great. Hug all the right places in a loose-ish form fit. They fall at a great ankle length for my height and look cute when rolled. Women on left and right are wearing the same overalls in different sizes. Left is a small. Right is a large
I wasn’t expecting these to fit as good as they did! I love them and will order more in different colors. I got so many compliments on them. I am 5’4 and 132lbs. I ordered a Med and they fit great.

I wasn't expecting top quality for the price, but these were so awful in so many ways that I don't think they're worth $1. The material feels very cheap and there isn't much stretch to the fabric. There's also these very odd puckers of extra fabric on the outside of the hips and on the inseam in the crotch area, which looks ridiculous. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and got the large size capris because I wanted a looser fit, but these were slightly snug in the waist and loose around the thighs, especially with that extra pucker of fabric. Also, these were not capri length on me. I have short legs and a long torso and, as you can see, they go down almost to my ankles. I'd have to roll them up many times to get the length I want, but the fabric isn't stretchy enough and is too snug around the lower legs to do this. Finally, there are back pockets, but the front pockets are fake. And be mindful of the tag that warns about bleeding dye even after washing. Given all of these faults, I wouldn't recommend these to anyone. There's really nothing positive to say about them. Don't waste your money.

I wasn't going to buy it because I have jean jackets already but I wanted a long one so I bought it!!! So happy I did, fits good, good length, and so versatile! I wore for my best friends birthday and wore it to a concert. Buy it if you love it. I bought a small I'm 5'2 and 130 lbs, its a little baggy but a super cute throwback style.

I wasn’t going to order these because I kept seeing that they ran really long but figured I could return them. They arrived today and I LOVE THEM! They fit me perfectly and are super soft almost satin feeling. Im 5’7” and weigh 140. Ordering more!

I wasn’t sure how fake leather pants from Amazon would go… But I actually love them! They fit perfectly, and are nice and stretchy. I only wish they were high waisted. For reference, I’m 5’4 and weigh 125, and I bought size 3.

I wasn’t sure how the quality or fit would be, as I feel like Amazon can be hit or miss. However, these were totally a winner for me. I got beige and it’s really a pretty color. The material seems really thin at first... like “can you see what color underwear I’m wearing” thin. However, they're not see through and don’t feel too fragile. I’m 5’0” and these were wayyyy too long for me. I cut probably 5-7 inches of length off the bottom and they are still just above the ground. They don’t have much stretch at all, but are still very comfortable. Even with those things considered, I love the fit and the style!!! I get so many compliment. They’re arguably my favorite pants. I just hope they hold up overtime.

I wasn't sure what to expect but they looked great in the pictures so I went for it. I was surprised when these arrived. They are actual jean material with lots of stretch so they are form fitting in all the right places. The V cut is very flattering. I could've had a bit more bell with the bell bottoms, but I'm pretty happy with them. I ordered a medium Short (30"inseam) based on the size chart and they fit me perfect. For reference I'm a petite pear shape with a short torso. 5'1" 131 lbs, 34" chest, 27.5" waist, 40" hips and typically wear a 4/6 dress.

I wasn't sure what to expect with this, but took a chance and I will actually be ordering a black one. It is so soft and I don't know how it stays put but it did, all day long. The band itself is a very comfy, very soft material. Reminds me of dry fit type material. It comes with two button extenders too. One short and one long. I am 5'3" and 193 lbs. I am 11 weeks pregnant and this one pair of jeans was a little too tight before my pregnancy and right now are not comfortably button-able. But other than that they still fit. This band allowed me to use the extender and cover the jeans so I didn't feel like anything was showing or falling down. The top tummy band is wide and I had no issues with it falling down at all. The bottom has an elastic under to keep the bottom of the band in place. I was concerned with the top falling down that at one point I flipped it, but it honestly works the best as it was intended! Trust the product! :D

I wear 14S in jeans, 5.2 " 180 LB,Amazon suggested Large but it was too snug in my fupa area, XL fits great and loose which is what I wanted with palazzo pants. A bit long but perfect in every other way. Not see thru because I sized up, I just wear higher shoes with them, so comfy!
I wear a 10-12 and got a large. I am about 5’4 and length was to the floor.

I wear a 10/12 and ordered a 12 and these fit perfectly! I am 5'3 and with no shoes they are on the ground but not crazy long. I need maybe 2-3" heels for them to sit off the ground. I love the checkered pattern on them! They have a great stretch to them and didn't get baggy when wearing them the first time. They are high waisted and not super thin material. Overall these are adorable jeans!

I wear a 2X if I want a top to fit against my skin (which do not). I wear a 3x if I want a little room. I'm a 38DD. This 3x is a bit tight around arms and chest for a 3x - it kinda clings to upper back and bra roll (ya, I said it)... but length, swing, belly and butt coverage are perfect. I do love it. Material is a light knit... and is not wrinkly nor clingy.

I wear a 2x/18w and I'm 5'7", this xxl fit perfectly. It hits about 2‐3" below the knee and is ideal. The fabric is quality denim and the way it was wrapped allowed me to shake out any wrinkles and wear immediately. The elastic waist band and the tie allows me to continue to lose weight and wear during this process. I look forward to wearing again. This time I paired with a tank top and a sweater with sandals; next wear I will go with a blouse and a scarf tied at the neck with some 2" heels. As with a favorite pair of jeans, this will prove to be versatile.
I wear a 6 petite and these fit right for me. Just know I usually wear petite and am 5’4” 135ish. Nice to have an alternative to white pants in the summer.

I wear a M and am 5’2”. These are not flattering. They’re also cut for a much longer leg than mine - I’d need to take 4” off the leg. It’s a cute pant at a decent price - but not meant for a petite woman.

I wear a size 16 and these are definitely a but tighter than my normal jeans in the waist area (got the xl since the reviews and sizing recommended that). My biggest issue is how short they are. The picture has them to the ankle while cuffed once, but they are about 4” above my ankle without being cuffed. I’m 5’9”. Trying to decide if I’ll keep or not. Maybe if they stretch more…
I wear a size 2/4 ordered the 4. Snug in the thighs and calves, which is fine. But have a gap at the waist in the back. (I’m 5’9” 143lbs)

I wear a size 31 waist jean size so I thought i was playing ot safe buying a large as the dementions for that size are for a 34"-40" waist size. It only fits on the last hole, so if I wore it with jeans my loops would be on top of the buckles. Really disappointed.

I wear a small in FP. This is a great dupe, super cute, and flowy. Love the material. I grabbed a small in black bc that’s all that was available at the time. Unfortunately the elastic is uncomfortable and digs into me. 5’7” 145lbs for reference. Waiting on my mediums to arrive this week hopefully.1st two photos are these pants size small on me. 2nd two photos are size small FP Quinn pants.

I wear an 18/20 and I originally ordered the 3XL and had to returned it for the 2XL. Big back and large bra size? This is the outfit for you! The top portion is wide, so if you are wide at the bottom and wide at the top it will fit. If you have smaller bra size then it will gape at the top. The stretch is just right not to stretchy and it won't lose shape after wearing it all day, but stretchy enough to be comfortable. For reference I am 240 pounds, 40 inch waist, 50 inch hips and 45 inch chest (bra size 38L). I typically wear a 18/20 and the 2XL fit me perfectly.

I wear S and am 5'7". These pants are super super wide in the thigh. I wish I loved them because the material and pleating is spot on but just too wide. Looks like clown pants. In other reviews the wide leg looked like it was lower. Use caution - it seems like not all pants are made the same way. If you have long legs they also come up too short. Just not the right fit :(

I wear size 6-8 in jeans. I first ordered these in size 8 and loved the fit and the faded whisker-look along the top. After walking around in them for a few minutes, I felt them sliding down a bit. We all know that jeans will continue to loosen throughout the day so I opted to exchange for size 6, which is what I typically wear in KUT from the Kloth corduroys. The fit was great though the whisker fade was much less prominent in the newly exchanged pair (dang it!) The photo I've included is of the size 8 jeans. I like the mid-rise fit. It feels secure and flattering around my mid section but still hip and stylish. I also like the ripped look but not so ripped that I feel like a teenager or homeless person. I have long legs (34 inseam) and these cropped in the perfect place on my calves. Love them!
I wear size M. I’m 5’1”. I got short 6 and it’s definitely true to size. So flowy and comfy. a win for the petite short girls! Yay!!

I wear these almost every day all winter. They're great for nordic skiing and great for under tunics at work. They run two sizes small--I usually wear a medium and have to wear an XL in these. (Reference: 5'6", 140lbs, athletic build) UPDATE: After 4 seasons skiing at least 75 times a year in these, they are starting to pill along the seams. That's incredibly durable, especially for the price. And my iphone SE fits fine into the front seam pocket, which keeps the battery from dying in the cold.
I wear these when I work (bar industry) and I get tons of compliments on them. I am wearing a medium and I’m 5’2 152 pounds and lots of booty. Get them!
I wear this almost everyday. It's super cute. Very comfortable and light. For reference, I'm 5'6 tall, 155 lbs, 36C in chest, 30" waist, 40" hips. It's flattering. I only wish it had pockets.

These are very comfortable and stylish, they have a ton of stretch, i would have even sized down one more because they are stretchy and i like my jeans a little tighter. I got them on sale and they are a good value. I like the pockets and the buttons are stylish. I will buy another color when they go on sale. I recommend.

I weight 190lbs and I’m about 5’7. I ordered an XL. These have great stretch I love the way they fit. The only reason I didnt give a full 5 stars was becuase I could never wear these out. They are very see through. I know they are white but Ive had a pair of solid white pants before that did not see through.

I weight about 173-175 pounds & I’m 5’4 most of my weight is in the booty and thighs, the XL was too big so I returned it and sized down to a L. In jeans I normally weight about a size 11! The large still stretched out a bit through the night and it doesn’t have belt loops so keep that in mind!

I went with my regular size, XL and it ended up being too big and definitely too long. I'm 5'7 and weigh 190. I don't mind rolling up the legs though and with a belt they work. I love the bright color and material!
I went with the gray, size small (5'3 100lbs) I was surprised how tight the legs were on me, usually small is big on me, I like the distressing and holes, no holes in back of pants.

I went with the size 3/32. I weigh 121 lbs and im 5'1 they fit great and they have some stretch to them. The length is perfect if I wear boots or cork heels. They do drag, but if I would've gotten shorter length they probably wouldn't look right on me. Hope this helps.

I went with the size chart & reviews of people with my height & weight....they just seem so long on me. They looked great on the reviews. I'm 5'3, 140 & I ordered a large. I had to flip my waist band down and they're still really long. I suppose oversized is what I wanted🤷

I WFH so I’m always trying to find comfortable but cute clothing, these are perfect. I bought 3 pairs :) I’m 5’6” 130lbs originally bought a medium they were really cute just slightly loose around the waste so I bought 2 more in a small

I will definitely be ordering more!! Listen I am 5’6 and can never find any work pants that fit my legs all the way down #thestruggleisreal but these.. oh baby! They are FLATTERING! COMFORTABLE AND PRETTY WARM !! Listen I dislike going to work and being uncomfortable and I think these are definitely gonna motivate me just a little more everyday to get my a$$ out of bed !

I will preface this by saying I am 5’4” & short-waisted, but these are not mid rise, but high waisted. While the leg is full & flowy (for denim), the waist is very fitted. I am between a 10-12 depending on fit, I always size up to be safe when ordering online. These fit but are tight through the waist. People slimmer than me won’t have this issue. If you are in between sizes, definitely size up. The denim is nice, but no stretch. I feel like a little spandex is put in a lot of pants these days, these are listed as 48% Cotton, 36% Rayon, 16% Polyester, so no give. The model has a cuff in the listing, but the material isn’t stiff enough to hold a cuff. If you want a cuff, extra steps will be necessary. As I am short, I was looking to cuff too. They are a smidge long on me, but not as much as I thought they would be, so they may be short in taller women. That might be a cute look, as long as you know what you’re getting. Love the patch pockets, but I do wish they didn’t have the buttons on the waist band, as I would like to wear with a belt sometimes. Overall, a cute look that is something a little different.

I will say first that these look nothing like leather in person, however, they are still cute. I'm 5'4 and a size 18 in womens and I got these in a XXL. They fit me just how I'd like. They go past my ankles and rise about 2-3 inches above my belly button. I haven't had any issues with rolling or anything like that. They're not super thick or super thin, there is no see-through of my derriere and above all they are pretty comfortable. Now with all that said I absolutely hate the pockets on these! They're shaped weird and they look even more weird when you have the leggings on. Whenever I wear these, I wear a top that covers the pockets. I would opt to get the version with no pockets. The other unfortunate thing about these is that I don't see these holding up for a long time which is crazy considering the price. I would say that when purchasing these don't expect them to be an everyday type of legging. Think of these as something to be pulled out semi-occassionally that can be dressed up for the impromptu date night or hanging with the girls when you want to be comfortable and cute.

I will start off by saying I have not naught a pair of jeans in over 5 years. I liked the thought of these being stretchy so I gave them a try. Instantly putting them on they bring out your curves and suck in the unwanted areas. I feel amazing in these pants. They have good stretch to them. I am 5’ 4” around 245 and ordered a xxl.

I wish I bought these months ago! I’m 5’7” 160 prego and I’m comfortable for the first time in months!! I was worried I’d have to stop working earlier than I wanted because of being so uncomfortable in my other jeans, that’s no longer the case. So happy and very relieved. I got a size L

I wish I had longer legs. Other than the length, these jeans fit me great. I ordered a Medium, which is my normal size (I’m 5’4” and 120 lbs). The length is about two inches too long. I noticed the inseam measurement in the sizing chart shows 31” for all sizes, which is kind of unusual, in my opinion. The cotton/spandex material feels a little stiff. They’re machine washable so hopefully they’ll soften after being tumbled. The 3-1/2” waistband offers some support. The pants have classic denim stitching at the seams, front and back pockets, and hem. They’re pretty comfy and ideal for casual days and nights out with friends. I’m going to find a seamstress who can adjust the length because I can’t wait to wear them with my favorite pair of boots.

I wish I would’ve dozed up. Very tight on my thighs. I’m 5’8” 180 lbs usually an 8-12 and I went with a 12 thinking that would give me enough room But my thighs are still very tight. Another thing is I specifically ordered full denim ones and I have two distressed knee cuts that were not like the picture, and not even centered in the knee area. They turn inward and don’t fit like normal knee holes distressed would. My advice would be size up 1-2 sizes and hope you don’t get the wrong kind. I’m sending mine back

I wish they had fit. I sized them based on another brand of jegging. Seems like they would have been very comfortable if I could have gotten them on, but at this rate I would need a large and they probably wouldn't fit right. 5'3" 140ish lbs and I weightlift. Size small.

I wish they had standard sizes because I love these pants so much. They're real high quality and not super thin jeans. You can tell they'll last a while. I'm a size 3, I'm 5"6' and weight 128lbs. If they had a size between 2 and 4 it would have been perfect. I'm not sure if 2 may have been better but I'd rather it be a tad looser than suffocating me. Even still I love them. The color is also exactly as advertised. I don't like many jeans they're either too high or too low. These are great.

I wish they had standard sizes because I love these pants so much. They're real high quality and not super thin jeans. You can tell they'll last a while. I'm a size 3, I'm 5"6' and weight 128lbs. I won’t give a bad review because the pants are quite comfortable. It’s a nice light weight material and the waist fit is good (ordered a large). I am 6 feet and wear a size 10-12........ however length is the issue. I took a chance, but I will be returning them because the pants end about 5-6 inches above the ankle. Booo. So if you’re 5’7-5’8 or shorter, I’m sure you will like these pants.... but us 6 footers.... keep looking.... these are capris on me. I only selected fit as expected in the review because of the waist fit. Length fit is yet another huge disappointment in the never ending quest to find pants long enough..... attention pants makers..... there are lots of tall women... we exist and we want pants.

I wore a 2X and I’m normally a size 18/20 in pants. These are light and airy but not see through. They are quick drying, flattering on the hips and belly, and perfect for vacation. I’m 5’5 and the length was perfect. If you’re tall… either wear them low rise or they probably won’t work. Great for the plus size ladies (The tan color was see through)

I wore a size 10 in Jules blue jean, and I love them! I’m 5’3 and between 145-160 lbs and typically wear medium, sometimes large. The high waist is very comfortable and doesn’t press in often, and the material is sturdy and thick and very comfortable.

I wore for a Christmas party with my boots and snowman scarf. 138lbs 5’6”. Size medium fit perfect. Length good with boots. The legs do catch and you have to break the threads but no sequins were lost. You learn to walk with legs slightly apart. Tons of compliments. Very comfortable and the waist doesn’t itch. I plan to wear to Dallas Cowboys game as well.

I wore them in Toronto. Super warm and stylish. I wore them with a sneaker. I could’ve gone down a size ( I ordered a L) however I’m tall ( 5’8 174lbs) so I didn’t want them to be short . I have added like 4 other colors to my cart I’m going to try at least one in a medium and go from there. These are totally elevated casual! I’ll be promoting these for awhile

I wore them to my husband’s work party and got lots of compliments. I’m 5’8 and a little under 170. I ordered a large and they fit perfectly. The length came to my ankles, not a long fit for my height.

I wore these all day and I gotta say they are COMFY! I ordered a 4 and I'm a 26-27" waist and they fit really well, I probably could have sized down for a tighter fit on the waist without issue. Obviously the cut of these pants allows for a lot of movement but the fabric stretches and bounces back with ease. I could squat and goblin walk in these and be totally comfy while doing it (we all know that's the true test). I wasn't sure about the aesthetic of these but I got multiple compliments from coworkers and they really grew on me. I did notice that the white threads started to fuzz up by the time I got home. It seems like they may have used cotton thread for these accents which would account for that. My only hesitation is that I wish they were an inch or two longer, but I'm 5'9 and they're definitely long enough. That's why I removed a star, but I think for *most* people the length will be absolutely perfect. These are honestly really great and the quality exceeds the price. Much better than similar pants I've gotten for more $ from popular retailers

I wore these during my time in Kenya and Tanzania (in March). They washed well during the trip, didn’t wrinkle easily, and were perfect for the cold early mornings and rainy days but also didn’t make me overheat on the hot days. I would 10/10 pack them again for another safari. True to size. I am a size 4, 130 lbs, and 5’7”. I ordered a small.

I wore these for a vacation in Florida and they were perfect. I wore them multiple days in a row at Universal Studios and they didn't lose their shape or get dirty. They wrinkle a little bit, but not terribly and they look like they're supposed to be that way. Very comfortable and lightweight. Temperatures were in the high 70s, low 80s and I felt great. Love the drawstring waist too! Great length at 5'6". Will be purchasing more colors!
I wore these pants for Thanksgiving I’m 168 pounds and I ordered a large they were a little big but they were fine

I wore these pearl jeans for brunch on my bachelorette and they are so perfect! Definitely going to style different and wear again! I’m 5’8” and wish they were a little longer so I could wear with heels! I am around 160 and a large fit well. It was a little loose in the groin again but otherwise it was perfect!
I wore these to a 70s party and they were super cute. I’m 5’7 135lbs and they were actually long enough too.

I wore these to a conference and ran into someone with what looked like the exact same pants except hers were Abercrombie and they looked exactly the same. The fabric and quality is top notch. I’m 5’2” 178 with wide hips and the XL short was a great fit.

I wore these to a town flea market and they were super comfy and light weight. My measurements for reference are 189#, 35" waist, 45" hip, thigh 27" and I'm 5ft3in. I bought an XL but I ordered another pair in large since the legs are wide. I have big butt and thighs and the xl fits well but I'm losing weight and feel like large will still fit good. They do come to my ankle but I like it so not gonna hem. Pockets are roomy also and waist is super stretchy and not hindering *Update* bought these in brown and after first wash the now look like there's bleach spots on them. I will be sending them back for another pair and try washing in cold water. I haven't dryed them bc I didn't want them to shrink.
I wore these to the Taylor Swift concert and they were so cute and comfy! Exactly like pictures and even long enough! I'm 6 ft.

I wore these to work the other day and got so many compliments! I was even stopped by a lady in the grocery store to compliment me! They're comfortable, the pockets are great, and versatile enough to wear casually for work, or dress up for a party. I think the front poufs out a little bit since I had a longer sweater, it wasn't noticeable. I want these in every color! For reference, I'm 5'8", 170lb, about a size 10. My mid waist is 33". I got size Large.

I wore these to work today and got so many compliments! They’re so comfortable and I felt confident in them. Only reason I gave 4 stars instead of 5 is the length. I am 5’4” and wearing 3” heels in the photo and the pants do not touch the ground. I like my pants to cover more of my shoe and touch the ground, everyone all day told me they’re fine though 😆

I wore these with a matte gold cami, a long black lace kimono, and medium heel pointed toe pumps. The satin is nice enough, didn't feel static, and I was comfortable in these to dance all evening. I'm 4'11", 108lbs and ordered Small. I have average to muscular thighs and these fit nice around laying flat in front. I did hem them. Oh, and pockets.
I wore this as part of my 70’s outfit. They are super comfortable but I’m 5’2 and I was wearing probably 3” wedges and they still touched the floor.
I wore this jumpsuit to a funeral in 109-degree weather - and it was very lightweight. I am 5'6 133lbs and ordered a size small. Great fit! Great material and very breathable.
I wore this jumpsuit to a funeral in 109-degree weather - and it was very lightweight. I am 5'6 133lbs and ordered a size small. Great fit! Great material and very breathable.
I wore this to a tea party and I received a lot of compliments on this suit. The breast part was a bit small on me. I ordered a Large, I wear a size 12 and am a 38DDD.

I wore this to a wedding rehearsal and got lots of compliments (see picture). I’m 5’6” and 120lbs and got a size small. I used a steamer to get the major wrinkles out then hung it up in the bathroom to let shower steam loosen the rest of the wrinkles out. I DO NOT RECOMMEND IRONING. Although the material is thin polyester, it was loose so panty lines weren’t an issue for me.

I wore this with a black dress to a wedding bc the theme was black and gold. It was the perfect addition to make my dress pop! It was very durable. I was skeptical bc a lot of reviews said cheap and broke easy but I did not encounter such. It’s metal and functional. Def not plastic. I have a 34” waist and it fit beautifully. You can hook it at any loop so it’s extremely adjustable to various sizes. It comes with extra link pieces in the event it breaks (they look like broken links so just heads up)
size_display: m: fit for waist up to 36 inches

user_comment: I work 12 hour shifts, a few days a week at a restaurant as a bartender/server. I needed new work pants that were durable, stain resistant, and comfortable. These were all of that and more. The restaurant I work at has a semi-strict dress code and these met their standards. I am 5’5” and 160 pounds, a medium fits perfect, just a little on the long side. I also want to add that I ordered a large first and it was very simple to return and order a new size.

I work as a para and and im constantly up and down off the floor with students. These have the durability, comfort, and stretch. I got a size large but would have enjoyed a medium more. I tie them at the bottom with the drawstrings because I don’t like how short they are on me and I’m 5’ 4”

I work in a really rough dirty plant and got tired of ruining jeans. These pants are comfortable but feel durable. And FINALLY no girl pockets! they fit as expected for me (5' 9" 210 lbs) I usually struggle to find pants that fit my waist and thighs, these pants do just that! fit great in all the right places! I will update if they wear out quickly but if there are no issues I plan to buy another pair soon!

I work in a saw mill and have lost over 60 pounds in the last 2 years and it's been so uncomfortable trying to find belts I don't have to fight with or belts I don't break within the first months time. With me being a female suspenders just don't work for me. I started using Bungie cords to try and keep my pants up while I lift logs all day long and after more weight lose they werent working for me either. My boss told me about adjustable belts and options outside normal belts you can buy at Walmart. So...I found these belts here on Amazon and ordered them. I wore one all week last week and love it. Depending on which pants I wear and how high up and low they fit on my waist will depend on if I have to wear the metal piece that comes along with the belts. Most times with me wearing my pants below my belly button I can hook these belts to the front belt loops and won't even have to use the piece of metal to hook the belt at the zipper line of my jeans. I love not having to fight a belt or constantly pulling up my pants or trying to constantly make adjustments just to work and keep my pants where they need to be. I would definitely buy these belts again. I'm a 29 inch waist and I tightened the belt as tight as possible and it keeps my pants up and keeps it comfortable. I don't have to worry about my pants dropping. 🤣 I would definitely buy these belts again.

I’m 5’8” with long legs and they still touch the floor, so for anyone shorter, these might definitely be too long but the bottom edge is raw so you could always just trim the length! I would definitely order a size down they are very stretchy. These are probably the most comfortable jeans I have ever worn. I will be ordering every color. I am 167 pounds 5’4 and the size 10 fits wonderful and even a little extra room in the waist. Would highly recommend! Color is as expected I’m very happy!

I would definitely size up on these. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and fit best in size small/x-small, size 2 jeans, with 24-26 inch waist - size 2 should have worked out but these are real snug. So, do size up. I should have ordered a side 4 in these. All the stitching and sewing is done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. The material is thin and stretchy. I do like the open hem. Cute jean shorts, just size up if you want a looser fit.

I would have give five stars if they would have been longer. I was so excited about the reviews that said they were too long. I am 5 ft 8 and 1/2 in tall. I'm 152 lb and it's hard for me to get jeans that are long enough. I usually wear a size 8. I ordered a six and the fit is perfect because they're so stretchy. They just come to the top of my foot. I like to wear them with boots so they can't have a heel. Still debating whether I'll keep them so wanted them to be longer! And I was disappointed that the bell bottom wasn't as pictured for the item.

I would have given these a 5 star, but the rise of the pant on the front is a little odd. The denim is good quality with a nice stretch and the fleece is soooooo soft! These are going to be awesome on cold days! Also, they are quite long, which is amazing for me as I am 6’2” tall! I’m sure this would be a negative for shorter gals, but these are great for us tall ladies!

I would have gotten black if I knew that the color would be SO off from what I saw online. I expected to see a nice, rich brown, but I got the coppery color of my eldest daughter’s hair, instead 😂. It isn’t what I expected, so I hope my review helps somebody out. This form of ‘brown’, goes with far few colors of tops in this shade, but they do have a couple pros to them. They are comfortable. Very soft to the touch, which is perfect for daily lounging & running errands. They do make me look a bit more hip-ey & run a bit wide on my frame. Ok, so it looks like my legs gained some weight, if that makes more sense. That does however, make my waist look more snatched (smaller). It’s a trade off, I reckon 😂. The price is fair for the quality. The color disappointed me & they fit a little biggish on my 5’6’’, 121 lb. Frame, but they’re wearable. Just not as much as I thought they would be. 3 stars ✨! ⭐️⭐️⭐️ ** I hope my review has been 😊! **

I would have kept this if it fit. I am more of an extra small and the waist line was too big for me so it wasn’t as high waisted as I’d hope. Unfortunately because it’s an elastic back and built in “belt” this does not have belt loops to tighten it. It did not work for me. The design of it is as pictured and is super cute. 5’2” ,113 pounds. The length was fine as long as I wore heels

I would have never bought these because of my body type until I saw an influencer style them. So many compliments, so comfy but can also be worn to work. Forgive the caution sign, lol. I am 5ft, 160lbs

I would say that these pants are comparable to the Time and Tru brand capris walmart has, but maybe a bit thinner than those. They're stretchy, comfortable and fit as expected. I'm 5'3 and with 200 pounds, xl fit great. Due to the material, cellulite can be seen a bit (but that has more to do with me than the pants lol). I got the olive color and I got compliments at work for them. The reviews stating that the inner thighs area can rip, well yeah, if you have thick thighs I'd say a year is really good life span considering the material isn't thick. We need to be realistic. Anyway, they're great! Price is still great for what you get. I'm short and the length is really nice.

I would say the feel of it is polyester. It is stretchy but very wide on the hips. I ordered a Large. I have 33” waist (postpartum), 43” hips. I think the band should be a bit stretchier. It’s best to use a spandex shorts to make your waist more snatched and not let the pants create the divide belly waist fat.

I would say these are true to size. The material is very lightweight. At 5’4” these are a little longer on me than they are on the model in the ad who is probably closer to 6 foot tall. Depending on your body shape, the fact that these are skinny pants will determine how they fit you. They were a little baggy in the upper thighs for me as skinny jeans, but they would’ve been more comfortable for me if they went higher in the back. It seemed like I was pulling them up in the back once in a while. Nice for knocking around the house or running to the store.

I wouldn’t call this boyfriend style. More of a skinny jean. But I have 18 inch calves so that might make a difference. I like the rip placements. Will likely cut off the hem. They are too long and I don’t like the rolled cuff. Definitely stretches and feels comfortable moving around in. The waist sits slightly above my belly button which holds everything in. I haven’t worn them all day so I’m not sure how well they stay up with the amount of stretch in them. I’m 5’5 188lbs with loose skin from weight loss surgery. Been fitting a 12 in most stores. 11 fit perfectly.
I wouldn’t say these were baggy on me- but a cute Jean regardless. Runs true to size- I wish they were a little longer though. (But I am 6’0 for reference )

I. Love. These. However, I ordered the light wash and was sent the little bit darker wash. I’m not really complaining because these things make my butt look real nice 😉 But I would like to order the light pair again to see if I get them because they’re CUTE. I'd been looking for wide-legged pants for a while, so I was excited when I found these. They are just long enough that you won't trip over them and while the waist looks extremely small, it's perfect. I couldn't give it 5 stars because the waistband is made of very thin elastic so it kept rolling down all day. A wider waistband would be awesone. Also I didn't care too much for the fabric but other than those two factors the pants are great! I'll probably purchase another pair because they have the look I love and the fit was perfect! I am 5'4" and I wear a 14/16 or Large in work pants from department stores.

I’d been on the hunt for a pair of green cargo pants that were short girl friendly and didn’t cost a lot. These fit great and are flattering. I’m 5”2 and you can cinch them up a bit at the bottom and I wear a 12 and got a large.

I'd give it 4 stars since it is indeed warmer than another fleece lined tights I tried. I ordered Small because other reviews state that the extra small is too tight in the waist. However, the small doesn't fit me perfectly (5'2" about 105LBs, 33Hips). A bit loose around legs thighs, knees, and ankles. The length is perfect though. so it's petite friendly. I would have still kept it but there's a hole in the back of the waistband. Brand new and already has a hole?! Can't say that gives me confidence in its quality. Also, the pants really attract lint. Keep that in mind if you have pets. Oh, that fashion logo is such an eye sore. Whose idea was this?!!

I’d say it really N.A. a little the big, although everything’s a little big when you’re only 5’0”! But loose, nonetheless. I like it. I’ll be adding photos of these on me soon. For now, I want to say I absolutely do love them, and want to wear them all day, every day. They have nice, deep pockets and are a nice weight Jean. True jeans, not some weird fabric. My only complaint/regret: But, I used the sizing chart, and knowing how wonky sizing can be on Amazon, I ordered 100% according to their size chart since I KNOW my measurements. They are HUGE. 😅 I’m swimming in them but they’re so comfortable that I still recommend them, just size down accordingly unless you’re really looking for that “painter/carpenter in overalls” look (nothing wrong with that!). I wish I had gotten a small or medium, and I got a large. My weight is 130 and I’m 5’2”. Also have to cuff the bottoms and have the buckles pretty high up and tight. I would love to be able to try the right size! The color truly reminded me 100% of painters overalls (brick), they are a burnt orange. If they had fit, the color would’ve been better. However, i decided to overdye them using RIT dye in Wine for about an hour. They took the dye beautifully and turned out a very deep red. I’m going to continue to dye them to keep them from getting too faded! UPDATED REVIEW: After having them for a little while, I wanted to add some notes. They are soo comfortable and I find myself wanting to wear them all the time. However, the pockets have Never laid quite right and this can really affect the way they look on. The front pocket bunches and leaves big gaps between my body and the overalls. The side pockets don’t want to fit correctly in their slots because due to the buttons pulling on them on each side, and the buttons aren’t even functional. I wash these by hand and hang them to dry, and even after steaming them, they still don’t look the way I want them to or as they are in the model photos. Maybe if the size chart had been more accurate I would’ve gotten the correct size and perhaps these issues would resolve themselves.

I'll start of with the fact that I'm 5'4" with a 29" inseam and about 185 lbs. I carry my weight in my belly and thighs. I purchased these to wear while walking my dogs in the winter. I bought the L\XL size. They're a bit long on me, but that's fine. The fit is great. Not too loose, not too tight. No muffin top. In the photos, I pulled one leg down to the middle of my foot so you get an idea of the length. They are high waisted, roughly 3" above my belly button (I'm touching my belly button in the one photo). The waist is a wide one and I haven't had any problems with it rolling down at all. The material is a little shiny but more knit than one of those slick athletic fabrics. It's a thin fleece too. I think I was expecting more of an athletic legging with thicker fleece. I just walked the dogs. It's 42F outside with a 10mph wind. My legs stayed warm. Hoping these hold up in colder temps. If not, I'll just throw on some silky long johns underneath too. Product was received on time as ordered.

I’ll start off by saying I’m 5’3” 142 lbs and 25 weeks pregnant. I got these in a size medium 8/10 I believe. They are EXTREMELY comfy and flattering. They are also just the right length not too short not too baggy (I have longer legs smaller torso). They are very stretchy and I could have definitely gone down a size, but I’m not sure if that would effect the length. Overall I’m very satisfied and highly recommend these pants to get you through your pregnancy and postpartum. Like I said LOTS of stretch especially in the waste, so I’m confident that these will carry me through the rest of this baby making process.

I'll start with I'm 5'4 170 with curves. I first purchased size 32 as recommended by Amazon and the sizing chart. They were large enough I could pull them off without unbuttoning them. Sized down to the 31 which is the size in the pics. The fabric and the top part of the jeans is the only reason these jeans get 2 stars. The waist sits nice and when I sit it doesn't do the stupid gap in the back. I think the cut and sizing all works well until you get to the calves and the inseam. These are long. So much that I had on 4in heels and they still were on the floor if I dared wear them with the heels. I was nervous my heels would get caught in all the excess fabric of the calf area. I get straight leg but at 9in wide (when the thighs are 10 there's some taper) I felt like I was in the oh so popular JNCO jeans. So I tried with tennis shoes. Again my shoe could get lost in the leg causing a trip hazard. So I arolled them up twice which only caused more bulk. If you're not statuesque or planning on cutting these into shorts I wouldn't recommend. But if you're going for that emo hot topic 90's baggy look go for these, though Levi's does have a 94 throwback that may be better for that.

I'll start with the positives and those are that these pajama pants run true to size and I ordered my normal size of large. They are also quite high waisted and long. I'm 5'8 and these are nearly too long. However, the quality is pretty bad. If I saw these in a store, I would not buy them. They are thin/transparent and within 1 wear the stitching in the crotch area came undone and created a hole. I would expect a lot better quality for $17 so these are a miss.

I'll write a review because I know it can be for other ladies on a quest for good jeans. I've probably ordered 10+ pairs on Amazon and these are the winners. My measurements: 28" waist. 37" hip. 30" inseam. About 5'3". 133lbs. Athletic build. 30 yr old mom of a toddler. These jeans stay up without a belt. They are mid rise just below my belly button. I gave up on low rise cut jeans awhile ago because I hate having to yank them up every hour. The Levis website says these are relaxed through the hip and thigh. But if you are an athletic build and occasionally do outdoor chores in these pants, I found that they fit great and still flatter. I dislike skinny jeans because I prefer to wear my pants over my winter/fall boots without looking awkward. These bootcut jeans are a rare find in the skinny jean saturated market. Material is medium weight cotton blend, not paper thin. Decent cold weather jeans. I may wear leggings underneath if the temps drop lower in NH. Hope this helps another lady who needs "do everything" jeans.

I'm 120 lbs and 5'7" and typically wear a slim size 8 for a small waist - these jeans fit me well. The leg length is good as I prefer a pant that covers my ankles - these do. The fabric is a medium weight with a lot of give. These jeans seem to recover their shape ok without getting too stretched-out and droopy. Looking at the inside, the sewing is good but not what I would want for tough work. The pockets are sewn to stretch along with the outer fabric, as they should be. The zipper works ok, not real smooth initially but will likely ease-up after some wear. The button/rivet at the top of the zipper is fine but the buttonhole is a bit too tight. These are decent light-duty to mid-duty denim pants that look nice and are comfortable to wear.
I'm 120lb, 5'0, and ordered a size 6. They fit pretty well and I like the raw hem, but I don't think they're super flattering. They're very comfortable though!

I’m 5’1 and these hit right at my ankle!

I'm 5'2, 120lbs, 34-26-36. I usually fit a 7 jeans because of my ample bottom. Upon reading the reviews I decided to order the small(4) because they stated the shorts run a little bit bigger.
