I can get them on and buttoned but being mid waist it's a little snug on my tummy. I would advise a small though if you have similar measurements but don't have the ample booty.

I’m 5’2, 124 Lbs these pants were a tad bit too long, but it never has bothered me. Ordered size small and really happy with the extra features making them unique. Besides the top elastic waist, the pleats with pockets elevates them to a casual or semi fancy date night attire. So flexible, they could be word year round in any season.

I'm 5'2, so they're pretty long, but still wearable! I love the elastic backing on the waist band, since I'm thicc in the hips and the booty. There isn't the awkward gapping in the back that you usually find in pants. Definitely one of my new favorites. They're so comfortable AND cute.

I’m 5’2.5” and currently 148 lbs. i carry most of my weight in my belly. I have no torso and long legs. I ordered sizes 10 and 12. The 10 was way too snug for my belly. The 12 fit okay. Thank goodness theirs is a lot of stretch on the waist. I wouldn’t size up bc the butt would be too roomy on me.They are a bit long. I wish these were offered in a “short” or “ankle” option. I wouldn’t be able to wear them with flip flops but with my sneakers they’re alright. The last two pics I have my sneakers on and you can see that the jeans are still a little long but not terrible.

I'm 5'2" (157.28cm) and 158~ lbs (71.5kg) Ordered the black pair in the size 8, tag on jean says 8/29. Purchased these to wear to work, 10hr shift in fab & assembly. First Impressions: Stretch is good, thicker fabric kinda scratchy Legs actually look longer with the bootcut, does the curves justice. Solid shade of black. No back gap, might need belt. Fit : 4/5 Fit my legs and especially my thighs and butt (the biggest part of my body) comfortably but is a little looser one my waist/stomach. I could go down a full 2 inches on the waistline but nothing a belt won't help with. The waistline sits right at my bellybutton (35" (89cm) to the floor) and doesn't ride too far down when I bend or squat, I have full coverage. I don't know if going down a size would be the right move for me just because I want them to be comfortable while I'm working and if they're too snug will hamper my range of movement. Length : 4/5 They are a little long, would go with a petite size if possible, but they will be worn with boots so it's not an issue. If you don't mind the hems dragging a bit or some hemming work like me then you'll be fine. Work pants are ment to get dirty. Fabric : 3/5 Seems like a sturdy denim. A bit scratchy but that'll be fixed with an initial wash. They do have a little stretch so if you are in between sizes then you're safe to go on the lower end. Seams are solid and the fabric is thicker than regular fashion jeans. Haven't put it through a workday yet but they seem like they'll hold up. Pockets : 2/5 Better than most jeans but could be bigger. When it comes to pockets size matters. At 16cm deep, just barely over 6", there's room for improvement where there isn't for my phone, keys, wallet, chapstick, and work fob. Can't carry a purse around the warehouse, ya feel? Price : 1.5/5 I personally don't like spending more than $20-$25 on a pair of pants but I need something sturdy and that fits over my boots. I bought these on a 20% off sale for $40 instead of $50 which is the only reason I considered these. Honestly just feels like I'm paying for the brand and not the pants at this point. I did order a pair of Wranglers that were on sale for $30 (29% off $42) that'll be here in a day or two. Depending on how well that pair fits I may return the Dickies and get another pair of W's (or 2, for that price). All that being said, I have not actually worn these to work so if I decide to keep them and they hold up for a long while then maybe they're worth it. For a $50 pair of jeans they at least better last me 3-5 years.

I’m 5’2” 140 pounds. The SMALL fits exactly like I wanted. Very stretchy waistband, deep pockets. The fabric is cool and light. Will probably be order more in different colors.

I'm 5'2" 150 lbs with a big belly. Mine fit fine. With legs unrolled they come to my ankles or roll them up to make them capri's (photo shows one of each). They're accurately shown and described. Butt shot is showing you that my underwear, which are skin tone, do not show through, but if I was wearing white under them I'm sure it would. I'm happy, nice boho/gypsy linen summer pants.

I'm 5'2" 35" hips 25" waist, petite At first I didn't expect much, but it's surprisingly flattering, the cut on everything fits super well and it does my butt justice! The inner pocket is too small to safely hold my iPhone 6s Plus and is about the size of a standard sticky post-it notepad (3 x 3 inches-ish, see my attached photo). I'm 5'2" and 151lbs. I got these in 30x30 short and they fit well. The material has decent stretch as well. They're a tad long at the heel but with the right shoes it shouldn't be an issue. Will be ordering another pair

I'm 5'2" and I wore them with heels and they were prefect. The material is very thin so if you're wearing any type of body shaper underneath make sure it's seamless or you'll be able to tell.

I’m 5’2” and these are cropped. Not at all the length of the picture. Also they run much smaller in the waist.

I'm 5'2" and these fall pretty perfect with shoes. They are super stretchy (no tummy control) and I love the dark wash. Definitely not real jean material, but they are very comfortable. Also they have 4 pockets and a foe button closure.

I’m 5’2” and these pants are a bit long for me but I love them! They fit great, good quality and I love the flowy, relaxed look. Didn’t shrink when I washed them, they do wrinkle easily but that can be fixed with an iron or steamer. I ordered these in XS

I'm 5'2" and weigh 138 lbs. My waist is 34" and my hips and thighs are very wide (45" and the widest point). I purchased a size large. I like the way they fit nicely on my waist (not too tight without the weird gap in the back), but they are very tight on my hips, which makes me worry that they will ware or tear in no time. The length is a tad long on my short stature, but manageable. Another reviewer mentioned that these are not meant for pear-shaped ladies, I would agree. I purchased these to try something other than skinny jeans for once. After testing about a half a dozen pairs of jeans, these were the most flattering. I plan to keep these and see how things go.
I'm 5'2" size 8, med fit perfectly just long. Wore with some wedges and they were perf. So comfy... Totally gonna buy some more.

I'm 5'2", 112 lb. I ordered the small, which were a bit loose but ok. The wide waistband lays flat, so no muffin top. It was just way too long for me because I don't intend to wear heels more than an inch or two with these pants (see photo). I hemmed it up 2.5 inches to go with low sandals. Because it's loose, I can push the waistband down a bit on the hip if I decide to wear higher heels. Pairs nicely with a flowy crop top shirt.

I’m 5’2”, 120# and pear shaped…. I bought these in a size 6–I think I could have gone with a 4 because of the stretch as I have some gapping in the back. They’re super comfy, more of a medium weight but will be good for spring/summer if it’s not too hot. I would rate them 4.5 if I could—I only docked a little bit because they stretch out with wear. The length hits me at the ankles—I wish they were about an inch shorter but not a deal breaker for me. I was going to buy another pair in a 4 to compare but they’re sold out.
I’m 5’2”, 130lbs, and bought the medium. They fit great. Minimal see through when bending over. Don’t bend, squat down to pick items up. Cozy and warm. Feels like a blanket hugging my legs.

I'm 5'2", 140 pounds. I ordered my usual size, Medium. They fit fine around the waist because of the stretchy elastic, but they are not capris on me. The legs come all the way down to the top of my foot. The color is definitely not maroon. It's terra cotta. They come out of the package with a faded, pre-washed look. Some people will like that, but it's not my cup of tea. To me they just look old and over-washed. They're also way too wide in the hips and legs. I understand that they're meant to be "wide-legged," and the width might be ok if you're over 5'8" and they're really capris on you. But on me there's way too much fabric. They end up looking baggy and ill-fitting, make me look dumpy. I'm going to have to take about 2" off the outside of the legs and maybe 6-8" off the hem. I washed and line-dried them before trying on, and they came out very wrinkled, which is a bummer because I'm not about to haul out the ironing board. Next time I'll try the dryer on low and see if that works better. On the plus side, they do appear to be well made and are a nice, light weight for summer wear. Final note: I am extremely sensitive to tags in clothes. I always have to remove them. This pair of pants, like so many others, has the tags sewn directly into the seam, so when I removed them it left a hole behind and a sewing chore for me to do. Annoying to have to mend a brand new garment!
I’m 5’2/3 and I weigh 114 and I love how it fits, I got it ina small and it’s not tight on the waist🗿💗

I’m 5’3, 120 lbs, normal build, ordered size small. Okay, so... my first two pictures should explain why these get only 3 stars. My husband and I both busted out laughing when I pulled these out of the package. They could easily be mistaken for a child’s pants by their size, until you turn them around and they look like a deflated pin cushion. But then I put them on, and was quite impressed actually at the material - I personally love this material for leggings, it’s the only kind I’ll buy. So kudos are due there. I immediately realized I should have ordered a size up to be safe (although the description/reviews say true to size). Granted they’re a size to small, I like how they fit (assuming they’ll be more impressive when I order a medium). The butt shaper detail is “okay” at best, it definitely gives some definition but the way the butt seam is sewn in makes it shiny so it looks like you’ve got a string up your crack. I’m not sure if that part will go away when I size up, but it’s not so bad that I’m not willing to risk it. I just recommend doing a “bend over” test before leaving the house in sunlight. Overall they’re alright, crossing my fingers a medium makes them 5 star. Key takeaway: SIZE UP!

I'm 5'3" tall, weight 150 pounds. This is a size medium. Plusses: They have the tailoring of the 1940s, with snug fit over hips and waist and flaring to the legs. The elastic on the waistband is wide and quite stretchable to get over hips to put on. Pockets are well made and have the placket to carry tons of stuff. The buttons are 5/8" across giving a nice decorative look. The pants are long for taller people, yet you can tie the legs to lift the hem above your shoes. Minuses: Wish the pockets were velcro closed as the buttons are hard to get through the buttonholes. And when unbuttoned, you can't see them at all. Strings on pant legs are long and could trip up the wearer unless double tied. Nice pair of cargo pants and will be my go-to for casual wear.
I'm 5'3" weigh 140 lb. and I ordered the large. They are very comfortable and easy to move and fit my build quite nicely.

I'm 5'3", 114 lbs and ordered the small. They fit as expected: since I'm short, they hit right at the ankle rather than a crop length. My sideview picture shows them riding a little lower on my hips, which still works. They're pretty comfortable and easy to throw on for lounging or taking walks. The pockets are a little too low to be practical, but they look cute. I ordered the dark blue, which in the product photos looks almost like denim, but it's not. In reality, it's a solid dark blue (more like the fabric closeup product photo). Oh and it came in a can, btw. Ha.

I’m 5’3”, 117 lbs. measuring 34x26x36. I bought size 7. I normally wear a size 6 or 7. These jeans are not tight! They are still very cute and I am happy with them, but if you want the tight butt lift and you are in between sizes go with the smaller size as there is a lot of stretch to this material.

I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

I’m 5’3”, 130lbs, and I ordered size small. They have great length, very comfortable, and breathable. The only thing I don’t like is the extremely long inseam. Other than that, I might would buy additional colors. The waist is very comfortable, but it’s not compression so it won’t hide the mom belly.

I'm 5'3", 134 lbs, with a 29.5" waist and 40.5" hip measurement - the size 9 fits just right. These shorts are super comfy and flattering. They do stretch out a bit as you wear them but I didn't find it to be bothersome and they stayed very comfortable all day. Galaxy grey is a little more taupey in person (more brown toned) than I expected based on the photos but I'm still happy with the purchase. I'll be buying more in the future!

I'm 5'3", 134 lbs, with a 29.5" waist and 40.5" hip measurement. The size 6, 32 inch inseam, feels just right. This is my first time trying Levi's (I got a few different types) and I'm really pleased with the fit on all of them. These fit almost the exact same as the 315 shaping bootcut and they were half the price. If I had to pick between the two, I would probably go with the 315 but the price is right on these and I have no complaints. I see some people saying they are too long but I actually really like the length. I love the color on this pair (Blue Laguna-waterless). One thing I will note is that these came without any tags on them so I suspect that they were returned previously. They don't have signs of use besides that though so I'm comfortable keeping them, especially since they are well priced and fit very well.

I’m 5’3”, 135lbs., normally wear an 27/28 or 6/7. I carry my weight in my bum/thigh/lower belly. I ordered a size small. Wasn’t expecting to like these as much as I do! They run a little on the big side, but in a good way. They are a loose fit and have a nice stretch, but they aren’t too loose and baggy. I would say order the size you normally wear; no need to size up. They’re totally comfy due to the stretch. They are def high-waisted. I don’t always look that flattering in high-waisted pants, but I feel good in these! My legs are short, so these look more like high waters on me rather than a capris, but I like the look with boots. But if you have short legs and want them shorter, keep in mind you may need to hem them. Planning to hang dry since they are quite stretchy and I don’t want to damage the elastic. They aren’t the best/thickest quality, which means they likely won’t last too long, but I bet they’ll be comfy when the weather starts to warm up. I was quite happy that I ordered these. We will see how they hold up!

user_comment: I'm 5'4" and currently 155 lbs., and the Medium size fits me almost perfectly (a tiny bit snug at the waist, but I'm losing weight so that won't be an issue for long). The elastic waist pulls in enough that I probably could also have worn a Large for a more relaxed fit through the seat and leg. The length is ideal for me, hitting just at the heel with a break at the ankle. That means if you're above average height, these pants may be a bit short for you. There's not much hem to let out. I usually have low expectations for knit and/or stretch pants, but this fabric is thicker than most. No panty lines visible, and the fabric has a pretty low stretch ratio -- it has enough give that you can move comfortably, but it's not clingy. The ribbing is visible, but neutral enough that from a few feet away the pants just look like dress slacks unless you're in super bright light (my photos were taken in direct sun to show detail, but in average room lighting they look darker). Obviously this applies to the black pants I'm reviewing; if you select a different color, texture visibility may vary. The pockets are large enough to fit my entire hand, but because they're set at an angle, I'm not sure I'd trust them with a large device like a smartphone. The only thing about these pants that I don't care for is the drawstring, which is 1) enormous and can't be tucked out of sight inside the waistband because it's so bulky, and 2) superfluous, because the waist is so heavily elasticized. I ended up pulling the drawstring out of mine (one end is untied, so it's easy) so the pants look less like sweatpants. I usually wear shirts untucked, so the holes in the waistband aren't likely to show. Overall, I'm pleased with the quality, and will put these into rotation with my fall casualwear!

I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs and my measurements are 44(38/40C)bust, 34/35 waist & 44 hips. Per the size guide provided I ordered the XL. After trying, I could’ve gone down a size, but I really like the loose fit of these - they fit how they appear on the model, similar to a boyfriend fit. In my image I’m wearing 2.5” clogs. I love the look. These are super comfortable and the elastic waistband is wide and not constricting at all. The legs are wide but not exaggerated. After washing, these did shrink a bit, but mostly in length, but they still cover the tops of my feet. Fortunately, these pants have a generous hem, approx 3.” So if I ever wanted to wear these with even higher platforms, I could easily let the hem down. These are perfect weight for Fl. I can easily wear these in warm weather or with a sweater when it becomes cooler. I’m super happy with these and I definitely recommend. I also like that the brand, GRAPENT, has an Amazon store. Once I find a brand that suits me, I love to see what else they offer.

I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs, my waist measures 36” & my hips are 44.” Per the size guide provided, a 14 would be ideal, but the largest size available is 12. I hoped with a fiber content of 75% cotton & 25% polyester there’d be some stretch - so I ordered the sz 12. Well, these fit, barely (waist-wise). There is zero stretch to these jeans. Although I love the look of these, the waist is definitely snug. Fortunately I sew well, so I have a few tricks up my sleeve. (Or I could lose those last pesky 10lbs). I received these through Amazon Vine & returns/exchanges or not an option. However, even If those options were available to me, I’m not sure I’d return these - the hips & everywhere else (like my bum) fit super nice. I really like the thick cuffs which are super on trend right now, although I’ll most likely sew them securely all around. The cuffs are currently only stitched in place in a few spots (pls see image). These do run long - I’m actually wearing 2.5” wedges in my image. Those deep cuffs could come in handy for anyone who wanted these shortened (or lengthened). I love the style of the front pockets & especially the placement of the back ones. The overall quality of these are excellent. I feel the price of these are fair, especially when compared to one of my fav brands for jeans, Free People. There’s a pair of jeans very similar to these by FP I’ve had my eye on, but they’re $128. I believe with a few tweaks I’ll be very happy with these. I do recommend these jeans but highly suggest following the size guide provided.

I’m 5’5,” 165lbs but carry excess weight in my tummy. My waist is currently 36/37” and per the brand’s size guide provided I went with the sz 18. I’ve never been a sz 18 - I’m typically sz 14 or 32/33 waist in Free People jeans, but I love these jeans and wanted them to work. Turns out they fit! But due to how incredibly stretchy these are I definitely could’ve sized down to a 16. I still love the way these look and they’re a great dupe for the FP barrel jeans I’ve purchased in the past (and a lot less expensive). They did shrink some in the washer/dryer, but after a few hours they tend to stretch out again. I received these through Amazon Vine, but I love them so much I’m purchasing on my own a pair in a smaller size. Plus, at the time of writing this review (8/23/24) they’re on sale. Will update my review when I receive the smaller size. I do recommend these jeans, but keep in mind the stretch factor. The quality is excellent and I especially love how lightweight these are. It’s hot and humid here in South Florida, but these are jeans I could sport year round.

When I want to sex it up a bit, I lose the shell underneath and wear just the jacket and compliment with a pair of heels and a small purse. I’ve ordered all the colors in this style!

I’m 5’5” 140lbs. I got beige in medium. Nice color and will be lovely for vacation my only ick is that the band/top part is not suitable for sight so I will be having to wear my shirt to hide it rather than a bodysuit type of look. But I can deal with it these are super comfy. Note: they will wrinkle plan to bring a steamer on vacay or use the in room iron. I also will NOT be drying this as it’ll likely shrink.

I’m 5’5” 147 lbs 38D and got these denim overalls in a small. The fit is spot on (I prefer something a bit more fitted), the length is just right for someone my height. The fabric is about medium weight, a bit thinner than Levi’s overalls. But I actually prefer something a bit thinner like these overalls because it’s more comfortable. Large side pockets, fits my iPhone 13 Pro Max. Looks great with a t-shirt or cami. I have to take 1 button off in order to take these on/off because my hips are pretty large. The fabric is easy care, I just wash it like my denim jeans. I prefer to air dry but do like to put it in the dryer for a few minutes after it’s fully dried to soften it up. At the current listed price of $40, I think it’s a pretty reasonable price. Good quality.

I’m 5’5” 150 lbs 38D and got a size small because I know most Levi’s run a bit large. My normal size is a medium. The small fits great and comfortable to wear all day. Easy to take off if I need to use the restroom. Love the large pockets, fits my iPhone 13 Pro Max. The material is nice and heavy (not too heavy though) like normal Levi’s jeans. Typical Levi’s quality that won’t disappointment. Just wash like normal jeans. I prefer to let it air dry but do like to throw it in the dryer for a few minutes to soften it up after it’s full dried.

I’m 5’5” 170lbs I bought a 12. I would say these fit just right however I like my pants with a little more room I could have sized up. I bought the dark denim these are perfect for denim color office slacks. They can pass for slacks. All day wear will be comfortable. If you have a bloated bottom area on your stomach these will show that. There’s a nice top waist band about 4” of hold then it shoots into a thinner material. These have no lower tummy control. But for $20. I’m happy with my purchase.
I'm 5'5" 185 I bought the xl I do love them, but they stretch out a lot. My next pair will be a large. They are SUPER comfy!

I'm 5'5" and 115 lbs but with long legs and a preference for pants that almost touch the ground, so I have a hard time finding pants long enough. These are a small in the 33" inseam. They fit well although I wish they were an inch longer. I'm debating getting a pair in 35" and see if they work, but that might be too long. It's nice to have side pockets but if you have a big phone (like S22 ultra) the top 1.5 or 2 inches of your phone will poke out. My only real complaint is the elastic that runs along the very top of the waistband. I have a 27" waist and it really digs into me - enough that it's kindof uncomfortable. As embarrassing as it is to show my belly in the pics, I feel like it's necessary to show the fit. Definitely not flattering on the belly, at least not if you've had kids! But for day to day, they look fine bc I don't walk around in belly shirts. The uncomfortable waistband is the only thing keeping me from getting another pair. They feel well made and the fabric is really nice - nice and thick material, very soft and very stretchy.

I’m 5’5” and 125 pounds, I purchased the small and love the fit of these trousers. The perfect wide leg look I was looking for, at a great price point. They do need to be cinched in with a belt, but thankfully there are belt loops! I recommend them.
I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs. I got a medium and they fit perfectly. Sure hope they don't shrink!!!! 🙏 Perfect length. I can see me wearing these with lots my new birthday tops!

I’m 5’5” and 138 lbs and wear Med or 4/6 size pants. It fits slightly bit loose side but I’m not sure how small would fit me. I like it so much I could wear it to anywhere. I’ll definitely order different color- black or white!!!

I’m 5’5” and 138 lbs and wear Med or 4/6 size pants. It fits slightly bit loose side but I’m not sure how small would fit me. I like it so much I could wear it to anywhere. I’ll definitely order different color- black or white!!!

I'm 5'5" and 140lb. I usually get a M, and this M fit perfectly. I love these pants! The fit and color are so cute. I was looking for something to wear to a Barbie party, and I've found it. Heels are a must since the bottoms are so long. The first pic is me in socks, the second pic is me in heels. I may have to shorten these with pins anyway.

I'm 5'5" and 150lb. Short torso and prefer high waist jeans. Usually wear around 10 to 12. Sizing chart for these was so confusing that I ordered an 8, 10 and 12. Went with the 10. Here's why: 8 - I knew by looking that they would not fit 10 - tight around belly, too tight at first. But butt and thighs fit good. 12 - Waist was much more comfortable, but butt and thighs had too much extra fabric. Wore the 10 all day and even went skating in them. Never sagged. Never got the "MC Hammer" crotch. The butt is still looser than I'd like, but it's not saggy. The waist was fine, even with sitting most of the day for work. Did not feel like I I had to pull them up after squatting down and standing up, although I still did this. Probably all jeans will do this unless they're jeggings. Have washed and dried (on low) twice and so far, size has not changed. Gave 4 stars instead of 5 because they just do not hug my little to no butt.

I'm 5'5" and 156lbs and I'm wearing a large after washing on cold and drying on high. They definitely have the feel of polyester. If you don't like that feel then these are not for you. They're comfortable and they'll be great for running errands or lounging around the house but they're not my favorite wide leg sweatpant as far as I'm the looks department and the way they fit. I would give a 3.5 on that. For the current price of under $19 and even better with the $6 off coupon making them under $13 they're, in my opinion, a decent pant to have for lounging. I give them a 4 for being as described, for not shrinking much, if at all, when washing and drying, and for being a decent pant for the current price point.
I’m 5’5” and 90 lbs and purchased size XS. I do have skinny stick legs but as you can see in the pics, they just aren’t a good fit overall. Returned them.

I’m 5’5” and almost 120lbs. The length is just about perfect for my height and the style is cute. They are very comfy and I will definitely be wearing them for the mere fact that they feel good. My only dislike is the are a bit big and “poochy” in the front when I lean forward etc…see last photo. I would like them to be a little more fitted in this area. Overall, not perfect but good! I think it just all depends on your body shape and fit preferences but the quality of the jeans appears very good.

I’m 5’5” and between 185-190, normally wear XL and sized up to XXL. The size is perfect and comfortable as it isn’t too snug and not too loose. I did not want a tight fit, where buttons would pop when sitting. I love the weight of the material as it has some stretch, it isn’t stiff or thick. It will be great during spring or fall. I folded the cuffs up and it’s perfect. My boyfriend immediately said how cute it was!
I’m 5’5” and the pants hit above my ankles.

I’m 5’5” and these are a great length for me. They feel like wearing pajamas for sweatpants but look very nice and business professional. I was afraid they would snag easily but they don’t. Pleats on me aren’t the most flattering, but that’s what made them so comfy and I didn’t feel they were unattractive on me.

I’m 5’5”, 115 and I got my normal size, 0, and these are so huge and baggy. The butt is baggy and I have a butt and the legs are baggy and don’t fit skinny. Disappointed because I got these and another pair to travel in. How is this a zero? Fits more like a 4 on me than a 0

I'm 5'5", 140lbs, little thicker in the belly/hip area and these pants accentuated all the wrong parts!! I got a Medium. There is a little stretch in the waist which is nice, but the waist does run pretty tight compared to the legs. Not a 'skinny leg' fit like the picture. Plus, the material is pretty thin and cheap looking. I paid about $35 for these pants and I would expect this kind of quality from pants half the price or less. I don't return items from Amazon often but I will be returning these. For the price I paid, I could get similar pants with a much better quality and fit. If you're on the chubbier side and looking for high waisted jegging-style pants for comfort and lower belly concealment, these are NOT for you!!

I’m 5’5”, 257 and us size 18-20 — the 2xl feels great! I saw these type of pants on tiktok and how great they looked on curvy plus size people so I had to try them myself. To me, these make my hips look wider and my butt bigger, which I’m sure some people may want but for me I like it a little more compressed (no shade, just preference!). These feel like pajama pants but I paired them with a bodysuit and they looked cute enough for a date or work outfit for sure. I’m not sure what boots to put with them but I threw some on for the sake of the pic. These hit right at my feet where pants normally do, not too long!

I’m 5’5”, about 115lbs and I got a size 2. I always have a hard time buying work pants for under $50 anywhere so I was really happy when these showed up and they were exactly what I wanted. Will definitely be buying more in other colors. I live in FL so super thick pants don’t work, which these aren’t. They’re thick enough, comfortable, the have a bit of stretch in the leg and even belt loops - just in case. Also they’re very professional looking. The only thing that would make these better is off the pockets worked lol.. but I know that’s a thing with girl pants, so no real complaints. 🤗

I'm 5'6 , wear a 5 or 7 sometimes in pants, 160lbs.. ordered a small & fit just perfect! No rhinestones were missing, very nice soooooo worth the price.. I Loved it! :) Looking to order the black one next..
I’m 5’6 130lbs and I ordered a size small and medium. I prefer the small, they fit perfectly. They are buttery soft and super cute. Can’t beat the price!

I'm 5'6 and right now I'm 132lbs. I wear size 26/2. I ordered a small and it's perfect. I have a small waist but thick thighs for my size. I hate having shorts squeeze my thighs. The waistband is stretchy and hits at the right spot so that it’s not too tight. They also definitely pass the sit test. Overall, for $17, definitely worth a try.

I'm 5'6 and weigh 235. I have a very bloated stomach due to a medical condition and I was worried that these wouldn't fit, that the waistband would be stretched to the maximum and make me uncomfortable. I’m 5’6 ish and I ordered a small, they fit great around my waist & they stop right at my ankles. The only reason I gave these 4 stars & not 5 is because there’s extra fabric around my cooch area in the front & it only goes away if I pull the pants up super high, like right under my tits. Maybe size down??

I’m 5’6, 125lb, 34DD. I usually wear a 26 and sometimes 27 in designer jeans and ordered size 26. I love these jeans! It’s like I’m reliving 1993! They are super stretchy and fit great. I took photos from three angles so you can see. They’re a nice fit all the way around but almost too tight in the waist for me. The material stretches so even though it’s tight, it’s not uncomfortable. I will, however, be careful to wash them in cold and hang them to dry. These are the highest waisted jeans I’ve ever owned but the buttons on the front really make them work. This is actually perfect for the crop tops that I sometimes hesitate to wear because now my midsection will be adequately covered. The length is perfect- I can wear tennies or boots or even a low heel and these jeans will look good with any of them. I highly recommend these jeans. If you want a looser fit, I would size up one size.

I'm 5'6, 132lbs, wear smalls/size 2(26). I ordered these pants in the small khaki color. The length is perfect for me but beware I'm more torso than I am legs. The pants hit right at the top of my feet. The waist has a thick elastic band making these very comfortable. The color is as pictured. The corduroy is good - sometimes I've seen really cheap looking corduroy that doesn't lay right. I like the fit and shape. I would have liked a slightly larger leg shape starting at the knee for a more exaggerated bell bottom look instead of just boot-cut look. If you are shorter or have a longer torso than legs these might be great for you. Cute jeans. Really happy with these. Add picture and wanted to add that these are so stretchy they do stretch out as the day goes on.

I’m 5’6, 140lbs and ordered Smalls! I normally order a Medium in these sizes. But these run true to size but stretch so go with your size, chart size. They could run a inch longer but they work with a 2” heel of any kind! The Flair lays perfectly over the shoe, making your legs look longer. 😍 They have a slight smell to them but came out with one wash! But that’s normal with Denim!

I’m 5’6, 176.6 lbs & bout size 10 with 30” length (this always helps me so much so I thought I’d add it in) I gain weight evenly with a little extra around my middle. I thought-because I hadn’t read the description, because I forget to do important things like that- that these we jegging style with no button or zipper. I’m glad I didn’t see that it had them because I wouldn’t have bought them. Since 2020 I believe strongly in “the closer to lounge wear the closer to God”. You can quote me. Alas these have a button and zipper but it doesn’t make my fupa look like a butt! (Insert every happy emoji here) That is always my fear with traditional jeans. - I have one butt I don’t need 2. Also these aren’t high rise jeans like our moms (I’m 38) or teenagers wear. They’re mid rise. Can I get a hell yea?!?! I put up some review pictures and in them I I sucking in my gut in one and I’m not in the other so that you can get an accurate idea of muffin top if you’re my size. I feel like these are the perfect height to not be too muffin top producing. I know you may not be able to tell because I don’t have a fully suck-inable gut but whatever I feel like it would help me if people did that for me.

The fabric is lightweight and feels like a thick chiffon. The pants have a button/zipper fly and deep hip pockets but the back pockets are fake. There are belt loops on the waistband but the back is elastic so the belt is optional. The color is a deep navy. The legs are long enough that I have to wear at least a short heel to keep them from dragging the ground. What I like: 1. The super high waist and wide leg style. 2. Lightweight and flowy, good for summer work pants. 3. Real (and deep) pockets. What I don’t like: 1. The material has a nylon pantyhose feel, just scratchy enough to be noticeable. 2. Profile of the pants is kind of shapeless. The fabric just hangs and does very little to flatter the figure.

Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

On closer inspection, it appears that they photoshopped out the white sneakers that the model was actually wearing and added in the black heels to make the pants look like they come down farther. According to the size chart, a small is 38.5" long (length of inseam isn't listed). My pair is exactly 40" long with a 32" inseam. The design is pretty and the pants are comfortable, but they are just WAY TOO SHORT!

These jeggings had a nice comfort band and the material was comfortable and a little thicker than I expected. It definitely is not a cheap looking or feeling pant. I sent it back because I didn't like how shirt it was on me. If it had been longer then it would have made a winter staple for sure due to the thickness of the material, which is a little heavier than I'm used to.

I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

I’m 60, 5’5” and 165lbs. I carry excess weight in my tummy which is 36 and my hips are 44.” The size guide did not make much sense. It stated an XL waist measured 23.6 which to me is more like an XXS. Fortunately I’ve purchased jeans and pants from this brand, LifeShe, so I ordered my “usual” size XL in Blue. The fit is slightly loose, which is the style/fit I prefer. These do not fit me low rise, but I’m very short waisted. The waistband is approx 36” relaxed. It’s elasticized but only on the sides and I’m especially happy there are belt loops. The care tag states cotton and polyester, no percentages but it looks like all cotton and it definitely feels like all cotton. It’s lightweight and breathable but not thin or flimsy; a nice quality denim constructed very well. I can’t think of anything I dislike about these jeans - from the deep pockets (the front right has a hidden change pocket) to the contrasting stitching. I love barrel jeans/pants and have quite a few, but I’d consider these more of a reg baggy style, although they do flare out in the hips (these measure 26” laying flat side to side). The length/inseam may not be “super tall friendly.” In my image I’m wearing platform Converses that are about 1.5.” I wish these were just an inch longer in case they shrink. If they do will update my review. I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review. However, I feel at just under $40 the price is very fair. They’re actually 10% off at the time of this review (plus free Prime shipping). I love these so much I purchased the black & white ones.

I’m 61, 5’3”, 179 lbs - Age is important, because it changes body shape. I’m excited to find these jeans, so I bought 4 different pairs, hoping something would work. Three of 4 are keepers. I particularly like the stretch, because they’re as comfortable as yoga pants. I don’t mind the waist being a little loose, and it has a slight gap. I’d rather it didn’t cut in and make a roll. It’s not to the point I’m pulling them up. I have thick thighs and don’t want pull lines you get when you cross from fitted to too small jeans. I’m at that age that comfort is important, but so is style. 😁 Over 10 years ago a blogger, Grasping For Objectivity, did a big series on jeans, showing how important pocket placement is along with pictures of the same women in different jeans. It was the only article like that, and it’s still the best source as it showed comparisons of real women and told the brands. (Never buy ON) Why is this relevant? THESE JEANS have the RIGHT pocket placement and aren’t remotely close to $100. What. A. Score! These will be my go-to from now on. I found the inseam on regular is longer than many other brands. At 5’3” I should always need petite length but have a short waist and long legs, so I usually require regular length, especially in cheap brands like Lee. You can see that regular length is very long on me. I could’ve ordered petite, but I don’t mind. If you wear heals, this would be perfect. I will buy these again. I’ve found my go-to jeans!

I’m a size 8, 5’7, 163ibs and I went with a large. The material is super comfortable. Not too heavy or light weight. They are a tad too short for me to wear straight, which I expected. Still perfect for tying at the bottom. I replaced my AE cargo pants with these, and I prefer these. Only knocked down a star for the length, otherwise, I would have made it 5.

I'm a straight-leg girl, but I liked the look of these online so I thought I'd give them a try. I actually like the way they look and feel overall. The flares balance my broad shoulders nicely...they definitely do away with that top-heavy look that skinny jeans and a blousy top can cause (I'm an inverted triangle shape, so leggings and skinnies can make me look like a chicken if I'm not careful lol). They're incredibly stretchy and soft, and I was shocked to find them long enough for my 33.5" inseam (I NEVER find jeans long enough unless I order tall, and even then they're sometimes only a 32"). The length alone made me celebrate, and I can actually wear them with heels! My only issue with them is that I am short-waisted, even though I'm tall, and these are not made for a short torso. They button at my ribs, which I kind of hate. It feels suffocating. I typically buy mid-rise jeans (I like them under my belly button an inch or so), so getting used to these literally poking the bottom of my breasts when I sit down will be a whole thing. They're ok when standing (no muffin top!), but sitting feels super...awkward. I don't think these will replace my usual Joe's jeans, but I enjoyed wearing them for a night out when we were in Vegas, and they were quite comfy. They'll be nice for occasional wear, and are a great compromise between slacks and jeans when it comes to dressing for those occasions in which you're unsure of the dress code. I ordered a 14 (I usually wear a 31 or 32 jeans) because I have a bigger booty, but these have so much stretch that sizing up was unnecessary. They're quite roomy in the waist...I think a 10 or 12 would probably have been better (if I reordered, I would go with the 10). They work, though, because they're so soft and stretchy. I would recommend these as long as the uber high waist doesn't bother you. If you're used to skinnies, these still have that soft feel, so they're a good transitional option before you dive into a pair of stiff, high waisted Levi's. I washed in cold and dried on medium. They did shrink a bit (which is what I was going for when I put them in the dryer). Now I would probably just hang them to dry.

The face is very shiny, and the back is black. They have a wide elastic waistband that's quite comfortable. They're a tiny bit short on me right now but should relax down a couple of inches as I get closer to a true large size. These are flashy and fun, and great for either a night out or just around the house!

I'm always a little wary of ordering pants online because the sizing can be way off. However, these fit exactly as I expected based on the product description and sizing chart. I'm 5'4" and weigh 125lb so I typically wear an XS or size 2 in pants. However, the sizing chart on Amazon indicated a waist and hip measurements in an XS that made me think they would be too small. So, I ordered the small and they fit me perfectly. I have short legs so I knew that a 29" inseam would be too long and that I would need to hem them. However, because of the excellent product description, I was not disappointed when the product arrived and it was exactly how I expected it to be. The actual pants themselves are beautifully soft and comfortable. The material is great quality and they are extremely well made. These are really lovely pants and I highly recommend them!

I'm always cold, so I finally started buying more fleece-lined clothes. These keep my legs warm and I am happy that I bought them. I would prefer a longer inseam though! -Sizing- I’m 5’3” and I bought "Elasticwaist Blue88" in medium when I was wearing about an eight in pants, but I couldn't get these on at all! The next winter I was down to a four or six - and these finally fit. Several reviewers said these ran large, but my experience is that the medium runs small. -Washing- These machine wash and dry easily, without any noticeable shrinking. Colorfast on the outside, fluffy warm on the inside. I wash these with my dark clothes. -Wearing- Thick fleece = bulky. The bulk isn’t unreasonable, but it is visible without a jacket. These pants are fairly stretchy, but my issue is the length. Walking is fine, but sitting gets uncomfortable because the pants are really tight on my kneecaps. So I pull these up to get more slack at the knees -which makes driving more comfortable- but then my ankles get cold because they've lost their cover. A longer inseam would be great. Until then I just need to be mindful about socks or boots to compensate for cold ankles at the expense of knee mobility. -Overall- Great buy! I can finally go outdoors in the winter and not freeze immediately. A longer inseam would be nice, but these are still worth buying if you’re always cold like me.

I'm currently replacing old belts in my wardrobe accessories, and I really like reversible ones because I like the two color options in one belt. So, getting this AWAYTR Reversible Leather Belt was an easy decision for me. The first thing for me to say is that before I ordered it, I noticed that its design looked more like something I would wear instead of something my wife would, even though it's advertised first as a woman's belt. Once I was able to look at it in person, the style's definitely something I could wear. With that observation out of the way, here is my assessment of the belt. It is well made with good thick leather, a stylish belt buckle, and the stitching is really good. Reversing the belt is easy to do, and it locks solidly in place. The length of the belt can be cut down for more of a proper fit, if necessary. I found this info on the product page (Instructions for doing this did not come with the belt). I cut about 2 1/2" from the length to give me the correct fit for my 34" waist. Also, the two colors, black/coffee, are exactly the same as belts that I'm getting rid of, so I am really satisfied with this new belt.

I’m currently: 30 weeks - 5’5” - 152lbs. Have mainly gained weight in belly/boobs not so much hips/legs/butt. My impressions: opening the package, yes they have a smell. I guarantee this goes away once washed. Look: I received what is modeled regarding the color and distressing. Size: maybe a little bit long, but I like that I can wear them as a full pant or cuffed (pictured both). With how and where I carry weight a Medium felt true to size for me. Fit: I didn’t look at the size chart but what I can say is that the belly band sits a little high for me and my liking. I prefer it either completely under or over my belly. I am able to fold just the front elastic down and that sits perfectly under my belly. The legs are just a little loose - but that’s how I like my jeans to fit. Stretch: the right amount of elastic. I’m 95% positive that I’ll be able to wear these for the remainder of my pregnancy. Overall: I’m not disappointed in purchasing these jeans. I feel that the price matches the quality/value of the pants.

I’m ecstatic how great these fit! My first time ever attempting to purchase jeans online. I’m 5’4 3/4” and length was PERFECT with clogs. I bought the black pair after trying these on. Great stretch and extra room in the waist for super comfortable fit.

I'm excited for these. I've only tried them on but they have a nice high waist and long inseam. I'm 5'9," and 32" inseams don't quite cut it. There was the option to buy 12 Long or 12 Tall, but under product description, it didn't have a definition for what tall meant, which is frustrating. But I bought the Tall size and when I got the pants, the sticker tag said 34" L which is what I was hoping for. I'm 180 and I got a size 12. They fit good now but I think I will probably want a belt because they are quite stretchy.

I’m feet tall, so at the time I ordered these in size 11, I weighed 148 lbs. They felt snug compared to the size 11 I ordered in another shade. I’ve since lost more weight and the size 11 still fits fine. If your short like me, knowing that Wallflower still doesn’t make their regular jeans in petite sizes, if you roll the cuffs down on this particular style, they will fit fine like the jeans length. They came down around my ankles, if your okay with that. For some reason, I’m constantly having to pull my pants up which is annoying. I don’t need to wear a belt as these are snug. The material is medium to thick imo. Color is nice and dark. No bad smell noticed, nothing ripped yet. Oh, it also came with pet hair.

I’m five six I purchase the 31 inseam usually that would be a good length for me but this fitted like a 29 inseam the quality is amazing makes my butt look amazing if you’re shorter or don’t have a problem with them being high water I recommend

I'm fond of these jeans. I love a straight leg and I love a patch pocket, so I was already primed to like them. I was a little apprehensive about them being only 85% cotton, but they feel like true denim to me (unlike some more polyester-y feeling jeans.) The "classic blue" color really is a classic. I love that they're a solid wash without any whiskering or fading (which I tend to despise.) I only wish they had a little more slackness in the thighs, because they do cling to the tops of my thighs a little bit, which is typically not something that happens with a straight leg. But it's not too bad; I'm probably my own worst critic where my upper thighs are concerned. They hold my tummy pooch in rather than emphasizing it, which is nice. I am a size 10 with wide hips, and ordered the large. The waistband is snug, so these definitely run on the small side. I'm 5'5" and the hem falls just above my heel in the back. I usually prefer an ankle length, but these jeans are the perfect full length, which is better for wintertime anyway. I think they will be a smidge more comfortable after I lose just a couple pounds, but I am pleased with these jeans and anticipate wearing them a lot in the months to come.

I'm generally a size medium (waist 30in, inseam 31) but I misread the measurements and thought a large would fit me best. It didn't. Also, the color is definitely more ashy in person than in the photos. I'd say that if you were wearing these in office lighting, it'll look slightly darker than these photos, like a murky, ashy brown. Not my favorite color, and not what I was hoping for. I would almost consider keeping them if not for the sizing. I'm going to return these, and maybe order a different color, and definitely a different size.

I'm going to a pink party for my friends birthday so I ordered these jeans and WOW! I am in shock at how much I love them. Perfect fit, super comfy, stretchy but not too stretchy and exactly as pictured. I'm 5'3" 148 lbs and I ordered a medium. I carry almost all my weight in my in my legs and tend to have an issue with pants (of any type) being pulled down from my waist/butt by my damn legs and calves. These jeans don't give that potential feeling at all. They have not been washed yet as the party is next week so I'll update this review if needed once they've been worn for a length of time. As of right now though, 100% recommend.

I'm happy with this purchase. These are medium weight and don't feel thin or flimsy. Color is classy dark denim. Comfortable! I'm very happy with the value for the price. I am buying these because I need more stretchy clothing for my post partum body for the summer. I think the only thing I might not like is that the waist is rolling down, and then feels a little tight, but I think that's mostly because of my baby belly. I"m 5'-10", 190 lbs, and measure 44" around my butt, and the Large fits perfectly. They are also nice and long! I rolled them up for more of a capri look, but they roll down very long. With no time to shop, I'm turned to amazon for my summer wardrobe, and I'm excited to wear these with some of the cute tops I've purchased on amazon!

I'm impressed by the quality of these jeans. The slight stretch is nice for comfort and fit. I wanted to try a boho look with a couple different tops and Boston clogs. I was happy with the fit. For reference, I am 5'3" - 125# - 36B with an athletic/boyish build (a little thicker around the middle than I used to be...so I really appreciated the stretch of the fabric - very comfortable!)

I'm in love with these pants. My only complaint is that they are LONG!!! I’m in love with these. I’m 13 weeks pregnant with twins so regular jeans are getting too tight so I got these for work. They’re so comfortable, and soft. I will say if you’re short like myself (5’0) they will be scrunched up at the bottoms but I just fold the bottoms and you can’t tell.

I’m in love! I bought these because my work won’t let us wear yogas anymore. My best friend bought a pair and she said she loved them… so i gave it a try. They FIT JUST LIKE YOGAS but give that professional look my work is now requiring. I highly suggest. Im 5’2 and 155lb I got the medium tall. I like my pants long.

I’m in the process of losing the 30lbs that I gained and I didn’t want I spend $100 or more at the mall to buy a pair of jeans that I will hopefully only wear for a few months. The jeans that I love to wear with cowboy boots are not fitting at the moment and the other jeans I can squeeze into, just don’t sit properly over the boot and onto the heel. I found these, and with great hesitation, not being able to try them on, and facing the probability of having to return them, I pulled the trigger. They’re perfect!!! Granted, I do not have a beach body, but they are exactly what I was hoping for. They fit great with a little stretch, they’re a bit lower rise than expected, but they fall onto my boot perfectly!!! I am 142lbs and 5’3”. I ordered a 5x30.

I’m just not feeling the bell bottoms coming back in style. For one I’m to short and thick so they tend to make me look bigger. They are a good fit thru the waist and legs. For reference I’m 5’1 148lbs and I ordered these in a medium. If you like the bell bottoms then I would recommend these as they are a nice pair of jeans.

I'm just shy of 5'6 and around 150lbs. I measured my self to get the right size according to the size chart. According to that I was a 12 but I ordered a 10, which turned out to be way too big. Thankfully my daughter fit them. So I reordered a 6, still a bit loose but comfortably loose. Leaves room just in case they shrink after washing and drying. Overall I really like them. They have tons of stretch and the length is perfect. In my opinion maybe size down if you use the size chart. Or order what you normally would wear in pants.

I’m leaving this review for other skeptical amazon buyers lol. I’m not big on leaving reviews but other reviews help me so I want to help back .... I'm not one to wear satin pants but these csught my eye. Since these don't stretch I got the large going by my waist size but should have gotten the medium. I thought since these were cropped they would be full length on 5'1" me & with taller shoes they wouldn't drag on the floor. They drag. They are so soft & comfortable & made so well with the nice lining sewed in. The front lies flat, has belt hoops & the pants have a front zipper. I really wish they weren't so overly big on me because I wanted to wear these to be dressy comfortable but they will go unused. I’m not sure about these pants. I like the bright whiteness of them and I like that there’s pockets. They’re definitely a yoga style pant however, I feel like you could wear them as leggings if you want to. My issue with them is that they’re not as thick as I would of hoped to have had them, now they are not see-through however, because they’re not that thick they don’t do everything you want, leggings or yoga pants to do. Hide “things”! It’s hard to tell in these pictures with great lighting(I am using a ring light), but they’re not hiding everything you would want to hide. Ss a matter of fact, in some bad lighting, or maybe even a little bit of shadowing, it kind of makes your legs look even worse than what they probably are. So if you have dimples or any kind of cottage cheese going on in the thigh/hamstring/glutes area, you’re not gonna wanna wear these by themselves, ladies you know what I mean. But I feel they would be a good layering pant. Or perfect for a young lady that has nice smooth legs or super fit no jiggle or sagging in the skin yet hahah! They are definitely stretchy, so that’s a plus. I am 5‘9“ 145 to 150 pounds and these are a size medium.

I'm not sure how I got such nice jeans on sale at such a great price, but I'm thankful so thought a review is the least I can do. First off, thank you for various inseam options! I’m not the HUGEST fan of skinny jeans, so my 4 stars are cuz of that. But these were a great price in my size 6 long in white and I needed some white. They fit true to size. I am always a 6 (28) in stretchy jeans. Then I found the khaki was on sale but size 6 regular… you can see in the pics that the khaki are like ankle jeans on me. I’m 5’9, 165 lbs apple shaped. I wouldn’t pay full price for these, but am happy with my purchase. If they shrink or do anything weird in the wash I will update. I try to wear jeans a bunch before washing. They are so stretchy that they are extremely comfortable but do not sag during the day!

While that may be the case, I honestly don't know if I like them enough to be miserable for the hours it'll take to make that happen. I will say that the Premium material is a bit softer than the other stiffer Levi's jeans, like the standard Wedgie Skinnies. They still aren't soft though, so if that's what you're looking for, I would say these aren't for you. I'd recommend these for someone looking for a sturdier denim with little to no give. I'm sure they will last a long time. I ended up returning these and am unsure if I'll ever bother to try a 31. I've decided I'm not really a lover of the button fly. Oh well.

I'm sharing because I find detailed reviews extremely if they contain details beyond "I love this." I hope this is to someone else shopping. _ I'm 5'7", weigh 150 lbs (sometimes 158 lbs.). My shape is hourglass, meaning I have hips (40") and a smaller waist (33") I am over 50, so I have done some shopping in my life. My US size is 10-12 at Macy's and 12 at Target. _I like clothes that do not bind, loose fit, but do not look like I am wearing an old canvas bag. _I like good fabric, and I am a longtime sewer, so craftsmanship of clothes is important to me. I look at the construction of a garment. So, these pants: _Linen fabric is quality and a mid-weight, no shine and has a great but subtle linen texture - the fabric is soft to the skin and does not scratch or itch. Good for three seasons in the north of the USA. The drape is excellent right out of the bag without ironing or putting them in the dryer for a touch-up. The elastic at the waist is about 1-1/4 inch wide and not stiff - the elastic waist could easily accommodate a waist 8 inches bigger than mine or more - it depends on the fit you like - I actually might make the elastic a little tighter, an easy 10 minute fix if you sew or know someone who does sew. The pants have two good sized pockets. The construction is excellent. The fabric is cut correctly so it drapes correctly. _Depending on your lifestyle and personal style these pants can be worn almost anywhere besides a formal event. How you style with a top, shoes and accessories is the key. Definitely outside pants, not sleeping pants. _When shopping online make sure you get a tape measure and know your measurements. I ordered US L and the fit was as expected for me - I could have sized down one size to a US Medium, but I like a loose fit. Also, make sure you look at the manufacturer's size chart, not the generic Amazon size chart to make your size selection. _My photos are of the pants right out of the bag, and in front of a dusty (sorry) mirror, but hopefully they are to you. _I have been wearing them for a few hours now and these pants are so comfortable, so cute. If this is your style, your silhouette, I can completely recommend these pants. IXIMO Women's Casual Cotton Linen Baggy Pants with Elastic Waist

I’m still trying to decide how I feel about these pants. The 12s were too big, so I purchased the 10s this time. For the most part, they fit well, though a bit snug in the waist (I do carry most weight in my stomach). They have enough stretch to be comfortable though. The back pockets are big, which would be fine (since I keep my phone in my butt pocket), except they’re big enough that it goes below the bottom of my butt and creates an odd look. I’m certainly not bootylicious, but these pants definitely make my booty look flatter than it is. If you have any chub in your stomach, these pants don’t look great with a tight/fitted shirt - you’ll want to pair with something looser. I have examples of two different shirts of reference. I love doing a French tuck with my tops (tucking the front into my pants) - it looks fine from the front, but from the side, the pants definitely accentuate my stomach pooch. Since measurements are when deciding to buy clothes online, mine are 40-33.5-39, 168 lbs. As you can see from the pictures, they’re definitely a longer pant - I’m just shy of 5’6” and they go just below my ankle bone. Super comfy, but I’m not sure yet if I’ll keep them, since I want to feel confident in whatever I’m wearing for work and these definitely accentuate some flaws and are a bit hard to match tops with.

They are a little heavier weight wise than normal jeans but when you put them on they just feel like normal & comfortable stretch jeans. These jeans are a really good fit and I highly recommend them.

I’m surprised by the quality of these belts. They are well made for the price point, and the simplicity of the buckle elevates any outfit. The belts were packaged with care. Each belt had a sleeve and the buckles had protective tape on them. They also came in a nice box. I got the large size and I am 170 lbs and carry weight around my belly. When I use the belt on my pants, I could button it on the 3rd to last notch. When I use it for an accent piece with my dresses, I can get it to the last notch because it sits higher on my waist. I could go for a smaller size, but I don’t like the look of the extra holes showing on the belt. With the larger size, most of the notches are hidden and makes the belt look more tailored and streamlined. The extra length of the belt that goes around the waist underneath doesn’t give much bulk and is still comfortable. So it’s up to personal preference whether you want to go with size or look. Both the brown and black are great colors to work with any outfit. Whether to pull the look together or to break up sections. I’d purchase it again in a different color.

I’m taller - 5’8” - so some shorts are just entirely too short but these are perfect! Great stretch. Fits perfect. I am now blessed with a cutie bootie but these are still flattering to my shape. Held up well in the wash too. I’ve ordered multiple versions of these from this vendor in the last two summers and still wear them all regularly!

I'm terrible at taking pics. But I got some of the pants, the length, and with an entire outfit. I hope they help. For reference, I am 5'0" and a out 130lbs (changes between 128-133 often, because well... food) anyway..... If you have any type of belly weight, I would recommend sizing up. My belly weight is just skin from losing a lot of weight (I was 165 last year, but being this short created a lot of extra skin in my fupa area). If you have extreme belly weight, these can still work for you. Just size up and wear a longer shirt. The pants are lovely. They have a great stretch and they help tuck in and hide a few problem areas for me. The length is perfect. I got a size small with 27" inseam. I have these in black and khaki now. I will absolutely be buying a few more in different colors and may go up a size on a few for a more relaxed fit. (Ya know.. some days bloating takes over and I just want it to hang a little more loose). If you're on the fence, I would recommend taking a leap of faith. You can dress them up and down. I mean, I wouldn't exactly wear them to a yoga class, but they could certainly handle the job if you wanted to try. I'm wearing them at work right now. Hence the bathroom pics b3cause I don't have full length mirrors at home.

I'm torn. These are soft and fit okay for plus size. However, these are very green/black and not a brighter green/teal, as pics in product description suggest. Also, the white is see through, so I wouod need to wear a tunic with these. Haven't decided if this is a keep or return, but I am leaning towards return. I’m trying to add looser non-skinny jeans to my closet. the elastic waistband makes these very comfortable. I wish they were a little longer but I am 6 ft tall, they will fit average height people well. These were ankle length for me I ordered xl.

I’m trying to add looser non-skinny jeans to my closet. the elastic waistband makes these very comfortable. I wish they were a little longer but I am 6 ft tall, they will fit average height people well. These were ankle length for me I ordered xl.

I'm typically an 8 or a 10 because my thighs are somewhat curvy, and read all the reviews and decided on a 30 and two 31s. I'm so sick of stretch in jeans and cropped lengths so was hopeful. Unlike others, I didn't find these to run that crazy small. They are made for a semi-curvy pear shape. My waist is about 30" and my thighs 41". Black 31's were not bad in the waist but big in the thighs, the dark wash 31s were too big, the dark wash 30's I'm keeping. If you have a similar proportion and are sick of waist gaps these are worth considering if the price is right.The denim material itself seems fine but the stitching is not that well done and the thread quality looks poor. I can update when I wear them but I'd thrift actual 90s jeans vs paying over $40 for these. They are not quality wise as good as ones from the 90s but where these differ is they are slimmer in the leg than most 90s jeans and are higher waisted than a number of them as well. I'm tired of companies today calling an 11" rise high. Not for a long torso. These are 13" on the 30's and 31's and actually hit the smallest part of my waist which is the whole point of a high rise!The bummer is they are so long. I wish they looked on me like some of the other reviewers. I'm 5'8" so I'm not short but I did have to roll them twice like the stock photo and it was bulky. Perhaps smaller sizes are shorter or they shrink in the wash? All the ones I bought had an inseam around 33.5". The amount of bulk is going to look bad if you're under 5'6". I might consider hemming them as my preference is not cuffing at all to keep them looking sleek.The black ones in the photo are size 31 in case it helps you on sizing. I feel like they measure exactly what their stated size is when pulled to their max - I.E. the 31's are 15.5" flat and closer to 15" relaxed.

I’m usually a 1-2x and after reading the reviews I ordered a 5x. On no planet but this one am I a 5x. I’m 5’4” and 250 lbs with a curvy backside and these fit perfectly. Ignore the try on shoes in the picture and I’d definitely roll them up and wear with a long top.

I'm usually a medium or large I got the extra large because of the reviews and I'm glad I did although I think a large would have been fine. Super comfy very stretchy thin material not see-through at all. Pockets are good size my phone Fitz. I took a point off because they're very unflattering like I don't think I'd wear these out unless I'm in my yard. Although I did gain weight and it might just be me I think I'm now 168lbs. 5.5ft

I'm usually a size 12 or xlarge but ordered up to a 14 in these and I'm glad I did these run a full size small. These are a nice heavy pair of jeans with such a soft inside I actually said WOW when I tried them on. These will keep you so nice and warm in the cold weather. I live in New England and it gets cold, my legs get cold so easily and I usually wear tights under my jean but I won't need them with these. These are not cheap flimsy jeans these are nice and thick made really well. I ordered the Light Blue and will be buying the Dark Blue so I'll have a couple of really warm pairs of jeans. These have a good amount of stretch in every direction for a good fit. I pulled one leg inside out so you can see how well these are made. Good price for the quality of these jeans. For reference I'm 5.3 150lbs big hips and thighs.

I’m very disappointed in the color. I ordered the purple and instead I got a pinky/lavender color. I’ve provided a side by side comparison of the product image and the actual color. I am 5’7” and I wish they were an inch or two longer, but I’m a little taller than average. Standing they about touch my ankles, but sitting they creep up to the calf. Very comfy though. I just wish they were the color as pictured.

I'm very disappointed in these pants, as they are a cute design and looked like they'd be comfortable. But I can't really wear them after washing them. For reference, I am 5'7" and 133#. I ordered size Small. My photos were taken BEFORE I washed them... 1) There are many loose threads hanging off these pants, due to low quality control. 2) The elastic waist is very tight. Too tight for a supposedly comfy pajama/lounge pant, especially one worn mid-rise. I think buyers should size-UP. 3) These pants are 100% Cotton, and definitely shrank after the first wash in cold water and tumble dry on Low heat. Now they feel even smaller and are too short for me. In general, I recommend that buyers SIZE-UP by 1-2 sizes, and either hang dry or dry on low heat for very short time. Too bad the quality is low, because the navy/white gingham design and "boxer" look is cute.

I'm very happy with everything about these pants. I'm 5'9 tall and 135 pnds and I ordered a Sz Medium, and they are a great fit and long enough!!!! Which is hard to find!! Very cute casual/dress pants.! Oh and I received compliments from so many people the entire shift ! And I work at a hotel, so the "Ooohs & Aaahs" from ALL Adult Ages, too! 💜Them!
I’m very impressed how this fit. I was worried it would be tight but it’s wasn’t. I bought the XL and I wear size 38DD bra. Bought this for wedding photos. Great purchase!

I’m very pleased with this dress. I ordered a Medium. I’m 5’2” 149 pounds. 38DD. The dress was a little long so I washed it in hot water and dried it in the dryer hoping to shrink it since it’s 85% cotton and 15% polyester. It did shrink and I’m happy with the length. I decided to also purchase the dress in 2 more colors.

I’m very self conscious and picky about my clothes, but I love these shorts so much I had to jump straight on here and write a review. They look a little different than the picture, lighter than expected but still super cute. They have big deep pockets on the front and back, which is a huge plus considering how women’s clothing is usually made with little or no pockets. (Or sometimes even fake pockets- really what’s up with that?!) Anyway, back to the point. They are high waisted so I rolled the waistband over because I can’t stand my bellybutton being covered. I already know I’m going to be wearing these until my thighs rub holes in them. So if you’re on the fence, go ahead and buy them. You can thank me later! Now if you’ll excuse me, I’m going to order a black pair. For reference: I am 5’3, 230 lbs wearing an XXL. They are a little snug but not uncomfortably so.

I’m wearing a SMALL! Not sure why it says medium up too!? Anyways… These are quite literally the best pair of maternity jeans I’ve ever tried on!!! I am 5’0” and 23 weeks pregnant… being able to actually BUTTON your jeans and tuck in a shirt with out having that weird stretchy unflattering (but comfy) waist band that most jeans have… these are TOP NOTCH! I am a huge Levi’s jeans fan and I miss wearing mine so much! I wore them as long as I could. These fit very similar to how my Levi’s did! Super trendy and very very flattering!!! Now my short little legs can have some length back now that I can do my front tuck again 😂🫶🏼
I’m wearing a xsmall. I am 5’1 and 110 pounds. I love it!

I’m writing this review approximately one year after I purchased them for hiking and running in colder weather. After a year of use, they are still holding up - no rips or tears. Unfortunately, after the first wash on gentle cycle, I noticed some mild pilling, which only continued with further washes. They look pretty worn now. I found these to be less warm than other fleece-lined tights, so I would recommend them for temperatures no less than 45 degrees Fahrenheit. However, I do love the fit and color. I am 5’3 and these aren’t baggy at the bottom.

I’ve always been obsessed with leopard print everything, so when I found out FP had leopard print barrel jeans I was stoked until I realized they were out of stock so I opted for these instead. I absolutely love them! They aren’t as thick as FP but that’s okay. I’m normally a M - L (145lbs 5’4”) and didn’t want to risk them being too small so I got the L and I can’t wear them without a belt. So if you’re between sizes, go with the size down. The best and cheaper alternative with the exact same look and fit. I bought these months ago and they’re still holding out really well. The button piece above the zipper does seem to be loosening but that’s fixable.

I've always liked this brand. These are low mid rise (above hip bone) so if you've a muffin top, prepare to cover it or show it good thin summer but nice fabric (not heavy denim if that's what you're thinking) I'm 5"1+ and could wear them unfolded as they came and are in pic. With about 1.5-2inches folded. I like them and think theyre one of the few white pants I've not tried on 1st bought without that I'll keep!

I've always scrolled past 2-piece sets since they seem impossible to truly fit. I'm typically a little smaller in tops than bottoms. On a whim I bought this before a work conference. It was amazing! The linen/light material is so flattering, and the elastic waist was a nice touch for a 10+ hour day. The V neck is not too deep for professional environments. Overall, I got so many compliments. I feel like vests are trending and this could be my whole personality now- vest sets! I;m 5'5" and wear S/M. I ordered a Medium.

I've always wanted a pair of overalls so I was excited to find these and try them. I love them and don't know why I waited so long to get a pair. The fabric on these is really comfortable. Though it has the look of denim, it is a soft denim with a mix of cotton, polyester and spandex. The spandex makes them stretchy and the cotton makes them soft. These fit perfectly. And they look cute for wearing around too without working in them. Overall, I can't find anything I don't like and would recommend them for anyone who is looking for a comfortable pair of overalls.

I’ve been looking around for these Barrel style jeans, and came across a few different styles. I’m so glad to have gotten these, the fabric is soft and the make and styling is truly so nice. I’ve been looking for a belt like this for a while and now I have one where I have this nude color on one side and the robin egg light blue on the other and it’s only a little over an inch wide with the silver buckle but it Calvin Klein just added its best really beautiful well-made And I will say that if you’re a 38 inch waist and you want a couple extra notches that I would go up on a bigger size than me if you’re in between sizes size that not down because it can run a little small although it’s Calvin Klein great quality

I've been looking for a decent pair of ladies cargo jeans when I came across these. They don't fit me the way it looks on the models but I'll probably still keep. For starters, they're really long. I’ve been looking for a pair of cream colored linen pants and I thought these would be it. Unfortunately they’re too short. They’re just coming to the bottom of my ankle when I have no shoes on. I wanted more length so I can wear wedges. I’m 5’7’’ so not super tall or anything.
