I’ve been looking for pants that don’t cut into me. I’m 8 months postpartum and none of my clothes feel right anymore. These are comfortable and they don’t look too shabby on the back side. They give me plenty of room to be comfortable in chasing the baby without it riding down. I’m 5’0 and carry most of my weight on my stomach and thighs and ordered a large for reference. My only complaints are: -it’s too long. But that’s an easy fix. -the front pockets are fake -there’s no belt loops

I’ve been looking for something other than leggings to wear around the house, so I decided to give these a try. I’m 5’6”, 190 pounds, size 12/14 in pants. According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart, I fit in their size large which is what I ordered. I’ve worn them around for a few days, here are my thoughts.What I liked.• The fabric is soft and drapes nicely.• The fabric doesn’t get stretched out and baggy.• They are machine wash cold, tumble dry low. I laundered mine that way and they came out great.• The pants are solidly constructed and designed well. The manufacturer went the extra mile to add a yoke on the back of the pants which creates a more flattering fit.• They have 2 on-seam pockets that are big and deep. I could securely carry my iPhone 15 Pro Max in them.• They are comfortable, mostly.• They fit, mostly. I liked the 29” inseam, which hit me below the ankle. For hanging around the house, the length was good. I wasn’t stepping all over the hem, especially going up and down the stairs.What I didn’t like.• The fabric is a little odd. Yes, it’s soft, but it is more of a heavy knit.• The fabric is hot. Because of the loose, wide leg I thought they’d be nice and cool during our heatwave. They were not.• The pants were not a great fit through the hips on me. They were just a tiny bit snug. As a result, I preferred the look of a long, oversized t-shirt over them. They also seemed a little short in the rise (crotch).Overall, I think these sweatpants fall squarely in the “they’re OK” category. I wasn’t crazy about the feel and weight of the fabric, or the fit. However, they are well made and do launder great. I’m giving them 4 stars.

I’ve been looking for wide leg jeans and I liked how these looked on some of the ladies in the reviews and decided to take a chance but once I got them and tried them on they were an immediate no for me. I wanted to make it work because they were very different than all of my jeans and I wanted to get out of my comfort zone but the two things that I always look for is: how my butt looks and the length. Both a big NO on me. I’m 5’4” 130lbs with wide hips. The hips were fitted and had no stretch. My butt looked so flat and the length was a little too short for my liking. As far as thickness and quality, the material was a thicker jean with no give on the hold but the legs were comfortable (obviously with the width lol) and it appeared to be good quality.

I’ve been looking forever for a high-quality denim jacket with just the right amount of authentic fade but at an affordable price. I should have known to check out Levi’s first! This jacket is soft yet structured. The denim has a little personality and fade but still goes with everything. The fit is right on point. Seriously it doesn’t get much better than this! I opted for a M in the Jeanie wash and LOVE it. I’m 5’3”, 130lbs with muscular shoulders which usually prevent me from wearing a small. The medium is just the right fit without looking bulky. Jacket runs true to size.
I’ve been loving the flare jeans on my body lately and Levi’s never disappoints! I’m about 5’2” and 144lbs for reference.

I've been on the quest to find the perfect sweatpants, and these are as close as I've found so far. I'm 5'10", and the 34" pants were long enough! These are soft, comfy, I love that they're high waisted, and they make my small booty pop. However, these pants are super snaggy. My cat got on my lap for just a minute and snagged my pants in multiple places. So cat owners beware.

I’ve been searching for a pair of jeans that hit at the bellybutton. I’m 5’9” but short waisted, so high waisted jeans are terribly uncomfortable and look awful on me. These are fantastic. Just the right stretch. I got a large but could have gone with a medium.

I've been searching for ages for a pair of high waisted jeggings, and I finally found them! These are just the right blend of fabrics to make it not too stiff (like pants), and not too thin (like leggings). I was worried because the reviews said these are fleece-lined, but the ones I got definitely aren't. (They were referring to the longer ones possibly?) I'm not a huge fan of the buttons down the front, but hey, I'm not going to complain considering these are the only pair of high-waisted jeggings I could find on the market (with pockets in the back AND front no less!) Regardless, this is probably one of the best purchases I have ever made. Will certainly be buying again! (For reference, I am 5'0 and 110 pounds and I bought a small.) Edit: I've had these for a couple weeks now and the material is beginning to pill. Love the look, but wondering if I should return these due to the fabric not being able to hold after washing.
I’ve been searching for comfortable stylish cargo jeans and these were just right. For reference I’m 5’1” and these fit mid rise.

I’ve been searching for light weight summer pants and these are ok. It never looks like the model but I’ll keep. My husband says I look like I’m on vacation. I’m 5’8 and 142 lbs and glad I got a medium for the loose relaxed look.

I've been searching for the perfect black pants and these are nearly it! They are not for us short stacks though. I had to tuck under about 4" to get the length right. I originally ordered a medium, but had to send back for a small. Could probably use an xs, but since that's not an option, I'll be using hot water cycle lol. They feel amazing on, but do attract lent and pet hair I'm 5'1, 130

I’ve been seeing these on lots of influencers and decided to give them a try. I got the light blue and it’s such a pretty color. Def size down if between sizes, I got a 12 and it fit perfectly and are stretchy for sure. I’m got the regular length and it looks good on my 5’4” frame. The pockets are functional, it looks super cute dressed up with a blouse and heels or down with a t shirt and tennis shoes. These jeans will be a staple for me this Fall. Stretchy and comfy, good for light tummy control for those who look for jeans that offer that (I do!).

I’ve been struggling looking for overalls that fit me right and these are perfect. they’re either way too long or don’t fit my hips. I originally got a medium, my usual size, but it was just a little too big. I could’ve gotten away with it, but I prefer more waist definition (28” waist). the small size ended up being perfect! I love how much extra room there is on the hips!! overalls don’t usually have that!! also love the belt loops. the only issue I have is how ridiculously hard the buttons on the sides are to unbutton. I would recommend 100%

I've been wanting a jean jacket for a while, so ordered this one and the Levi's version ($10 more). I ended up keeping this one and returning the Levi's one. I'm 5'8" and 165, with super broad shoulders and a 38D chest. I'm normally between a medium and a large. I ordered a large in each jacket, and each fit, but this one is much more comfortable. The Levi's jacket is cut narrow in the shoulders, and has a more boxy shape. This one is broader in the shoulders and has a more tapered shape. The fabric is decently stretchy, and the jacket just molds to your body like a good-fitting pair of jeans. Looks super cute with jeans or a skirt and boots. The only negative I can think of is that the front pockets are seriously tiny, so small that my keys barely fit in there. (There are 2 inner phone pockets that are great. Can't live without a phone pocket!) But for $29 I'm thrilled.

I’ve been wanting some overalls and these are super cute, but the bib pokes out in a weird V shape and there’s no way to get it to lay flat. I’m ultimately sending these back because of the bib issue. Other than that, they are adorable. Size small, 5ft2 130lbs
I’ve been wanting this style pant for a while. Finally lost the weight I’ve been wanting to lose and ordered them. I’m 5’ll and 170. Size 12 is a perfect fit. Love love love!

I've been wanting to get away from the slim style of jeans. I decided on a size 12 here I am 5 feet 3 in tall and length is perfect at the regular length. I do have a belly but not much hip or butt. For me, the waist is just a little tight after I went out to dinner this evening. The legs but hips fit great. Nice Jean material as well.

I've been wearing IUGA capri yoga pants for years, so was excited when they came out with these jeggings. They're comfortable and fit well. They're a tiny bit less stretchy than their regular yoga pants, but still have great stretch. I can crouch down in them easily. I like that they are high waisted for tummy control. The front pockets are real pockets, but I will likely not use them, as it is an awkward place for pockets on leggings. The butt pockets are sufficient. For size reference, I'm 5'2'', 150 lbs, and am wearing a medium.

I’ve been wearing Silver jeans for over 10 years. I have Tuesday, Avery and Suki: skinny, slim boot cut and straight. I have both mid and low rise. I have the Boyfriend shorts too. I have tried other brands and nothing fits nor feels as good on as these jeans. I always go back to Silver. I just got these Tuesday low rise. I’m a short, curvy woman. 5’1” and 129lbs. These jeans are 27W x 31L. Out of the package they fit great. After one wash, even better. They are so confortable. Look closely at the denim and color options. Some denim is more distressed than others, some with intentional holes. I think they are great value for the money as I have an original pair from 10+ years ago.

I've been wearing skinny jeans and straight leg jeans for a long time, so I decided to 'step out' with these Ripped Baggy Wide Leg Jeans! They are regular denim, non-stretchy. I ordered my usual size 4 and they fit pretty well. For reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#. The length is perfect, the waist is pretty good (very high-waisted) although a little snug, and the butt fits okay (a little loose). They are baggy and wide from the upper thighs all the way down to the wide bottoms. I'd say the legs are super loose and comfy, but the waist is what is snug since it's so high-waisted. The amount of rips is perfect, and I think necessary to off-set the bagginess and amount (lots) of material that these jeans have. I was able to stay in them all day, although would have been more comfortable if the waist was not so snug. I love the way these jeans look in the front, but do not love the way my butt looks. I guess since there is no design on the back pockets and since the butt is loose, it makes my big butt look big :(. I'm giving these jeans 4 stars as I wish the waist was slightly looser to be more comfortable, but I wish the butt was tighter to look better. Glad to own these fun new jeans though! My teenage daughter saw me wearing these jeans and said, "What are those? You are turning into me!" (And she approved!) Thank you!

I've been wearing these pants for a couple of weeks now, and while they are a bit firmer than my other Motherhood Maternity skinny pants, they are still pretty comfortable. I wouldn't call these stretchy. For reference, I'm 5'4 and currently weigh probably around 130 pounds. I'm 5 months pregnant but I'm showing as though I were already 7 or 8! Pictures included for reference on fit. I was a little confused when I got these because they are in fact labeled as "Secret Fit Belly" but the actual pants label on the back says 'Branch & Twig' instead of Motherhood Maternity. Not sure if I got the right ones? But based on the photos, they look the same. These fit me well, not too short or not too long. My only slight complain is that at times the top of the tummy can ride down just a tad. But as I have gotten bigger, that has not been such an issue. There seems to be a better hold now. Overall, I think these look really good on me. I love the dark wash color and they have yet to get any sort of piling or lose their color brightness.

I’ve been wearing these sweats in my rotation at home since August, and they’re not my top pick, but they are pretty nice. To be fair, I like a sweatpant with a really loose fit, especially in the waist. I’m pretty average sized (5’4”, 150lbs, athletic, longer torso and shorter legs), and I would not call these baggy by any means. The waist is definitely tight enough to where you can feel that it’s there; these are more the type of sweatpants that I wouldn’t feel at all uncomfortable wearing out of the house. They’ve got a cinched ankle, a cute tie in the waistband, and good pockets. If you prefer some sweats that hug your bum and won’t slide down at all, or if you are a waif queen, these will definitely be a nice option for you! They are quite soft and comfy in terms of material. As a side note, I know the selling page says to hand wash, but I’ve been tossing these in the laundry with everything else and haven’t had a problem. Like with all my drawstring pants, I tie a little bunny knot with the strings so they don’t get lost in the waistband. The price is right, and for some, these might be the perfect sweaties!

I've bought multiple of these adorable pants from various brands and all were child like in size and costume material appearance. But these are actual thick soft quilty cotton material that gives and forms to your body for the cutest flattering fit. High waisted XL cut for an ADULT! I was shocked when I pulled them out the bag to see the size. I held them up to me and knew they'd fit. I was so happy cuz these pants are so cute and I'd been trying to find a decent pair and these are better than expected! I'm 233 lbs 5'4" most weight in curvy plus hips and butt and these XL fit perfect. So comfy

I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

I've got a smaller waist but big ol' hips--am 5'5" and "curvy." These fit great, and I like the Dark Wash color option. The only annoying thing is that the bottom flares out (not tapered like in the picture). Gonna look for a tailor and see if they can fix that. I've tried soooo many capris it's ridiculous; for me, these were definitely the best that I've come across, so going through the effort of fixing the flare is is worth it. Hope this helps!

I’ve gotten multiple compliments on these pants in just on night. They’re thin, so good for the hot Florida weather. I love how big they are and they fit my 5’3 short self. And I got a medium. The draw string doesn’t tighten the pants; they’re just for show.
I’ve gotten so many compliments on this dress. A causal dress appropriate for most of my work events, comfortable and true to size. 5’ 3” and 135lbs
I've gotten so many compliments wearing these jeans. They're comfy and look super secure qt the same time. I'm 5'9" and bouth a size XS
I've gotten so many compliments wearing these jeans. They're comfy and look super secure qt the same time. I'm 5'9" and bouth a size XS

I've had a reallyyyyyy hard time finding decent maternity clothing in general, and I'm pretty picky about my jean shorts. I'm the typical American Eagle jean short wearer, so I was hoping these would be stretchy like those, and they sure are!! My ONLY complaint is they don't fit close enough to the bum around the legs (I hate the diaper butt look), but I could've sized down and that would probably be fixed. They are very comfortable and have ample amount of stretch. The length is great too — short enough to be "hip" but not too short to be hoochie mama : ) For reference I'm 5'3, 145lbs, 6 months pregnant, and wore a size small before getting pregnant in all bottoms (was 130lbs). I ordered these in a medium but probably could've ordered a small to fix the fit in the rear. They should consider making a "distressed" version of these!!

I’ve had a vision of what I want my jeans to do and look like on me and I finally found them! They’re PERFECT! So perfect that I can’t stop sneaking a peek at my butt any time I walk past a mirror in these lol. I am 5’5”, about 125 lbs, waist measures 27”, hips/butt measure 39”, and these are a size 26 in Tango Light. Before Levi’s, my go to jeans were American Eagle size 2 for a snug fit or 4 for a comfortable relaxed fit. According to the Levi’s size chart, my measurements put me in size 26 for my waist and size 27 for my hips. I have a pair of originals in size 27 because they are 100% cotton and I was afraid buying to fit my waist would flatten my butt, but they have relaxed really nicely to more of a boyfriend look. Seeing how well the 100% cotton originals stretched, I felt confident buying the Wedgies in size 26 and I’m glad I did. They *feel* like 100% cotton, they hold in and support like 100% cotton, but that 1% of elastane makes bending over and bending at the knee so much more comfortable, and they fit beautifully. A tiny bit of butt flattening at first, but once I wore them around the house for an hour they were perfect!

I’ve had my eye on these jeans for a while. I hesitate buying jeans without trying them on though, because it’s a hit or miss if they fit or not. I’m so glad I ordered them. I love these jeans!! The quality is great, they don’t feel like cheap or generic denim. They have good stretch too, and comfortable enough to move around in. The inseam is pretty long though. Even with 2-3 inch heels I still have to fold them at the bottom. Then again, I am only 5’2” lol. I like how it has the drawstring at the bottom, bc I was able to pull them a little tighter to prevent from dragging too much when I’m wearing my platform Chucks. In the picture I’m wearing chunky 3 in heels and with it rolled up once, they’re the perfect length. I followed the size chart and bought the right size (XL) according to the size chart. They fit perfect. For reference, I have a 33.5 in waist, curvy hips, I have some booty back there lol and thick thighs. And they fit like a glove on me. I felt like they hugged me in all the right spots. If you want the loose, baggy look, I would size up. But I love these jeans so much and I feel like they can be dressed up or dressed down. They remind me of the jeans we wore in high school, and I love the flap pockets on the butt. It’s more flattering for your booty. Only reason I gave 4 stars is because they are super long. And with the drawstring, can’t really hem them. It would be awesome if made these with different inseam lengths. Still love the jeans either way, these jeans are gonna be a part of my regular rotation.

If you email me and ask me if I’m satisfied, I will absolutely say yes yes and yes. The waist size is great. The length is great and the elasticity in the jeans is great. Just for reference I am 5’.5”, I weigh 138 pounds and I purchased size extra small.

I’ve had this dress saved in my cart for months. Finally decided to order it and I’m so glad I did!! It fits perfect. I’m 5’3, 170lbs. And I carry my weight in my middle. Ordered the large. This is comfy and hides my problem area. And it’s a great length. I’m 46 y.o. And it’s not too long or too short. My teenage daughter said I look “super cute!” That’s all the approval anyone needs lol

I've had this jacket now since March and I've lived in it. Wore it daily and it's been washed several times. I am 5'6 and 230lbs and I ordered a 3X. As you can see from the picture of me wearing it, it fits great with some room to breathe. As for warmth, it's not a winter jacket but if you're wearing a long sleeve underneath and it's about 45 degrees, you're going to be fine. Plus size approved! Order it, ladies! You won't be disappointed.

I’ve never found overalls that fit as well as these. I’m 63 and am all about comfort. At 5’3” and 135 lb. I got a size medium and they’re a perfect fit. A little stretchy so definitely comfortable and let you move. I already ordered more in different colors.

I've ordered another wax denim in a size 11 that fit well but these were a little too tight, might be because the other pair had more stretch and these weren't very stretchy (for reference, I'm 5'8", 160 lbs, 36D, 32" waist, 40" hips). The waist cut into me and gave me a muffin top and the legs gave me serious "stuffed sausage" look below my butt. The fabric is thick enough so my underwear didn't show through and seemed good quality. I worry about jeans being too short but these were actually a bit long on me causing the fabric to gather around my ankles which I didn't really mind. I love the 3 button detail and white jeans are a staple item so if you can get the sizing right, I recommend buying them.

I've ordered several loungewear sets from amazon and this is my absolute fave. I typically order a size small but I decided to get this in a size medium. So glad that I did. It is so comfortable and roomy, but not too big at all. I'm 5'7" and 125 lbs for reference. It's incredibly soft, the color doesn't bleed when I wash it, and it's become a total go-to for when I'm working from home. AND the top is a super cute sweater that I've definitely gone out in. So I get to wear this at home when I'm feeling cozy and dress the top up for a super cute fall look.

I’ve ordered the dark blue ones before. They are a constant fit. I’ll order of 14 petite and the length is just right I am 5 foot two and they were very comfortable on me. Plus I do not like real tight fitting and they’re just right. They last for years.

I've owned two different lengths of these pants, and have learned some things you'll want to know before you buy. First of all, the material on these is great. They feel like professional grade pants, unlike most pull on pants of this style that are made with a cottony material, that while comfy, is also a lot like sweatpants in a different cut, and just feel very casual to me for going to the office. This material still has a lot of stretch to it, and the strength of the material plus the high waist keeps me "tucked in" around the middle, and not in an uncomfortable way. These pants do not have real pockets, so if you want those, move along. These are slim fit, so pockets would create lumps and bumps, so I'm okay letting that go for these. The biggest unknown when it comes to these pants is the length. The Regular size is listed as 32" and the Tall size is listed as 35". I am 5' 8 1/2" tall, and have issues with pants being too short, but long ones being too long. The Regular size look fine if I'm in flats and standing, but the stretchy nature of these pants means that when you sit, they move quite a long ways up your leg. That's why I included a picture of me with my leg up in the air like a stork. For the Tall length pants, 35" in any other pant would be WAY too long for me, and they are definitely long when standing, I have to have some heels on (though you can see in the picture, the heels don't have to be crazy tall). But when I sit in the Tall pants, they are the right length. For me, sitting at my desk at work in the Regular size pants, the short length drove me batty. My socks poke out, and they are super high water, so I'd rather have the long size and wear some heels with them. This is my personal preference. Also, they are quite slim fit, I am wearing a size 8 and normally I wear a size 6 in pants. I am 135 lbs. I hope this review helps you with these pants. I have several pairs now that I've dialed down the sizing I like.

I’ve read a lot of the reviews. Yes, the pants are thin however like linen they’re gonna be wrinkly and thinner. The fit is amazing. I’m 5 foot nine and 125lbs. The vest is so cute and the pants are tailored well. Not too short. I could wear a wedge but flats best.

I’ve read some bad reviews for these jeans but I have no complaints. They fit me perfectly, I’m 5’7 and 162 lbs. I generally wore medium before I was pregnant so that’s the size I ordered. They are very comfortable and attractive. Makes my butt look good too! My husband approves :) I’ve only just received them so I don’t know how well they hold up after washes, but for $30 they are everything I could ask for.

I’ve seen the trend of barrel jeans/pants and thought to my old millennial self that that trend would never be something I’d put in my wardrobe. But I saw these animal print barrel pants on someone at a theme park and thought they looked really cute on the older woman wearing them and so I figured I’d find me a pair and give it a shot. I’m 5’7” and weigh about 172lbs. I ordered a large in “brown” as I wasn’t sure what the other colors would look like. These pants fit great and honestly are a subtle barrel cut so they aren’t so in your face like some styles I’ve seen. I highly recommend these if you’re looking to try out the barrel jeans and want something subtle but still trendy!

I’ve tried a few barrel type pants and these are the best fitting so far! I’m 5’9”, 145lbs and these are so cozy. Fit snug but not tight in the waist, and best of all don’t highlight my pooch. Definitely buying other colors. Fabric quality is good, not thick. Color is cute

I’ve tried a few barrel type pants and these are the best fitting so far! I’m 5’9”, 145lbs and these are so cozy. Fit snug but not tight in the waist, and best of all don’t highlight my pooch. Definitely buying other colors. Fabric quality is good, not thick. Color is cute

I’ve tried about 10 jeans from Amazon and these are my favorite. They’re stretchy but keep their shape and are not too long or high waisted. I’m 5’1” and weigh 120 lbs and size 4 fit great (Amazon recommended size 6 but I had to return). They’re flattering and worth the price. I was trying to avoid paying so much for jeans but these are my favorite from Amazon.

I’ve tried on so many jeans. I’m 5’4 205 lbs, 61 years old. These look and feel really great. They have a lot of stretch which I love. I spent 1/2 day trying on jeans at AE and Old Navy and love these so much more. I may order more in other colors.

I’ve tried SO MANY overalls and these are pretty great! I’m tall, (5’9” and 160lbs) so I wish they were a tad longer but overall they’re perfect. Soft, a little stretchy, didn’t shrink in the wash, true to size. I’ll be ordering more in different colors

I’ve tried some other brands on Amazon, and the pants flared out at the bottom and just didn’t hang nicely. These fit perfectly, and I am in love! The perfect way to sneak comfort into my outfits for the office. I bought basically every color. I’m 5’4” and 132lbs. X-small fit like a glove even though Amazon recommended a small.

I’ve tried the designer barrel jeans and wasn’t ready to shell out that much yet for a trendier style. I saw these reviewed by some influencers and decided to give them a try. They’re honestly so lightweight and a great dupe of the FP ones. I’m 5’2” and they’re full length on me. I sized up to a 6 after reading the reviews and they fit me the way I wanted them to. They are def a high rise though, not a mid. My only complaint is the first pair I received has clearly been worn/washed and smelled of perfume/had pilling inside. Ordered a replacement and they are in new condition and have zero issues! Thanks Amazon. :) I would buy again in another wash too! Highly recommend if you’re wanting to try the barrel trend!

I’ve wanted these pants for a year, and now I’m pretty disappointed. I’m honestly considering returning them. They are way too short. I don’t even mind the sheerness that much, but the fact that they are too short for my height is kind of a dealbreaker. I’m not even that tall either, I’m only 5’6”. I’m 200 lbs and ordered a XL, which is their largest size for some reason.

I've worn NYDJ jeans for several years now and always worn a size 10. I ordered the NYDJ's Ponte Trouser in a size 10 and found the fit to be as expected for a knit trouser. Snug in the seat and upper thigh, with a very slight flare at the cuff. The length of these trousers on my 5'6" frame was slightly longer than expected but perfect with a 2-3" heel. These trousers are super comfortable. They are a mid-weight knit and look great paired with a sweater or blouse. They provide a snug, classy look that is perfect for work or play. Good looking, well made and easy to fall in love with because they feel so good on, the Ponte Trouser by NYDJ's is the wardrobe basic you've been looking for. Definitely recommend them! NOTE: I ordered both black and charcoal colors. One of the included photos are the two colors side by side.

I've worn Q Babies for years, they fit OK, better than anything else I'd come across, maybe a 7 out of 10. They definitely needed hiking every time I stood up. I decided to try Willows in the same size ( I'm 5'9" 140 pounds and wear 7/8 34) to see if there was a difference. Oh yah! I carry my weight in my thighs versus tush. This picture is day 2 after sitting at a desk half the day. Never hitched once. Amazing. Fabric is soft but heavy enough to wear to the barn; they seem to have a little more room in the crotch, not visible really but you can feel the difference. Now if only they had a straight leg version in white...

I’ve worn these jeans 3 times so far and I get so many compliments and inquires as to where they are from. I’m now coming back to write my review and order them in black. Love the fit and happy to find they were long enough. I’m 5’9” 155lb and the length is perfect for flats with a fun raw hem. I wash but let them air dry.

iChosey Women's Straight Leg Dress/Work Pant I chose was Navy Blue in size 6. The material content is 53% Cotton, 39% Nylon and 8% Spandex. It is supposed to be an ankle pant but being I am 5' 4" and 122 lbs the pants come longer on me. I would say that this is a pair of pants just right for work and for women that need a pair of pants that "hold" back some extra weight and/or bulges. Functional pockets are on each side of the hip. I personally like a pant whether for work or dress to have a natural flow to it and there is no flow that graces ones shape with this pair of slacks. Thank you.
Id rather not give 1 star. Pockets on legs crooked. Length? These are so not a 29 inch in length. I am 5'7 and these are way to short. I will not buy from this company again. Waste of money.

I'm a shortstack at 5'2" so my only concern is that the hem rides up a little too high but I can work with it even if that means the pockets sit too high as well. Just a warning for my fellow shorties out there.

If I had the right body type for these pants, I wouldn’t hesitate to give them 5 stars because they are really well made, the fabric is heavy and looks expressive. Unfortunately they don’t work for me. First of all, the length - I’m 5’7 and these pants are just way too short. They don’t work as ankle pants because of the wide leg, and I would need at least 3 extra inches of length in order to be able to wear them. Sizing- I got a Large for my 33” waist, 43” hip. You can tell the size is not right for me by the way they balloon on the front in the stomach area. I should have gotten a Medium. Keep in mind that the waist has an elastic so the waist stretches a good bit and the fabric itself is stretchy so my hips definitely would have been ok in a Medium. My suggestion is that if you’re 5’5 or under, give these pants a chance and if you’re in between two sizes, go with the smaller size.

If I pull on the back there is a gap but when not pulling it sits fairly flush to my back (big issue with clothes gapping here if you're hourglass/pear shaped you know what mean). They are sturdy and comfortable and I like that the inseam isn't 2 inches but doesn't come to my knees either. The "mid rise" on a lot of jeans comes to my belly button or just under and that's still too high for me to be comfortable. If they made this in "Low rise" I would definitely keep them but I'll be returning mine. I got a size 6 and they fit with just a little extra room. If you are 5'3" ish and 130lbs or under I'd go with the next size down.

If it wasn’t for the length, these jeans would get 5 stars. I’m 5’10 and I prefer the hem to hit the floor so I can wear different kinds of shoes - not just sandals. These hit the tops of my feet. Hips: 40”, Waist: 30.5” - Amazon recommended size 8, which fit perfectly. These have great stretch and still look/feel like denim. Front & back pockets are real. Color matches the pic. Excellent value - too bad they’re not longer.

If it were half the price of the $42.99 i paid, I might keep them. The right pant leg is almost one inch shorter than the left which is a deal breaker for $43. I did not wash pants, instructions say to drip dry. Returning pants but reordering to see if next pair is same length or otherwise okay. Fabric is very comfortable, doesn't wrinkle. Good for office wear but would not call them dress slacks. I would say the fabric is good for spring or fall, but too hot for summer. Good for a cool/cold office. I bought extra large which is the 30 inch inseam. A curvy 5'7" 180lbs. 30inch inseam is the half-break to full-break pant leg that I prefer. 2 front pockets are deep. No back pockets, pull on pants. Comfortable waistband, did not make muffin tops.

If you are curvy then you should probably size up. The pants were a little too tight on me. I’m 5’2 and the pants were the perfect length. They do look more flared than straight though. Also, the zipper was poorly made on the pair I received and feels like it could break easily. Overall cute pants but could probably find the same pair for less on SHEIN. I would have kept the pants if they actually fit me.
If you are even thinking about these BUY THEM!!!! I’m 5’6” and have an hour glass figure I got the large but definitely could have done the medium #earnscommission

If you are interested in buying these pants, you should go ahead and do it. I'm 5'6, 130lbs, ordered a size small. These are great. Very comfortable and fit perfectly. I want a pair in every color now. The only thing I wasn't fond of at first, was the elastic band at the waist. It was very tight and made my hips look square when it squeezed me. I pulled them down a little more and it didn't make my shape look as weird. I am always anxious about ordering clothes on line because I prefer to try on, and not go through the hassle of shipping back. These were my first clothes item that I have ordered from Amazon, and I am not disappointed.

If you have hips, these pants are not for you. The pleats on the front are absolutely horrible. You can't tell from looking at them, but they do actually fit me ok - they just bunch horribly from the pleats. Also, the zipper on the side is terrible and catches on the fabric, making them very difficult to zip or unzip. FYI, I'm only 5'2 and these didn't feel ridiculously long on me (which most pants that aren't petite/short do), so I'm guessing they'd be pretty short on someone of average height.

If you ladies know how hard is to find the most comfortable pants being 7 weeks postpartum, you might know how happy I am to find the most comfortable one ever! Super soft material, pockets are indeed a nice plus… nice shape and very well made. I’m in love and definetely ordering more!!! I’m a 5’-3” and the 28 fits amazing.

If you like your jeans with a giant gap at the waist, then these are for you. Personally I hate that. also, I was hoping for a higher rise. If it’s any indication the zipper length was 3 inches. For reference, I am 5‘2“ and about 125 pounds. Athletic build. Size 4 fit snug through the thighs and bug gap at the waist.

If you want reallllly soft comfy jogger pants then buy these. I have wide hips and I just pull them right on top and it fits so nicely. Even my boyfriend complimented them and touched them and was like woah I’m gonna wear those haha. Really great for the price and I never wanna take them off. I’m a size 8 in jeans, 5’3” and 153lbs. I purchased a Medium.

If you’re a short girl like me and always looking at cropped pants wondering if they’ll be good “regular pants” on you, here you go: I’m 5’ tall and these are full length on me, just right for flat sandals in the summer. They’re a Medium and I normally wear a 6-8 pant, so I’d say true to size and the perfect throw-em-on thing for basically any occasion in the summer. I could get away with these at my office or going to dinner, running errands, whatever. They’re light and airy and the green is a great neutral. Also, yay for pockets!

If you’re a skinny gal these will fit just perfect after a few washes in warm or hot water. They were a little big on me at first but after washing and drying on hot they fit amazing, still have the loose fit look but fitted at the waist and butt. I purchased a size 2 and I’m between 120-125lbs.

If you're a toddler parent at this time then you know Halloween is all about dressing as Ms. Rachel. Since I could no longer fit into my pre-pregnancy overalls I love so much and got in Europe so can never replace, I went to Amazon for some "cheap" overalls to throw on for Halloween. I'm 5' 6" and 150lbs give or take a few lbs and I ordered the Medium and these fit perfectly! Not only do they fit perfectly but they're pretty darn flattering, for overalls, lol. The material is not thin, it looks and feels like I spent a good amount of money on them so I was pleasantly surprised. I had a few moms asking where I got them so from other perspectives they must be nice. I definitely feel like these are more durable than my favorite pair. They're not thick in the way that woukd limit motion, they're thick as in they don't feel like they'll tear when bending and moving around but they're not hot or stiff. I would definitely and have already recommended these to others.

If you’re anything like me I am always hesitant to buy off brand due to previous quality issues. These pants have a lot of stretch and are quality material (not thin or see through). Super comfy, love the pocket, and I will likely buy more in different colors. For reference I am 5’3” and got the shortest length which I think is perfect for me, I’m 6 months and it fit my belly perfectly but if you want them looser I’d maybe size up one.

If you're considering, buy them! I was hesitant because of the price but was too intrigued and they're definitely made very well. The fabric is a canvas/linen type material but not cheap or flimsy. The rose design is done really nicely and overall, I would like to wear these every single day.... I wish there were more options because I would absolutely buy them all. As far as the fit, I'm 5'1 128lbs and got the small which fit perfectly. There is zero stretch heads up.

If you're hesitant, just order em' already!! 😂 They are super soft, comfy, and fit perfectly. I am about 5'3 and approx. 135lbs ordered a medium and they are just perfect, ordered 2 different designs and they both do not disappoint!! Going to be ordering more! I had bought basic leggings without pockets at the Leggings Depot store at the mall before they closed and figured if they were just as soft as those I would love these!! I was right!! 10/10! 👍💯🥰

If you're like me you wanted to buy the free people version of these, but didn't want to spend the money. THESE are the best dupes! If you’re looking for a great pair of jeans, I think these are if. Perfect fit and so soft, comfortable and stretchy. Fit is true to size. For reference I’m 5’ 7” 145 lbs wearing a medium 28/tall.

If you’re on the fence, i’m here to tell you to add to cart immediately. Im 5 ft 135 lbs and normally between 3-5 pants but i got a 6 because of reviews.. hopefully they shrink in the crotch is too lose and they ride really high up my stomach. I ordered the light blue as well.

Im 5'5" a little over 35.5" in waist, 45" hips, 24" diameter thighs and 16" diameter legs/calves, no thigh gap. What drew me to these pants were that they were high waisted and on a lightning deal for like 20 bucks. I didnt think much about the cut beyond the waist but figured tapered legs would be attractive. I think the deal breaker for me is the style. They look kind of like straight legs on me, and since I have large thighs it's just more fabric than I want in the leg area. I dont like how the fabric bunches behind my knee and though this isn't entirely on the jeans I don't think they're doing my bum any favors. I did go a size larger than i probably should have with the 16, I just hated the idea of the top of the jeans pinching my stomach. I also got the regular instead of the short but I dont think the tapers sharp enough for the extra length to make a difference here, meaning to give shape to my legs. I think the taper's only really attractive if your thighs arent super disproportionate to your legs. Maybe like 2 or 3 inches max wider diameter in the thighs in relation to your calves. At least for the fit I want. I was gonna send these back in exchange for some 14's but I think I'll have to go with one of the other cuts to get the fit I'm looking for. Besides the fit I think these were a good buy for the price. Nice stretch and color. not as thick as some denim blends, a bit thicker than like denim leggings. Comfortable to move around in, liked fit at the waist. Better than most things you'd find between 20 - 30. If I had to recommend based on fabric and comfort vs leg fit I'd recommend.

It's true about fashion. Wait long enough and it comes back in style. Flared bottoms and corduroy fabric, I'm back in Jr. high. But these pants, omg, they are NOT the pants from back then. The fabric is solid corduroy, not striped, super soft, and made from cotton and spandex like jeans these days so they have give and stretch. As soon as I opened the package I could tell I was in love. They hug your hips, high waisted, cute jean type buttons for your fly (yes no zipper but adds to the charm) 4 pockets, slimmer thigh to the knee, then flare out to the bottom. I walked into the living room for my husband to see and his eyes got big and said NICE! and now you're in style! (head slap for that).A couple of things. Normally I wear 8, sometimes 10 if no stretch or give. According to their size chart, because I have a 40" hip I went with their size 12. I should have paid more attention to the fabric because upon first try, no problem but slightly bigger than my body, a little sag in the crotch and from the pic you can see touches the ground. If you're wearing high heels or wedge, that's perfect. I could have easily gone to a 10. I decided to wash them last night, alone because they were new and black, on cool water wash, then dried on high. They came out slightly shrunk, which I hoped they would, and then tried them on again. I didn't take pics, but they still were easy to put on, hugged my hips, no crotch sag, waist a little more fitting and the length does not touch the floor anymore, just about less than 1/2" off the floor. I feel like I made them more like a size 10. So give some consideration that these are stretchy and either order your normal size and watch how you wash, or maybe try a size up and see how they fit, possibly knowing you can make them a little smaller in the wash. I did not notice any lint or fading after I washed them.I would say these pants are quality made, cute, cute, cute, very soft and comfortable to wear and will flatter just about any body type with it's cut. You do have to button them up. I'm glad the style is coming back so all shapes can look good, which to me means flattering to your body type. I would say the price to me is on the high side. I've always been thrifty and look for bargains or discounts, especially on a Sr fixed income. If you're willing to pay this for 1 pair of pants, go for it! They will not disappoint.

It’s usually hard for me to find bottoms that are long enough. (I’m 5’8 and almost 200 pounds) These fit JUST right, though! I got the 10 Long size, and I was really shocked when it was actually the right length! Plus, they stretch some, and they are comfortable!

Since they are stretchy I can still wear them, but they were more comfortable before washing. It is hard to say if the next size up would fit better or be a bit too big. I am including (terrible) pics of before and after washing and drying. They are not as dark in color as the pics look. Excuse my poor editing program and skills. Just trying to show the difference in length. The price at under $40 is amazing. I paid more than that for flannel lined jeans over 10 years ago. Highly recommended, but you may want to size up.

They are a boot-cut and fit as expected, slimmer above the knee and flared below the knee. The material is very soft and a bit stretchy. I feel like the next size up would be very comfortable. Appears to be made of great quality, actually much better than I even expected. Has a little bit of stretch but not overly. I can only highly recommend this it looks awesome on.
Just as advertised. I’m 5’ 6” 170 and ordered a size 8, my usual size. The pants have some stretch, and no smell out of package. Buttons seem secure.

Just as described! It is oversized but I love that!! I am 5'5 120 pounds and got the small. It has pockets. I have had no problems with washing and drying. You can style it to your liking as it pretty much goes with anything. It is kind of stiff at first but after washing it will relax. You can roll the sleeves up or leave them down. I am happy with it! :)
Just bought my 4th pair. These are comfortable, attractive, and reasonably priced. I am 5’ 3”, about 140 and chose petite medium. Great fit and good length. These can go anywhere, dressed up or down.
Just bought my 4th pair. These are comfortable, attractive, and reasonably priced. I am 5’ 3”, about 140 and chose petite medium. Great fit and good length. These can go anywhere, dressed up or down.

Just buy it! So comfortable and worth it! I’m 5’1 approx 230lbs and the XL fit perfect. Usually use size 14-16 jeans depending how good of a stretch. These have great stretch possibly could fit size 18 even. So damn cute!

Just buy it!!!! So flattering, I thought it was big once I opened but once I put it in my body the small size was just right. I am 5.5 130 pounds, have big boobs and I still needed a bra to fit in the boob area so if you have my measurements but small boobs I recommend maybe a extra small

Just call me Kim Possible, at this point! These pants are SO cute!! They are really good quality and super soft as well. I am 5’6”, 206 lbs and the XL was a bit loose on me, so I had to wear a belt, but I don’t mind the looseness, because they’re SO comfy! If you’re looking for a more snug fit, size them down. :)

Just for reference, I am 5'5", 150 lbs. I saw a shirt on the tv show Shrinking and loved it, came to Amazon and searched for "denim pullover" and in about two minutes found it - it's this shirt. I was so happy to see it came in several colors of denim. I bought the grey and I love it. It's very wide but cropped, it's actually a lot less heavy weight of fabric than it looks, which is good for me. Some pockets would be nice of course, but not a deal breaker! I might get this in another color and do some embroidery on it.

Just got a compliment on these from someone in my house & she is glad to take these off my hands as they're pretty massive on me ~ Hip = 45" / Front rise 13.5" / Back Rise 18.5" I am 5'4 and pulling up to my belly button I still have 2' left hanging down in the crotch (and also must use the drawstring even to get them to stay as the elastic is also made for someone a bit larger). So it's not a fix to make them look tighter/more fitted just pulling them up unless you are taller with a wider hip. I range from xs to 4/6 all depends but these to me feel like a medium or like one of those very generous vanity sizing smalls. Also just realized the knees stretch so I get the kangaroo look like I'm bending my knees when I'm standing straight. The front panels of fabric down the legs are for some reason narrower than the back, so essentially you can see both inseams & outward seams from the front & not sure what that's doing to the silouette & wear. The wide hip takes away from the wide leg because it's almost seems like it's tapering, even if it's the same width.. there's just a whole lot more upper fabric + rise for me than I'm personally used to in a pant. Nice saturated teal just as in the ad, very drapey and linen-y to some extent (like linen washed a lot and blended with something else) There is no materials tag in the pant, only a brand tag with size & care tag on reverse of it. If you got a lot of hip, are preg or looking for a not-too restrictive short pant with a wide leg & high rise, give em a try! :)

Just got these and love them! Fabric is not too thin so gives some support. Back pockets are faux but has deep front pockets. A little long on me but not awful. I am 5’6” and usually wear a size 10 pant. Ps just realized I need to clean my mirror!

Just got these and tried them on and immediately love! I am 5’2” and around 160lbs and ordered the large. The waist fits so comfortably without being too loose or too snug. The length is exactly right for me, but I have not washed them so I don’t know if they will shrink. I will likely hang them to dry just in case. I know my photo isn’t super clear but hopefully it’s !

Just got these sweatpants in and I’m obsessed! I’m 5’3” and the length in XL is perfect for me but they may run short if you’re taller than 5’6” or so. They’re so soft and the material is great for loungewear or wearing out of the house. They are not fleece, they have a slick almost satin-like finish inside and are more like the “scuba” fabric. They’re heavy and thick enough to wear out of the house comfortably without being too hot. Will be ordering more colors!

Just got these, so this is based off of unwashed straight outta the bag. I bought an XL. I am 5'9" and between 170-180 lbs bottom heavy. Big tummy, hips, thighs and butt. They are comfy and stretchy. They fit just above my ankles. I would definitely order more if they had Jean or black color in my size. The material is fairly thin. I have a lot of cellulite dimples and they definitely show more in these white pair than a denim colored pair I received through a different retailer. The denim pair are also heavier material than these white ones I purchased on Amazon. I go commando and I feel like you can almost see through them. I certainly wouldn't wear any type of colored panties underneath. I think some nylons underneath would help smooth some of the dimples. Over all I think these are pretty awesome for a $13 pair of pants. I've played more for pants I've liked less. I think the top you pair them with is gonna be what makes the difference if you are curvy.

Just had a baby and was looking for pants that would cover my baby pouch. I'm wearing a medium and I'm 5'8 and it fit me well. And I feel like once I lose a little more weight it will fit better. I'll still be able to wear it at a smaller size because of the stretch material.
Just nope. Not even close to what I expected. I think I'm done buying pants on Amazon. I'm 5'4" 125lbs. These pants give me a fupa....I don't even have a fupa😑

Just not for me. These are cute. But I am 5ft tall and 128 pounds. First, they are puffy just below your waist. And even though my waist is 25", I have subconscious issues about that area. But.. hello.. they do make me look like I lost my butt.. so they are going back. If you have a JLo butt you will be fine..lol

Just received it today! Tried it on... I guess they do run big! I am normally a size 7-9 in womens pants! I read the reviews and decided to go a few sizes down.. I am 5'4.5" tall and weigh 140pounds... I read from someone's review which is shorter by and inc and weighed 10 pounds less had gotten a 6 and was till kind of loose...and maybe someone else shrunk a bit... So I went with a size 4!!! Any way it fits perfect! I did a squat down and it did not pull down! Fabric is thick not cheap like. I will post and update when I finally wear it for a day or two! Hopefully it doesn't shrink nor stretches out! 🤞🤞😊 I hope this review helps!

Just received my jeans today. I'm very happy with them and will probably order more colors! Denim is soft and has some stretch to it so you don't have to be super precise with sizing. They're very comfortable and length is good. I think they have a slimming affect from the side profile due to the cut of them, which I will NOT complain about! Lol. I ordered size 12 (normally a 10-12 depending on the brand). For reference, I'm 5'6" and 170 lbs. and ordered them in the color "blue."

Just received the pants today, and I'm OBSESSED. These jeans are very close perfection in my opinion! For starters, I love the distressed look without having holes or looking TOO distressed. They look like something that would say Ralph Lauren on the label tbh. The fabric is soft (not a stiff denim), not too thin or too thick, and they have a nice amount of stretch to them. I'm usually a size 10 (but sometimes 12 depending on the brand). For reference, I'm 5'6" and 170 lbs. I ordered a size 12 because I don't like anything tight around my waist and I was concerned with them running small. The 12 fits nicely and I DO think that if I ordered a size 10, they may have pulled around the waist just enough to be able to see some of the white interior of the pockets (I hope that makes sense). The legs have just the right amount of "barrel" to them (not as barrel shaped as FP jeans). These jeans are PERFECT for anyone looking to try out the barrel jeans trend without committing to a full on barrel jean. I'd rate these jeans 10/10 and ended up ordering 2 more in other colors!!! WORTH EVERY PENNY!!!

Large/Navy-67. I am 4"8/140 pounds, thick booty and thighs. Fits perfect waist through leg. I am short so I had to cut about a foot off from length to make above ankle length. Comfortable, stretchy, thinish material. Great for summer.

Lee shirts, 6” seam. They do not show cheeks, they do not ride up the front or back. Relaxed fit, washed and dried in dryer beautifully. Length is perfect for flats and sneakers. 5'4" 125lbs. Medium fits nicely. Nice they aren't too long. I can pull them on without using the buttons. Nice fit in the tushy. Comfortable and stretchy. I will probably sew down the front pockets.

Length was great. It's hard to find dress pants long enough when you're 6' tall. I'm glad I read previous reviews and sized up. I normally wear 2x. I ordered 5x and they fit nicely. Do need to verify where made though. USA or Mexico?

Length was perfect, which is a hard to find when you're short. Only issue is that the waist size is larger than indicated in sizing. I ordered a 2S -26" waist - actual waist size had additional room/running larger than indicated ( is 28" inch waist). Will need to take them in to wear w/o belt. Definitely size down if you can.

Let me first off say I wanted to love this product, but I simply can't. Not made for big rear, small waist ladies. My hips around widest part are 39-40. My waist is 25 at smallest portion. The pockets pucker in back creating an obvious dent in my hiney. Even my husband who could care less said how obvious it was. Oh that is unless you're a fan of camel toe and butt wedgie and pull them way up then the line isn't obvious as in my second picture (everyone loves camel toe and butt wedgie right) Very comfortable super good fabric with good stretch but the darn FAKE POCKETS pull!!! :( length was perfect too and I am 5'4"

Let me first point out that these are good quality jeans. A little big around the waist, but I sized up because I’m in between sizes. I ordered the bigger size 8, but now I’m thinking 6 would have fit better, because of the stretch. I like that these are good sturdy jeans, very soft, comfortable and they stretch. I also like the olive color of these jeans. Lastly, they are long enough for me and reach my ankles I’m 5ft7. The only con is that the waist is loose, but since I go up and down in weight that isn’t a big deal. 👍🆙 recommend.

Let me lay out my body negatives so you know just how awesome these wide leg jeans are. I'm 5'3 weighing 180lbs, short legs & short torso. I have a solid athletic build with a firm muffin-top and small fupa. I carry most of my weight in my stomach and breast. I'm never without shapewear on; most times two of them on. I purchased a medium in these, the perfect boot cut denim stretch jeans, and boy are they comfortable. At first when I pulled them from the package, the waist looked really small, esp in comparison to the nice wide leg and ample bottom part. So of course I tried them on without shapewear just to see how much stretch was in the waist part. I was able to button them, but not comfortably. I knew they would be perfect with my shapewear on. I ordered the 'wased black'; I love the faded look. The stretch on these jeans is a nice firm stretch. And because they are not really denim material, they are not heavy like denim, but sturdy enough to still feel like a great pair of jeans. The best part, they got my booty looking great. I absolutely love these jeans, so I plan on getting two more pairs.

Let me share my experience with this white 2-piece denim set—it's a tale of size surprises and durable style. As someone who stands at 5'9" but typically orders larger sizes to avoid pants being too short, I opted for a size large. The result? Well, the pant length is spot-on, but the fit elsewhere is, shall we say, roomy. The waist and top are especially loose, thanks to my underestimation of the size and fit. But let's talk about the positives. The material is top-notch—durable, non-transparent, and heavyweight. Plus, the solid stitching and hems throughout ensure longevity and quality. Despite the sizing hiccup, I can't deny the potential of this outfit. It's stylish, well-made, and versatile. My advice? Order the right size for a flawless fit. With the correct sizing, I have no doubt this set would be a wardrobe staple. So, if you're in the market for a chic denim ensemble, don't hesitate to give this one a try—just be sure to get your sizing spot-on for the perfect look and feel.

Let me start by saying I’m a plus sized girl with all my weight on the bottom half, so shorts are difficult. I am 230 lbs, size 16 jeans and ordered a 2x. I gave this 3stars because they are wayyy short, like indecently short. That being said, they are super stretchy and fit my thunder thighs just fine. They are not too tight anywhere on me, I think due to the stretchiness. The back of them are so short I feel like my buttcheeks are popping out, if that were the look I was going for I would have made them 5 stars. I’m going to keep them, more as a swimsuit coverup than an everyday wear pair of shorts. They are fine for being out on the boat but there’s no way I would wear them anywhere else.

Let me start first by giving measurements for reference: 5'3" , 137lbs , 29" waist, 38" hips, 27" inseam That being said, I have tried all 6/8 short/petite relaxed/curvy pants on Amazon (Rafaella Gaberdine curvy, Signature Levi curvy Straight, Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda & GV Curvy Skinny, Dickies Curvy Straight, Lee Relaxed Side Elastic Straight, etc) and ALL of them either had a gap in the waist, were too tight across the rear, or were simply just too long.. however THESE pants fit great and had neither issue! I finally found a pants that not only fit, but are modest for work-- flattering but not figure hugging. I bought these in size 6 petite in colors (pictured from left to right) Indigo Wash (like a blue jean color/type fabric) Black Imperial Blue Mini Glenplaid (Black on light grey- NO BLUE in this, which i actually prefer) Cypress (Dark Olive Green) Jet Black/White Rockhill Plaid Falcon (pinkish brown) Upon close inspection, I noticed 1 of the 6 pairs I had purchased (Falcon) were defective. On the back leg, it appears as if holes were accidentally cut in the pants and then attempted to be "patched" by resewing it back together. (see pictures) Very disappointed that Amazon would allow this and have reordered/requested a return on this one pair. Because of the issue and trouble, I am deducting 1 star.

Let me start off by saying I hate buying pants. Doesn't matter if it is online or in the store. I can try on 20 pairs of pants in a store and maybe, MAYBE walk out of the store with 1 pair of pants. I have a high waist, little to no eh-hem rear, and thicker thighs. This can make it very difficult to find pants that I love and that fit well. To make matters worse I am a size 7 or 27 which I have found is an increasingly hard pants size to find when you are a frugal shopper. For reference I am 5' 4" and 133 lbs. I came across these and decided to give the size Medium a shot. These pants are all I've ever wanted in pants. So far I have bought two colors: Dusty Rose and Burgundy. The colors are gorgeous. The material feels similar to jeans, but it is much softer. They are super stretchy and they fit like a glove. I really feel beautiful in them. They can be dressed up with a nice work blouse for the office or dressed down with a band t-shirt for a concert. I want to own a pair in every color and toss all my poor fitting pants. I just hope these stay available for long enough for me to buy them when I have funds. Edit: By the way... They have pockets. Real pockets. Deep pockets! Women's pants never have pockets. These are the perfect pants.

Let me start out by saying I have NEVER found anything high waisted that I looked good in, until now. These are so cute, they’re soft and not constricting so you feel very comfy in them. I’m 5’2” and weigh 128 pounds. The sizing guide suggested I get the medium and they fit very comfortably. I’m really excited to be able to mix up my summer shorts with my tops now!

Let me start this by saying I wasn't expecting to fit in these as well as I do. I bought these knowing I could pass them down to my sister if I didn't fit in them as I wanted to. Jeans are hard for me to find that fit well, and I usually opt for trousers or leggings instead. Day 1 of their arrival I wore them to work (see picture) and I had no issues with them. They proved to withstand all the bending over, on my knees and fast movement through an 8 hour shift. They didn't make me uncomfortable, they didn't cause any chafing, or didn't unzip and expose my underwear like I've also experienced with jeans. I got a few compliments from coworkers and customers on them, as they usually dont see me in jeans. The flare on them is adorable, however I will say as im 5'3, I did have to roll them so they didn't drag while at work. So shorties, be aware of the flare drag! Color is beautiful I love the wash. I didn't notice any weird smell from them. And they're very thick, as I stated before I'm constantly on my knees at work and don't feel much stretch from them. I attribute that to their mostly cotton makeup, it's not cheap polyester that easily tears under pressure.

Let me start with stats: I'm 5'6" 150lbs (athletic build, or pretty straight up n down build but spreading with age 😭so maybe I'm an apple now, lol. Anyway...), a 36DD Bust (usually M top,large in button ups typically) , a 29"waist (usually size 6 in jeans/pants or 4, brand makes a big difference there), and a 31" inseam. I got these in a 6 and if say they for well. There's plenty of stretch to the very soft fabric making these comfortable and flexible. They definitely just fit me comfortably in the waist line (no belt needed) any tighter they'd be uncomfortable. these also are definitely midrise pants which at my age I prefer. The length is perfect for flat or low heel shoe. I washed and dried these and did not notice any shrinkage. They came out softer and ready to wear. My use for these is for camping. Great value. These seem like great everyday pants as well. Breen color is pretty. Brownish green. True to product picture. I would also say these are a they're straight leg pant. These for over my ugh boots snugly, over a hiking boot nicely and with athletic shoes without dragging. I highly recommend these at this price.

Let me start with… I think these are great pants, I’m 5’2 and the small short is a great fit HOWEVER they are not chocolate brown as they appear to be in every photo advertised. (see photos, esp one with the pants beside what I consider a true brown blazer) My intent was to SPECIFICALLY order chocolate brown pants not just any cute pants that fall somewhere in the brown/khaki category. So I’ll be returning them, BUT, if you are looking for comfy pants and aren’t needing them to match a certain color, go for it as the fit as well as the material are both nice quality. (3 stars solely because the advertised photos should better represent the true color of the product.)

Let me tell you, These pants are perfect! Runs a little smaller. Mediums would usually fit flowy on me so if that's what you want, size up! If u want them to fit similar to my photo, get ur usual size! I'm 5'0" 140lbs. Also perfect for those bloated days, the paperbag waist + the tie really hides the tummy!

Lets dive in, The dress itself has a very loose fitting. So I added a belt to give it some shape. The buttons work perfectly, and they align evenly. Beautiful denim color. Lets talk denim. This is my first ever jean purchase from Amazon and I am so impressed. They fit amazing against my curves. Like how it was too long since I’m 4’ll but overall loved how it fit

Like many things on Amazon -- if you're in a pinch, this will do; it'll hold you over until you can find something nicer. The deep green color is gorgeous. Was hoping this would be a winner but had to return.
Like most people said… size down from your usual size. I’m 5’2 120 lbs and got a size 26 that fits perfectly

Listen, I didn't ask them to, I didn't know about it in advance, heck, I didn't even know they were aware of my existence. But obviously, Gap did & does. They went ahead and designed a pair of custom jean leggings made to my exact measurements. Now, even though I plan to buy every single pair of size 6/28, I decided it would only be fair to share my stats with you as well as the stats of MY custom made, comfortable, flattering & 100% perfect fitting jean leggings. Me: 140-145# 5'3" 33.5" waist (measured at the bottom of my bellybutton because that's where MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS sit) 40" hips (measured at the widest point, mid-bum) MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS (Measured flat) 9" rise 14" waist 17" hips They have a REAL zipper fly, not one of those "just for show" kind commonly found on jean leggings. Every pair I've tried in that past with a "fake fly" always had extra fabric that bunched up at the crotch & made me look like I was smuggling oranges whenever I sat down (& they didn't look any better when standing up, either). The traditional 5 pockets are fully functional (real) as well. The stitching/color are absolute perfection & the plain, proportionately sized back pockets are what my denim dreams are made of. If you have time pls check out the pic I attached to see another one of the reasons why these fit so well. I hope my humble opinion helps you in some way. Thank you for reading.

Love these jeans! I've had fleece lined jeans before from LL Bean and I'd say these are definitely a contender for the savings. I like how they look, great color, good rise length, and not thick so they stay kinda flattering. Overall, I definitely recommend them if you're looking for an affordable and comfortable pair of warm jeans for cool weather.

LOVE these pants! Not shiny or super fake looking at all. They are a little on the shorter as some other reviewers noted, but the perfect length with heels even though I’m tall (5’8”). As for the smell - there was a bit of the typical faux leather smell, and based on other reviews that said they smelled horrible by the end of the night, I preemptively flipped them inside out, sprayed with Febreeze and ran them through the dryer with a dryer sheet on low heat for about 30 mins. No more smell, and still smelled good at the end of the night - and I live in Miami where it is HUMID to say the least. If you are on the fence, buy them! You won’t be disappointed, especially for the price!

Don’t wait on this - purchase it already! You will not regret it and it’s Levi’s brand so probably will last a long time.

I have absolutely nothing negative to say about it. I saw these Sweezarmo denim stretch joggers with an elasticized drawstring waistband and ordered them in my usual size small after consulting the company’s provided size chart. The Sky Blue color is represented accurately in the description photos, and the size runs true to the chart measurements. In person, the fabric looks like a true pair of denim jeans and to touch, feels like regular denim….with a little stretch. I was surprised at how much stretch they have in them. The weight of the denim is even close to what I’d expect for a pair of summer jeans. Prior to receiving them, I had some was concern about the waist having enough stretch, since my waist measurement is at least a couple inches larger than the posted measurement on the size chart. Sometimes elasticized waistbands can be constricting without much stretch, but that’s not the case with these. For those with a smaller waist, the drawstring is functional and can be cinched up for the right fit. On me, I feel the pants make my hips look big…but maybe some of that’s due to me being fuller in the waist!?! One feature I absolutely love about the pants is the pockets. Two in the front and two on the back. The front pockets are nice and deep for slipping hands or a cell phone in. I feel the pants are comfortable to wear and on the right figure (i.e. models in the listing photos) they look adorable! The quality of these is good from the materials used to the craftsmanship in the stitching. The pants are actually long enough that I’d be comfortable wearing them with sandals or flats without cuffing them, or rolling them up once or twice for an ankle or cropped look. Due to the thickness of the denim fabric, the elasticized waistband is a little bulky looking and feeling, so I think cropped tops or tops tucked in look the best with these bottoms. Overall I’m pleased with the denim pants (I personally don’t refer to them as joggers). They’re comfortable and wash up well, following care instructions. I will get wear out of them, but they won’t be bottoms I wear out and about often. They are so cute, but just not flattering on my body build. I appreciate the opportunity to try these, but in seeing how they look on me, I probably wouldn’t order them again for the price. But, for others that are considering them, I would recommend them for their quality and suggest staying true to size. For reference, my measurements are: Height - 5’4” Bust - 35” Waist - 30” Arm length - 22.5” Shoulder width - 15” Weight - 120# Inseam - 31”

My stats: 5' tall, 114 pounds, 29 1/2 inch waist, 36 inch hips, 22 inch inseam; 60 years old, carry my weight in my abdomen and thighs. I purchased size small/short. I have to fold up the bottoms a couple of inches, which I usually have to do, so not a problem there. While they are comfortable and I wear them a lot, I am not totally satisfied with them and would try another brand/style rather than purchasing them again. On the plus side, they are very stretchy yet are heavy enough to look like jeans. The back pockets are nice. I washed them in cold water and dried on medium heat and they did not shrink on me. What I don't like: Mainly the front pockets, which are deep and they allow too much fabric in front of them, such that the abdomen area looks poofy and the thighs look wrinkly. I was hoping for something a little more flattering.
